urn:contentItem:6JJ1-J493-RRW2-B314-00000-00CROWLEY MARITIME - UPDATES FROM PEERS ACROSS THE SECTOR2026-05-09T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:36:34ZLexisNexisSTOCKCROWLEY MARITIME - UPDATES FROM PEERS ACROSS THE SECTOR

DASHBOARD: CROWLEY MARITIME

PermID4295904276Websitehttp://www.crowley.com/IndustryMaritime & Shipping Logistics

ACTIVITIES:

Crowley Maritime Corporation, founded in 1892, is a family-owned logistics and maritime services company headquartered in Jacksonville, Florida. Employing over 5,300 people, it operates in shipping, energy, and supply chain management, generating over $2 billion annually. Crowley provides ocean transport, LNG distribution, and logistics for government and commercial clients. In 2024, it expanded renewable energy logistics, supporting offshore wind projects. Its culture emphasizes safety, innovation, and sustainability, with robust employee training. Competing with Maersk, Crowley stands out for its U.S.-focused operations and government contracts. Recent initiatives include decarbonization efforts and digital tracking systems, aligning with green logistics trends.

+ CROWLEY MARITIME [3 News That Matters]

+ CROWLEY MARITIME EXPANDS INTERESTS AT PORT MCKENZIE WITH NEW FUEL TERMINAL STUDY

Crowley Maritime is set to capitalize on the increasing interest surrounding Port McKenzie by securing a permit to study the development of a large fuel terminal. This strategic move could significantly enhance the company's operational footprint in Alaska's Matanuska-Susitna Borough.On the 7th of January 2026, Crowley Maritime announced its plans to conduct a comprehensive study aimed at establishing a fuel terminal at Port McKenzie. The initiative comes amid rising commercial activities and interest in the region, which is poised to benefit from enhanced logistics and transportation capabilities.The proposed fuel terminal at Port McKenzie is expected to play a critical role in supporting local industries and businesses by providing a reliable fuel supply chain. Crowley Maritime's investment in this infrastructure is seen as a response to the growing demand for fuel in both marine and land-based operations in Alaska, a region that has been increasingly recognized for its potential in various sectors.As part of its strategic growth plan, Crowley Maritime is focusing on expanding its services in Alaska, leveraging the advantageous location of Port McKenzie. The terminal is anticipated to not only streamline operations for Crowley but also attract other businesses looking to establish a presence in the region, further enhancing the economic landscape of the Matanuska-Susitna Borough.With the approval of this permit, Crowley Maritime is now positioned to move forward with its feasibility studies and engage with local stakeholders. The development of the fuel terminal could mark a significant milestone for both Crowley and Port McKenzie, potentially paving the way for future investments and growth in the area.

+ CROWLEY MARITIME RESTRUCTURES LEADERSHIP TO DRIVE GROWTH IN SHIPPING AND ENERGY

Crowley Maritime has announced a strategic consolidation of its business structure aimed at enhancing efficiency and value for customers. On 6th January 2026, the company revealed the establishment of two new divisions: Shipping and Logistics, led by Executive Vice President and Division President James C. Fowler, and Energy, overseen by Executive Vice President and Division President Kollin Fencil. This reorganization is designed to improve operational execution and agility, positioning Crowley Maritime for accelerated growth across its shipping, logistics, and energy sectors.The Shipping and Logistics Division, under Fowler's leadership, merges Crowley's logistics, land transportation, and global ship management businesses. This division is set to serve both commercial and government customers, providing a comprehensive array of ocean shipping, inland trucking, multimodal transportation, and warehousing solutions. Fowler emphasized that this new structure will ensure that the entire transportation value chain for their customers is managed cohesively, ultimately driving new growth through safe and efficient supply chain solutions.Meanwhile, the Energy Division, guided by Fencil, consolidates operations including Crowley Fuels in Alaska, liquefied natural gas (LNG) services, and advanced energy solutions. Fencil noted that the division's new structure enables Crowley Maritime to innovate and scale more effectively within traditional and emerging energy markets. With a long history of supporting diverse energy needs across the U.S., the realignment is expected to further enhance Crowley's commitment to meeting evolving customer demands for both traditional fuels and advanced energy solutions.Both Fowler and Fencil bring significant experience to their new roles, having joined Crowley Maritime as senior vice presidents in 2023. Under their respective leadership, the company has seen improvements in financial performance and operational efficiency within their business units. Chief Operating Officer Megan Davidson remarked that this realignment empowers faster decision-making and enhances the company's ability to adapt to market demands, ensuring that Crowley Maritime remains a leader in the maritime, energy, and logistics sectors.Crowley Maritime, a privately held U.S.-owned company, has been providing innovative transportation services for over 130 years. With operations spanning 36 nations and territories, the company is recognized as one of the leading employers of U.S. mariners. As Crowley Maritime embarks on this new chapter, stakeholders will be closely monitoring the impact of these changes on the company's growth trajectory and operational capabilities.

+ CROWLEY AND SEACOR JOIN FORCES IN NEW JONES ACT TANKER VENTURE

Crowley Maritime and SEACOR Holdings, through its subsidiary Seabulk Tankers, have formed a joint venture called Fairwater Holdings to integrate their liquid energy and chemical transportation assets. This collaboration aims to address the evolving demands of the U.S. domestic market. The agreement was finalized on 2nd January 2026, establishing Fairwater Holdings as an independent U.S. Jones Act service provider in the maritime sector.The new entity will amalgamate the strengths of both companies, providing a suite of transportation solutions across the petroleum and chemical trades, alongside third-party ship management services. Fairwater Holdings will manage a fleet comprising 20 ocean-going articulated tug-barges and 11 tankers, primarily under long-term charters. Furthermore, the joint venture will offer crewing and technical management for an additional 21 vessels owned by third parties, thereby enhancing its operational capacity and service offerings.Daniel Thorogood, currently the CEO of Seabulk, will take on the CEO role at Fairwater Holdings upon completion of the deal. With over a decade of experience leading Seabulk and managing SEACOR's short-sea shipping and logistics investments, Thorogood is expected to steer the new venture towards innovation and sustainable practices. In his statement, he emphasized that Fairwater marks a significant milestone in delivering safe and efficient maritime transportation solutions tailored to the needs of the U.S. energy sector.Tom Crowley, Chairman and CEO of Crowley Maritime, expressed confidence in the partnership, describing Seabulk as an ideal collaborator with a proven track record in the maritime industry. He noted that the joint venture aligns with Crowley's growth strategy, further solidifying its position as a leader in the U.S. maritime market, where it operates the largest American-flag petroleum and chemical tank vessel fleet, boasting a capacity exceeding 12 million barrels.As both companies work towards integrating their assets and services, the joint venture is expected to commence operations after meeting customary closing conditions and securing regulatory approvals. This integration effort is anticipated to create a unified and seamless transition for customers, ensuring uninterrupted support for their energy supply chains. Fairwater Holdings is set to begin operations in the first quarter of 2024, marking an exciting new chapter in U.S. maritime transportation.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ CMA CGM LOGISTICS [3 News That Matters]

+ MAWANI STRENGTHENS TRADE TIES WITH CMA CGM’S NEW ROUTE

RIYADH: Maritime connectivity between Saudi Arabia and key Asian and European markets is set to expand after the Kingdom added CMA CGM’s 'Ocean Rise' shipping service to Jeddah Islamic Port. This new route, which commenced on the 3rd of May 2026, connects Jeddah Islamic Port with Kobe, Nagoya, and Yokohama in Japan; Xiamen, Yantian, and Nansha in China; Rotterdam in the Netherlands; Hamburg in Germany; and Southampton in the UK.According to Saudi Arabia’s Ports Authority, known as Mawani, the service has a handling capacity of up to 3,001 twenty-foot equivalent units (TEUs) and is aimed at supporting the movement of Saudi exports and imports in cooperation with international shipping lines. This addition is part of Mawani’s ongoing strategy to enhance the Kingdom’s global logistics capabilities.Mawani stated that this new service aligns with its efforts to enhance Saudi Arabia’s ranking in global performance indicators and support global trade flows. The move is in line with the objectives of the National Transport and Logistics Strategy, which aims to reinforce the Kingdom’s standing as a global logistics hub connecting three continents.During the current period, Mawani has launched 18 maritime shipping services, including the latest CMA CGM offering, with a combined estimated handling capacity of 123,552 TEUs. This expansion aims to ensure efficient delivery of goods to markets and enhance operational readiness across Saudi ports in cooperation with key global shipping companies, including MSC, Maersk, PIL, and Hapag-Lloyd.The Kingdom’s port activity has continued to expand, with Saudi ports recording a 6.66 percent annual increase in cargo throughput in March. Mawani-overseen ports handled 667,882 TEUs in February, marking a significant 20.89 percent increase from the same month a year earlier. Jeddah Islamic Port remains one of the Kingdom’s key maritime gateways, equipped with advanced facilities to bolster its capacity for international trade.

+ CMA CGM EXPANDS FLEET WITH TWO MORE VESSELS REGISTERED IN INDIA

CMACGM is transferring two more containerships into the Indian registry, according to officials in India. This development was reported by the Maritime Executive on April 29th, 2026, and marks an important step in the shipping company's strategy to enhance its presence in the Indian market.The shipping giant began moving vessels under the Indian flag last year to align with revised cabotage regulations. Earlier in April 2026, the CMA CGM Vila do Conde, a 33,434-dwt vessel built in 2009 with a capacity of 2,556 TEU, was officially transferred from the Maltese flag to India. This vessel has been part of CMACGM's fleet since 2023.Papers have also been filed for the transfer of the CMA CGM Semarang, a vessel constructed in 2007 with a deadweight tonnage of 38,000 and a capacity of 2,700 TEU. These two new transfers will bring the total number of vessels moved into the Indian registry by CMACGM to six, following the earlier transfers of the CMA CGM Victoria, CMA CGM Mendelssohn, CMA CGM Diamond, and CMA CGM Manaus.This initiative is largely a response to India's recent changes in cabotage regulations, which now restrict foreign-flagged vessels from carrying domestic cargo. Since January 2026, the Ministry of Ports, Shipping, and Waterways has rolled back a previous exemption that permitted foreign vessels to transship certain cargoes. CMA CGM's proactive approach to register vessels in India allows the company to navigate these new rules effectively while bolstering the domestic shipping industry.CMA CGM was the first major carrier to transition vessels into the Indian registry, with competitors like Maersk and MSC Mediterranean Shipping Company following suit. The new regulations also necessitate that registered vessels employ Indian seafarers, a move CMA CGM has already begun, employing 1,000 Indian seafarers with plans to increase this number to 1,500 by year-end. Furthermore, CMA CGM signed a contract with Cochin Shipyard in February 2026 for the construction of new vessels powered by LNG, reflecting its commitment to growth and sustainability in the region.

+ CMACGM'S AI-FIRST APPROACH TO REDEFINING INDIA'S LOGISTICS LANDSCAPE

As supply chains grow more volatile and customer expectations more exacting, logistics is undergoing a profound reinvention. CMA CGM Group outlines how India is emerging as both a strategic growth market and a global innovation hub, while AI, ecosystem partnerships, and digital platforms are redefining everything from port operations to customer experience. The result: a shift from reactive logistics to predictive, intelligent, and increasingly autonomous supply chains.On 28th April 2026, CMA CGM highlighted its commitment to harnessing artificial intelligence to enhance operational efficiencies within the logistics sector. The company is focusing on developing AI-driven solutions that optimize supply chain management, thereby minimizing disruptions caused by unpredictable market dynamics. By leveraging advanced analytics and machine learning, CMA CGM aims to create a more resilient logistics framework that meets the evolving demands of its customers.CMACGM's strategy involves forging partnerships with Indian tech companies to build a robust ecosystem that supports the integration of AI into logistics operations. This collaborative approach not only accelerates innovation but also positions India as a critical player in the global logistics landscape. The company's vision is to establish AI as a core component of its operational model, facilitating smarter decision-making and improved customer service.In addition to AI integration, CMA CGM is investing in digital platforms that enhance transparency and facilitate real-time tracking of shipments. This technological advancement empowers businesses to make informed decisions, thereby improving overall supply chain performance. As CMACGM continues to evolve its logistics capabilities, the emphasis is placed on creating a seamless experience for clients, ultimately driving growth in the e-commerce and manufacturing sectors.With CMA CGM at the forefront of this transformation, the logistics industry is poised for significant change. The company's focus on an AI-first strategy underscores the importance of innovation in maintaining competitive advantage. As global trade continues to evolve, CMACGM's initiatives in India may serve as a blueprint for other logistics providers aiming to navigate the complexities of modern supply chains.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

urn:contentItem:6JHT-JYG3-RT56-73XB-00000-00CROWLEY MARITIME - SECTOR UPDATES FROM PEERS2026-05-08T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:36:34ZLexisNexisSTOCKCROWLEY MARITIME - SECTOR UPDATES FROM PEERS

DASHBOARD: CROWLEY MARITIME

PermID4295904276Websitehttp://www.crowley.com/IndustryMaritime & Shipping Logistics

ACTIVITIES:

Crowley Maritime Corporation, founded in 1892, is a family-owned logistics and maritime services company headquartered in Jacksonville, Florida. Employing over 5,300 people, it operates in shipping, energy, and supply chain management, generating over $2 billion annually. Crowley provides ocean transport, LNG distribution, and logistics for government and commercial clients. In 2024, it expanded renewable energy logistics, supporting offshore wind projects. Its culture emphasizes safety, innovation, and sustainability, with robust employee training. Competing with Maersk, Crowley stands out for its U.S.-focused operations and government contracts. Recent initiatives include decarbonization efforts and digital tracking systems, aligning with green logistics trends.

+ CROWLEY MARITIME [3 News That Matters]

+ CROWLEY MARITIME EXPANDS INTERESTS AT PORT MCKENZIE WITH NEW FUEL TERMINAL STUDY

Crowley Maritime is set to capitalize on the increasing interest surrounding Port McKenzie by securing a permit to study the development of a large fuel terminal. This strategic move could significantly enhance the company's operational footprint in Alaska's Matanuska-Susitna Borough.On the 7th of January 2026, Crowley Maritime announced its plans to conduct a comprehensive study aimed at establishing a fuel terminal at Port McKenzie. The initiative comes amid rising commercial activities and interest in the region, which is poised to benefit from enhanced logistics and transportation capabilities.The proposed fuel terminal at Port McKenzie is expected to play a critical role in supporting local industries and businesses by providing a reliable fuel supply chain. Crowley Maritime's investment in this infrastructure is seen as a response to the growing demand for fuel in both marine and land-based operations in Alaska, a region that has been increasingly recognized for its potential in various sectors.As part of its strategic growth plan, Crowley Maritime is focusing on expanding its services in Alaska, leveraging the advantageous location of Port McKenzie. The terminal is anticipated to not only streamline operations for Crowley but also attract other businesses looking to establish a presence in the region, further enhancing the economic landscape of the Matanuska-Susitna Borough.With the approval of this permit, Crowley Maritime is now positioned to move forward with its feasibility studies and engage with local stakeholders. The development of the fuel terminal could mark a significant milestone for both Crowley and Port McKenzie, potentially paving the way for future investments and growth in the area.

+ CROWLEY MARITIME RESTRUCTURES LEADERSHIP TO DRIVE GROWTH IN SHIPPING AND ENERGY

Crowley Maritime has announced a strategic consolidation of its business structure aimed at enhancing efficiency and value for customers. On 6th January 2026, the company revealed the establishment of two new divisions: Shipping and Logistics, led by Executive Vice President and Division President James C. Fowler, and Energy, overseen by Executive Vice President and Division President Kollin Fencil. This reorganization is designed to improve operational execution and agility, positioning Crowley Maritime for accelerated growth across its shipping, logistics, and energy sectors.The Shipping and Logistics Division, under Fowler's leadership, merges Crowley's logistics, land transportation, and global ship management businesses. This division is set to serve both commercial and government customers, providing a comprehensive array of ocean shipping, inland trucking, multimodal transportation, and warehousing solutions. Fowler emphasized that this new structure will ensure that the entire transportation value chain for their customers is managed cohesively, ultimately driving new growth through safe and efficient supply chain solutions.Meanwhile, the Energy Division, guided by Fencil, consolidates operations including Crowley Fuels in Alaska, liquefied natural gas (LNG) services, and advanced energy solutions. Fencil noted that the division's new structure enables Crowley Maritime to innovate and scale more effectively within traditional and emerging energy markets. With a long history of supporting diverse energy needs across the U.S., the realignment is expected to further enhance Crowley's commitment to meeting evolving customer demands for both traditional fuels and advanced energy solutions.Both Fowler and Fencil bring significant experience to their new roles, having joined Crowley Maritime as senior vice presidents in 2023. Under their respective leadership, the company has seen improvements in financial performance and operational efficiency within their business units. Chief Operating Officer Megan Davidson remarked that this realignment empowers faster decision-making and enhances the company's ability to adapt to market demands, ensuring that Crowley Maritime remains a leader in the maritime, energy, and logistics sectors.Crowley Maritime, a privately held U.S.-owned company, has been providing innovative transportation services for over 130 years. With operations spanning 36 nations and territories, the company is recognized as one of the leading employers of U.S. mariners. As Crowley Maritime embarks on this new chapter, stakeholders will be closely monitoring the impact of these changes on the company's growth trajectory and operational capabilities.

+ CROWLEY AND SEACOR JOIN FORCES IN NEW JONES ACT TANKER VENTURE

Crowley Maritime and SEACOR Holdings, through its subsidiary Seabulk Tankers, have formed a joint venture called Fairwater Holdings to integrate their liquid energy and chemical transportation assets. This collaboration aims to address the evolving demands of the U.S. domestic market. The agreement was finalized on 2nd January 2026, establishing Fairwater Holdings as an independent U.S. Jones Act service provider in the maritime sector.The new entity will amalgamate the strengths of both companies, providing a suite of transportation solutions across the petroleum and chemical trades, alongside third-party ship management services. Fairwater Holdings will manage a fleet comprising 20 ocean-going articulated tug-barges and 11 tankers, primarily under long-term charters. Furthermore, the joint venture will offer crewing and technical management for an additional 21 vessels owned by third parties, thereby enhancing its operational capacity and service offerings.Daniel Thorogood, currently the CEO of Seabulk, will take on the CEO role at Fairwater Holdings upon completion of the deal. With over a decade of experience leading Seabulk and managing SEACOR's short-sea shipping and logistics investments, Thorogood is expected to steer the new venture towards innovation and sustainable practices. In his statement, he emphasized that Fairwater marks a significant milestone in delivering safe and efficient maritime transportation solutions tailored to the needs of the U.S. energy sector.Tom Crowley, Chairman and CEO of Crowley Maritime, expressed confidence in the partnership, describing Seabulk as an ideal collaborator with a proven track record in the maritime industry. He noted that the joint venture aligns with Crowley's growth strategy, further solidifying its position as a leader in the U.S. maritime market, where it operates the largest American-flag petroleum and chemical tank vessel fleet, boasting a capacity exceeding 12 million barrels.As both companies work towards integrating their assets and services, the joint venture is expected to commence operations after meeting customary closing conditions and securing regulatory approvals. This integration effort is anticipated to create a unified and seamless transition for customers, ensuring uninterrupted support for their energy supply chains. Fairwater Holdings is set to begin operations in the first quarter of 2024, marking an exciting new chapter in U.S. maritime transportation.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ CMA CGM LOGISTICS [3 News That Matters]

+ MAWANI STRENGTHENS TRADE TIES WITH CMA CGM’S NEW ROUTE

RIYADH: Maritime connectivity between Saudi Arabia and key Asian and European markets is set to expand after the Kingdom added CMA CGM’s 'Ocean Rise' shipping service to Jeddah Islamic Port. This new route, which commenced on the 3rd of May 2026, connects Jeddah Islamic Port with Kobe, Nagoya, and Yokohama in Japan; Xiamen, Yantian, and Nansha in China; Rotterdam in the Netherlands; Hamburg in Germany; and Southampton in the UK.According to Saudi Arabia’s Ports Authority, known as Mawani, the service has a handling capacity of up to 3,001 twenty-foot equivalent units (TEUs) and is aimed at supporting the movement of Saudi exports and imports in cooperation with international shipping lines. This addition is part of Mawani’s ongoing strategy to enhance the Kingdom’s global logistics capabilities.Mawani stated that this new service aligns with its efforts to enhance Saudi Arabia’s ranking in global performance indicators and support global trade flows. The move is in line with the objectives of the National Transport and Logistics Strategy, which aims to reinforce the Kingdom’s standing as a global logistics hub connecting three continents.During the current period, Mawani has launched 18 maritime shipping services, including the latest CMA CGM offering, with a combined estimated handling capacity of 123,552 TEUs. This expansion aims to ensure efficient delivery of goods to markets and enhance operational readiness across Saudi ports in cooperation with key global shipping companies, including MSC, Maersk, PIL, and Hapag-Lloyd.The Kingdom’s port activity has continued to expand, with Saudi ports recording a 6.66 percent annual increase in cargo throughput in March. Mawani-overseen ports handled 667,882 TEUs in February, marking a significant 20.89 percent increase from the same month a year earlier. Jeddah Islamic Port remains one of the Kingdom’s key maritime gateways, equipped with advanced facilities to bolster its capacity for international trade.

+ CMA CGM EXPANDS FLEET WITH TWO MORE VESSELS REGISTERED IN INDIA

CMACGM is transferring two more containerships into the Indian registry, according to officials in India. This development was reported by the Maritime Executive on April 29th, 2026, and marks an important step in the shipping company's strategy to enhance its presence in the Indian market.The shipping giant began moving vessels under the Indian flag last year to align with revised cabotage regulations. Earlier in April 2026, the CMA CGM Vila do Conde, a 33,434-dwt vessel built in 2009 with a capacity of 2,556 TEU, was officially transferred from the Maltese flag to India. This vessel has been part of CMACGM's fleet since 2023.Papers have also been filed for the transfer of the CMA CGM Semarang, a vessel constructed in 2007 with a deadweight tonnage of 38,000 and a capacity of 2,700 TEU. These two new transfers will bring the total number of vessels moved into the Indian registry by CMACGM to six, following the earlier transfers of the CMA CGM Victoria, CMA CGM Mendelssohn, CMA CGM Diamond, and CMA CGM Manaus.This initiative is largely a response to India's recent changes in cabotage regulations, which now restrict foreign-flagged vessels from carrying domestic cargo. Since January 2026, the Ministry of Ports, Shipping, and Waterways has rolled back a previous exemption that permitted foreign vessels to transship certain cargoes. CMA CGM's proactive approach to register vessels in India allows the company to navigate these new rules effectively while bolstering the domestic shipping industry.CMA CGM was the first major carrier to transition vessels into the Indian registry, with competitors like Maersk and MSC Mediterranean Shipping Company following suit. The new regulations also necessitate that registered vessels employ Indian seafarers, a move CMA CGM has already begun, employing 1,000 Indian seafarers with plans to increase this number to 1,500 by year-end. Furthermore, CMA CGM signed a contract with Cochin Shipyard in February 2026 for the construction of new vessels powered by LNG, reflecting its commitment to growth and sustainability in the region.

+ CMACGM'S AI-FIRST APPROACH TO REDEFINING INDIA'S LOGISTICS LANDSCAPE

As supply chains grow more volatile and customer expectations more exacting, logistics is undergoing a profound reinvention. CMA CGM Group outlines how India is emerging as both a strategic growth market and a global innovation hub, while AI, ecosystem partnerships, and digital platforms are redefining everything from port operations to customer experience. The result: a shift from reactive logistics to predictive, intelligent, and increasingly autonomous supply chains.On 28th April 2026, CMA CGM highlighted its commitment to harnessing artificial intelligence to enhance operational efficiencies within the logistics sector. The company is focusing on developing AI-driven solutions that optimize supply chain management, thereby minimizing disruptions caused by unpredictable market dynamics. By leveraging advanced analytics and machine learning, CMA CGM aims to create a more resilient logistics framework that meets the evolving demands of its customers.CMACGM's strategy involves forging partnerships with Indian tech companies to build a robust ecosystem that supports the integration of AI into logistics operations. This collaborative approach not only accelerates innovation but also positions India as a critical player in the global logistics landscape. The company's vision is to establish AI as a core component of its operational model, facilitating smarter decision-making and improved customer service.In addition to AI integration, CMA CGM is investing in digital platforms that enhance transparency and facilitate real-time tracking of shipments. This technological advancement empowers businesses to make informed decisions, thereby improving overall supply chain performance. As CMACGM continues to evolve its logistics capabilities, the emphasis is placed on creating a seamless experience for clients, ultimately driving growth in the e-commerce and manufacturing sectors.With CMA CGM at the forefront of this transformation, the logistics industry is poised for significant change. The company's focus on an AI-first strategy underscores the importance of innovation in maintaining competitive advantage. As global trade continues to evolve, CMACGM's initiatives in India may serve as a blueprint for other logistics providers aiming to navigate the complexities of modern supply chains.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

urn:contentItem:6JHK-KSM3-S0RF-V525-00000-00CROWLEY MARITIME - THE RECENT SECTOR HEADLINES WITH PEERS2026-05-07T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:36:34ZLexisNexisSTOCKCROWLEY MARITIME - THE RECENT SECTOR HEADLINES WITH PEERS

DASHBOARD: CROWLEY MARITIME

PermID4295904276Websitehttp://www.crowley.com/IndustryMaritime & Shipping Logistics

ACTIVITIES:

Crowley Maritime Corporation, founded in 1892, is a family-owned logistics and maritime services company headquartered in Jacksonville, Florida. Employing over 5,300 people, it operates in shipping, energy, and supply chain management, generating over $2 billion annually. Crowley provides ocean transport, LNG distribution, and logistics for government and commercial clients. In 2024, it expanded renewable energy logistics, supporting offshore wind projects. Its culture emphasizes safety, innovation, and sustainability, with robust employee training. Competing with Maersk, Crowley stands out for its U.S.-focused operations and government contracts. Recent initiatives include decarbonization efforts and digital tracking systems, aligning with green logistics trends.

+ CROWLEY MARITIME [3 News That Matters]

+ CROWLEY MARITIME EXPANDS INTERESTS AT PORT MCKENZIE WITH NEW FUEL TERMINAL STUDY

Crowley Maritime is set to capitalize on the increasing interest surrounding Port McKenzie by securing a permit to study the development of a large fuel terminal. This strategic move could significantly enhance the company's operational footprint in Alaska's Matanuska-Susitna Borough.On the 7th of January 2026, Crowley Maritime announced its plans to conduct a comprehensive study aimed at establishing a fuel terminal at Port McKenzie. The initiative comes amid rising commercial activities and interest in the region, which is poised to benefit from enhanced logistics and transportation capabilities.The proposed fuel terminal at Port McKenzie is expected to play a critical role in supporting local industries and businesses by providing a reliable fuel supply chain. Crowley Maritime's investment in this infrastructure is seen as a response to the growing demand for fuel in both marine and land-based operations in Alaska, a region that has been increasingly recognized for its potential in various sectors.As part of its strategic growth plan, Crowley Maritime is focusing on expanding its services in Alaska, leveraging the advantageous location of Port McKenzie. The terminal is anticipated to not only streamline operations for Crowley but also attract other businesses looking to establish a presence in the region, further enhancing the economic landscape of the Matanuska-Susitna Borough.With the approval of this permit, Crowley Maritime is now positioned to move forward with its feasibility studies and engage with local stakeholders. The development of the fuel terminal could mark a significant milestone for both Crowley and Port McKenzie, potentially paving the way for future investments and growth in the area.

+ CROWLEY MARITIME RESTRUCTURES LEADERSHIP TO DRIVE GROWTH IN SHIPPING AND ENERGY

Crowley Maritime has announced a strategic consolidation of its business structure aimed at enhancing efficiency and value for customers. On 6th January 2026, the company revealed the establishment of two new divisions: Shipping and Logistics, led by Executive Vice President and Division President James C. Fowler, and Energy, overseen by Executive Vice President and Division President Kollin Fencil. This reorganization is designed to improve operational execution and agility, positioning Crowley Maritime for accelerated growth across its shipping, logistics, and energy sectors.The Shipping and Logistics Division, under Fowler's leadership, merges Crowley's logistics, land transportation, and global ship management businesses. This division is set to serve both commercial and government customers, providing a comprehensive array of ocean shipping, inland trucking, multimodal transportation, and warehousing solutions. Fowler emphasized that this new structure will ensure that the entire transportation value chain for their customers is managed cohesively, ultimately driving new growth through safe and efficient supply chain solutions.Meanwhile, the Energy Division, guided by Fencil, consolidates operations including Crowley Fuels in Alaska, liquefied natural gas (LNG) services, and advanced energy solutions. Fencil noted that the division's new structure enables Crowley Maritime to innovate and scale more effectively within traditional and emerging energy markets. With a long history of supporting diverse energy needs across the U.S., the realignment is expected to further enhance Crowley's commitment to meeting evolving customer demands for both traditional fuels and advanced energy solutions.Both Fowler and Fencil bring significant experience to their new roles, having joined Crowley Maritime as senior vice presidents in 2023. Under their respective leadership, the company has seen improvements in financial performance and operational efficiency within their business units. Chief Operating Officer Megan Davidson remarked that this realignment empowers faster decision-making and enhances the company's ability to adapt to market demands, ensuring that Crowley Maritime remains a leader in the maritime, energy, and logistics sectors.Crowley Maritime, a privately held U.S.-owned company, has been providing innovative transportation services for over 130 years. With operations spanning 36 nations and territories, the company is recognized as one of the leading employers of U.S. mariners. As Crowley Maritime embarks on this new chapter, stakeholders will be closely monitoring the impact of these changes on the company's growth trajectory and operational capabilities.

+ CROWLEY AND SEACOR JOIN FORCES IN NEW JONES ACT TANKER VENTURE

Crowley Maritime and SEACOR Holdings, through its subsidiary Seabulk Tankers, have formed a joint venture called Fairwater Holdings to integrate their liquid energy and chemical transportation assets. This collaboration aims to address the evolving demands of the U.S. domestic market. The agreement was finalized on 2nd January 2026, establishing Fairwater Holdings as an independent U.S. Jones Act service provider in the maritime sector.The new entity will amalgamate the strengths of both companies, providing a suite of transportation solutions across the petroleum and chemical trades, alongside third-party ship management services. Fairwater Holdings will manage a fleet comprising 20 ocean-going articulated tug-barges and 11 tankers, primarily under long-term charters. Furthermore, the joint venture will offer crewing and technical management for an additional 21 vessels owned by third parties, thereby enhancing its operational capacity and service offerings.Daniel Thorogood, currently the CEO of Seabulk, will take on the CEO role at Fairwater Holdings upon completion of the deal. With over a decade of experience leading Seabulk and managing SEACOR's short-sea shipping and logistics investments, Thorogood is expected to steer the new venture towards innovation and sustainable practices. In his statement, he emphasized that Fairwater marks a significant milestone in delivering safe and efficient maritime transportation solutions tailored to the needs of the U.S. energy sector.Tom Crowley, Chairman and CEO of Crowley Maritime, expressed confidence in the partnership, describing Seabulk as an ideal collaborator with a proven track record in the maritime industry. He noted that the joint venture aligns with Crowley's growth strategy, further solidifying its position as a leader in the U.S. maritime market, where it operates the largest American-flag petroleum and chemical tank vessel fleet, boasting a capacity exceeding 12 million barrels.As both companies work towards integrating their assets and services, the joint venture is expected to commence operations after meeting customary closing conditions and securing regulatory approvals. This integration effort is anticipated to create a unified and seamless transition for customers, ensuring uninterrupted support for their energy supply chains. Fairwater Holdings is set to begin operations in the first quarter of 2024, marking an exciting new chapter in U.S. maritime transportation.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ CMA CGM LOGISTICS [3 News That Matters]

+ MAWANI STRENGTHENS TRADE TIES WITH CMA CGM’S NEW ROUTE

RIYADH: Maritime connectivity between Saudi Arabia and key Asian and European markets is set to expand after the Kingdom added CMA CGM’s 'Ocean Rise' shipping service to Jeddah Islamic Port. This new route, which commenced on the 3rd of May 2026, connects Jeddah Islamic Port with Kobe, Nagoya, and Yokohama in Japan; Xiamen, Yantian, and Nansha in China; Rotterdam in the Netherlands; Hamburg in Germany; and Southampton in the UK.According to Saudi Arabia’s Ports Authority, known as Mawani, the service has a handling capacity of up to 3,001 twenty-foot equivalent units (TEUs) and is aimed at supporting the movement of Saudi exports and imports in cooperation with international shipping lines. This addition is part of Mawani’s ongoing strategy to enhance the Kingdom’s global logistics capabilities.Mawani stated that this new service aligns with its efforts to enhance Saudi Arabia’s ranking in global performance indicators and support global trade flows. The move is in line with the objectives of the National Transport and Logistics Strategy, which aims to reinforce the Kingdom’s standing as a global logistics hub connecting three continents.During the current period, Mawani has launched 18 maritime shipping services, including the latest CMA CGM offering, with a combined estimated handling capacity of 123,552 TEUs. This expansion aims to ensure efficient delivery of goods to markets and enhance operational readiness across Saudi ports in cooperation with key global shipping companies, including MSC, Maersk, PIL, and Hapag-Lloyd.The Kingdom’s port activity has continued to expand, with Saudi ports recording a 6.66 percent annual increase in cargo throughput in March. Mawani-overseen ports handled 667,882 TEUs in February, marking a significant 20.89 percent increase from the same month a year earlier. Jeddah Islamic Port remains one of the Kingdom’s key maritime gateways, equipped with advanced facilities to bolster its capacity for international trade.

+ CMA CGM EXPANDS FLEET WITH TWO MORE VESSELS REGISTERED IN INDIA

CMACGM is transferring two more containerships into the Indian registry, according to officials in India. This development was reported by the Maritime Executive on April 29th, 2026, and marks an important step in the shipping company's strategy to enhance its presence in the Indian market.The shipping giant began moving vessels under the Indian flag last year to align with revised cabotage regulations. Earlier in April 2026, the CMA CGM Vila do Conde, a 33,434-dwt vessel built in 2009 with a capacity of 2,556 TEU, was officially transferred from the Maltese flag to India. This vessel has been part of CMACGM's fleet since 2023.Papers have also been filed for the transfer of the CMA CGM Semarang, a vessel constructed in 2007 with a deadweight tonnage of 38,000 and a capacity of 2,700 TEU. These two new transfers will bring the total number of vessels moved into the Indian registry by CMACGM to six, following the earlier transfers of the CMA CGM Victoria, CMA CGM Mendelssohn, CMA CGM Diamond, and CMA CGM Manaus.This initiative is largely a response to India's recent changes in cabotage regulations, which now restrict foreign-flagged vessels from carrying domestic cargo. Since January 2026, the Ministry of Ports, Shipping, and Waterways has rolled back a previous exemption that permitted foreign vessels to transship certain cargoes. CMA CGM's proactive approach to register vessels in India allows the company to navigate these new rules effectively while bolstering the domestic shipping industry.CMA CGM was the first major carrier to transition vessels into the Indian registry, with competitors like Maersk and MSC Mediterranean Shipping Company following suit. The new regulations also necessitate that registered vessels employ Indian seafarers, a move CMA CGM has already begun, employing 1,000 Indian seafarers with plans to increase this number to 1,500 by year-end. Furthermore, CMA CGM signed a contract with Cochin Shipyard in February 2026 for the construction of new vessels powered by LNG, reflecting its commitment to growth and sustainability in the region.

+ CMACGM'S AI-FIRST APPROACH TO REDEFINING INDIA'S LOGISTICS LANDSCAPE

As supply chains grow more volatile and customer expectations more exacting, logistics is undergoing a profound reinvention. CMA CGM Group outlines how India is emerging as both a strategic growth market and a global innovation hub, while AI, ecosystem partnerships, and digital platforms are redefining everything from port operations to customer experience. The result: a shift from reactive logistics to predictive, intelligent, and increasingly autonomous supply chains.On 28th April 2026, CMA CGM highlighted its commitment to harnessing artificial intelligence to enhance operational efficiencies within the logistics sector. The company is focusing on developing AI-driven solutions that optimize supply chain management, thereby minimizing disruptions caused by unpredictable market dynamics. By leveraging advanced analytics and machine learning, CMA CGM aims to create a more resilient logistics framework that meets the evolving demands of its customers.CMACGM's strategy involves forging partnerships with Indian tech companies to build a robust ecosystem that supports the integration of AI into logistics operations. This collaborative approach not only accelerates innovation but also positions India as a critical player in the global logistics landscape. The company's vision is to establish AI as a core component of its operational model, facilitating smarter decision-making and improved customer service.In addition to AI integration, CMA CGM is investing in digital platforms that enhance transparency and facilitate real-time tracking of shipments. This technological advancement empowers businesses to make informed decisions, thereby improving overall supply chain performance. As CMACGM continues to evolve its logistics capabilities, the emphasis is placed on creating a seamless experience for clients, ultimately driving growth in the e-commerce and manufacturing sectors.With CMA CGM at the forefront of this transformation, the logistics industry is poised for significant change. The company's focus on an AI-first strategy underscores the importance of innovation in maintaining competitive advantage. As global trade continues to evolve, CMACGM's initiatives in India may serve as a blueprint for other logistics providers aiming to navigate the complexities of modern supply chains.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

urn:contentItem:6JHR-Y0K3-RSK9-H47N-00000-00FORTRESS INVESTMENT GROUP LLC - RECENT INDUSTRY AND PEER NEWS ROUNDUP2026-05-07T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:46:15ZLexisNexisSTOCKFORTRESS INVESTMENT GROUP LLC - RECENT INDUSTRY AND PEER NEWS ROUNDUP

DASHBOARD: FORTRESS INVESTMENT GROUP LLC

PermID4295901538Websitehttps://www.fortress.com/IndustryAlternative InvestmentsAddress1345 Avenue OF The Americas Florida 46 New York, NY, 10105-4302 United States

ACTIVITIES:

Fortress Investment Group LLC, founded in 1998, is a global investment firm headquartered in New York City. Employing over 900 people, it manages $48 billion in assets across private equity, credit, and real estate. Acquired by SoftBank in 2017, Fortress operates independently, focusing on distressed assets and infrastructure. In 2024, it invested $2 billion in renewable energy projects. Its culture emphasizes risk management, innovation, and agility, with training in asset valuation and fintech. Competing with Apollo Global, Fortress stands out for its credit expertise and opportunistic investments. Recent initiatives include blockchain-based real estate funds and ESG-focused portfolios, aligning with investment trends. Fortress diversified strategy ensures resilience in volatile markets.

+ FORTRESS INVESTMENT GROUP LLC [3 News That Matters]

+ PURE SALMON JAPAN ADVANCES FLAGSHIP LAND-BASED FACILITY AS FORTRESS COMMITS USD 180 MILLION

Pure Salmon Japan secured over USD 180 million in preferred-note financing led by Fortress Investment Group affiliates. The deal closed on 1st April 2026 and will fund the build-out of the company’s land-based salmon facility in Japan.The investment signals robust institutional support for a project targeting 10,000 tonnes of annual production and aiming to become Japan’s largest land-based salmon operation. The facility will implement recirculating aquaculture system (RAS) technology to help reduce imports and strengthen domestic seafood supply.CEO Erol Emed commented, "We are deeply encouraged by the dedication and conviction shown by our investors in supporting Pure Salmon Japan through this financing. Their commitment underscores the growing relevance of sustainable domestic seafood production in Japan." Fortress Investment Group, with a long-standing presence in Japan, brings both capital and strategic know-how. Eric Golden, Co-Head of Asia at Fortress, highlighted the project’s role in enhancing Japan's food production infrastructure.

+ FORTRESS INVESTMENT GROUP EXPANDS PALM COAST SENIOR HOUSING WITH TUSCAN GARDENS ACQUISITION

Fortress Investment Group LLC has announced the acquisition of Tuscan Gardens of Palm Coast, a Class A seniors housing community in Palm Coast, Florida, with the deal dated 12th March 2026.Tuscan Gardens has 130 units and offers a full continuum of care, including assisted living, enhanced assisted living and memory care, on an 11-acre site about one mile from the Atlantic Ocean.Established in 2018, the Palm Coast community serves an affluent and growing senior population along Florida's northeast coast, and occupancy has improved under AgeWell Solvere Living since 2023.Fortress notes the asset's location and quality position it for long-term growth in the senior housing sector, aligning with the firm's strategy to invest in well-located properties.

+ FORTRESS CLOSES $96 MILLION LOAN TO SUPPORT JASPER'S CLASS A APARTMENT COMMUNITY IN DOWNTOWN LOS ANGELES

Fortress Investment Group closed a $96 million whole loan for Jasper, a 296-unit Class A multifamily community in DowntownLos Angeles. The financing was finalized on Feb. 9, 2026, as Fortress continues to back high-quality urban housing developments near major university campuses.Jasper is developed by Cityview and situated near the University of Southern California. The property comprises studios, and one- and two-bedroom units with upscale finishes and a broad amenity package that includes affordable housing units. Proceeds will refinance construction debt and support leasing progress for the new asset.Cityview's Damian Gancman praised Fortress for a smooth, solution-driven process and seamless execution from start to finish. Fortress's Noah Shore emphasized Jasper's fit with the firm's focus on backing well-capitalized, newly built multifamily projects with experienced sponsors, reflecting Fortress's broader investment approach.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ KKR & CO [3 News That Matters]

+ VERTIS INFRASTRUCTURE TRUST (NYSE:VIT) PURSUES DRAFT PAPERS FOR ₹3,000 CRORE INVIT IPO BY END MAY

Vertis Infrastructure Trust (NYSE:VIT) plans to file draft papers for an initial public offering by end May 2026, targeting a raise of up to ₹3,000 crore through a mix of primary and secondary share sales. The InvIT operates a portfolio of toll and annuity road projects across India, largely assembled by KKR's Asian infrastructure fund since 2021, with growth in 2024-25 through the acquisition of hybrid annuity model projects from PNC Infratech. The portfolio comprises 28 road projects across nine states, valued at ₹26,500 crore.The final size of the offer remains under discussion, as management weighs limited requirements for fresh primary capital against a competitive fundraising environment for InvITs. Industry observers note that the market for infrastructure InvITs continues to attract institutional investors seeking stable, long-term yields, even as macro headwinds persist.Momentum in the sector is evident, with NHAI raising over ₹9,000 crore through two InvIT offerings in March and Citius Transnet Investment Trust raising ₹1,105 crore in April. Vertis Infrastructure Trust seeks to participate in this ongoing cycle with a disciplined approach to capital formation

+ KKR & CO. INC. (NYSE:KKR) COMMITS $10 BILLION TO AI POWER AND DATA CENTER EXPANSION

KKR & Co. Inc. (NYSE:KKR) allocates $10 billion to AI power plants and data centers, expanding the firm’s footprint in AI infrastructure.On 3rd May 2026, this move aligns with a broader trend where Google, Microsoft, Amazon, and Meta are investing over $100 billion worldwide in AI infrastructure.The investment seeks long-term power agreements to secure capacity as demand from AI data centers grows.Geopolitical dynamics are noted, with Canada and Germany coordinating to counterbalance U.S.–China AI influence, and Google's prospects are cited by market analysts as potentially supporting a stock price near $310.

+ KKR & CO. INC. (NYSE: KKR) TO SHARE STRATEGIC OUTLOOK AT BANK OF AMERICA SECURITIES CONFERENCE

KKR & Co. Inc. (NYSE: KKR) will have its Chief Financial Officer Robert H. Lewin speak at the Bank of America Securities 2026 Financial Services Conference on Feb. 10, 2026 at 2:40 PM ET. The presentation will be available via a live webcast in the Investor Center on KKR's site, with a replay obtainable shortly after the event. KKR operates as a global investment firm focused on alternative asset management, capital markets, and insurance solutions, with a portfolio across private equity, credit, and real assets and partnerships that manage hedge funds. Its insurance activities are conducted through Global Atlantic Financial Group, providing retirement, life, and reinsurance products. For more information on KKR & Co. Inc. (NYSE: KKR) and its strategies, visit the company’s website. Investor Relations contact details are available there.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ CARLYLE GROUP [3 News That Matters]

+ CARLYLE GROUP INC. (NASDAQ:CG) ACQUIRES KNACK RCM AND EQUALIZERCM TO BUILD INTEGRATED RCM PLATFORM

Carlyle Group Inc. (NASDAQ:CG) acquires a majority stake in Knack RCM and EqualizeRCM, broadening its healthcare portfolio. The May 4, 2026 release notes the transaction includes a majority stake in both firms, with terms undisclosed. Founders Rajiv Sharma and Nagi Rao will reinvest a portion of their proceeds into the platform to support ongoing developmentKnack RCM and EqualizeRCM serve physician groups, durable medical equipment providers, and rural hospitals, and the partnership aims to address rural cost reports and intricate anesthesia cases, according to Knack Chief Executive Gautam Barai. Carlyle views this as a step to deepen its presence in the RCM sector and to pursue further opportunities in healthcare managementThe deal underscores Carlyle's focus on technology-enabled revenue cycle management and the potential to deliver enhanced, analytics-driven solutions to providers

+ CARLYLE GROUP L.P. (NASDAQ:CG) ADVANCES REVENUE CYCLE MANAGEMENT THROUGH INTEGRATED HEALTHCARE PLATFORM

Carlyle Group L.P. (NASDAQ:CG) announced on the 4th of May, 2026 that it is combining Knack RCM and EqualizeRCM into a single platform focused on advanced digital capabilities in healthcare services. The integrated platform targets physician groups, rural hospitals, and specialty providers, aiming to address administrative complexity and evolving care models. Knack supplies a globally distributed operational framework while Equalize adds predictive analytics and process optimization tools to improve revenue cycle management, billing, and payer coordination. The move reflects a broader trend toward technology-driven healthcare services as automation and data-driven systems become central to clinical administration.

+ CARLYLE GROUP L.P. (NASDAQ:CG) APPOINTS TRIO AS CO-PRESIDENTS TO DRIVE UNIFIED DECISION-MAKING

Carlyle Group L.P. (NASDAQ:CG) appoints three senior executives as co-presidents under Chief Executive Officer Harvey Schwartz, effective after 22nd April 2026, to accelerate decision-making and align operations with its evolving strategy.The co-presidents will share oversight of investment priorities, fundraising, and day-to-day management across Carlyle Group L.P. (NASDAQ:CG)'s businesses, with the goal of improving execution as market conditions shift.Schwartz, who assumed leadership during a transitional period, seeks clearer accountability and faster decisions, a model that aligns with practices at other major firms to maintain momentum.The firm will assess the impact on investment activity and financial performance in forthcoming quarters.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ OAKTREE CAPITAL MANAGEMENT LP [3 News That Matters]

+ ESTABLISHMENT LABS INKS $300 MILLION TERM LOAN WITH OAKTREE TO BACK MOTIVA PLATFORM EXPANSION

Establishment Labs Holdings Inc. secured a US$300 million senior secured first-lien term loan facility from Oaktree Capital Management, completed on 3rd May 2026, with refinancing through 2031 and US$35 million reserved for growth.The loan offers fixed-rate, no-amortization terms and a first-priority lien on assets, designed to boost funding capacity and balance-sheet flexibility as the company pursues expansion.Analysts view Motiva as a driver of global adoption for its implants and minimally invasive procedures. Management projects 2026 revenue of US$264-266 million, up about 25-26% year over year. The facility provides liquidity to address losses and cash burn without forcing an immediate equity raise, though ongoing cash burn remains a risk.Some watchers project roughly 30% revenue growth with earnings around US$73.5 million by 2029, a trajectory that could underpin optimism about growth while highlighting the path to profitability.

+ BIOXCEL THERAPEUTICS ANNOUNCES NINTH AMENDMENT TO CREDIT AGREEMENT AND NEW WARRANT ISSUANCE

BioXcel Therapeutics, Inc. said it has expanded its financing framework through a Fourth Amended and Restated Registration Rights Agreement with Oaktree Capital Management and other institutional holders. The new agreement, disclosed in a Form 8-K, was announced with the filing date of 18th April 2026 and encompasses changes to prior terms as well as the issuance of additional equity warrants to lenders, reflecting the company’s ongoing capital management strategy. Separately, the Ninth Amendment to BioXcel's Credit Agreement was executed on 27th March 2026, and on 15th April 2026 the company issued unregistered warrants in a private placement. The warrants give lenders the right to acquire more BioXcel common stock, and the private placement means the warrants were not registered under the Securities Act of 1933. BioXcel also stated it will file registration statements with the SEC to enable potential resale of the underlying shares, a move intended to provide liquidity and investor transparency. Lenders have said they will hold the securities for investment purposes, which could reduce immediate selling pressure but may still lead to a higher total share count if the warrants are exercised and the shares registered for resale. The company notes it is not an emerging growth company and will continue to comply with full regulatory reporting. With participation from multiple Oaktree entities, the deal illustrates a continued commitment to securities compliance and to monitoring upcoming filings and announcements that could influence BioXcel’s stock performance.

+ OAKTREE, NORTH MILL EXECUTE $355.8 MILLION ASSET-BACKED SECURITIZATION

Oaktree Capital Management and North Mill Equipment Finance have closed a $355.8 million asset-backed securitization, OAKEF 2026-1, on 8th April 2026, reflecting healthy investor demand for quality equipment finance assets.The securitized assets originated from Midland Equipment Finance in a November 2025 deal. The notes were rated AAA through A and drew strong demand, about 6.3 times subscriptions, while delivering a weighted average spread of 99 basis points and a 4.7% yield.Truist Securities led structuring with Deutsche Bank as co-manager. Brendan Beer of Oaktree noted the market reception as evidence of investor confidence in the strategy and in North Mill's role.North Mill's David Lee emphasized asset quality and the servicing platform, reiterating that NMEF will continue as the primary servicer for the assets, ensuring ongoing management.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ APOLLO GLOBAL MANAGEMENT [3 News That Matters]

+ MEDICAL SURGICAL SOLUTIONS, LLC AT $9.62 BILLION POST-MONEY VALUATION WITH $1.25 BILLION FROM APOLLO GLOBAL MANAGEMENT

Medical Surgical Solutions, LLC secures $1.25 billion from Apollo Funds, managed by Apollo Global Management, Inc., delivering a 13% stake via convertible preferred shares. The investment was formalized on April 20, 2026 and is contingent on regulatory approvals and customary closing conditions. The post-money valuation stands at about $9.62 billion, and the capital will be used to bolster operations and expand the company’s market footprint as Apollo provides strategic backing through its funds.

+ APOLLO GLOBAL MANAGEMENT LLC (NYSE:APO) REPORTS $1.25 BILLION CONVERTIBLE PREFERRED EQUITY INVESTMENT FOR 13% MCKESSON MEDICAL-SURGICAL SOLUTIONS STAKE

On Apr. 20, 2026, Apollo Global Management LLC (NYSE:APO) Invests $1.25 Billion in McKesson Corporation (NYSE:MCK) for a 13% stake in its Medical-Surgical Solutions division via convertible preferred equity, valuing the unit at approximately $13 billion. The capital supports McKesson's planned separation of the Medical-Surgical Solutions unit and funds strategic initiatives within the business. The transaction reflects a strategic alignment between Apollo and McKesson as the latter moves toward separating its Medical-Surgical Solutions segment and concentrating on core operations. Apollo Global Management oversees about $938.4 billion in assets under management and has a market capitalization near $72.06 billion, with a trailing P/E ratio of 22.58, indicating market expectations for continued growth. Insider activity raises caution, with six insider sells versus one insider buy over the past year, and Apollo's financial strength is rated 6 out of 10. Market participants will watch the arrangement for potential impacts on McKesson's separation plan.

+ APOLLO BROADENS UK HOUSING FOOTPRINT VIA GATEHOUSE LIVING GROUP PURCHASE

Apollo Global Management Inc. has acquired Gatehouse Living Group from Gatehouse Bank, completed on 1st April 2026, bringing Gatehouse Investment Management and Ascend Properties into its UK housing platform.Financial terms were not disclosed.GLG is a Build-to-Rent single-family housing leader with more than 10,000 properties and 5,000 homes acquired, and will continue to be led by CEO Paul Stockwell with Ascend Properties handling external portfolio management.The deal strengthens Apollo's UK strategy and follows its existing interests in Miller Homes and Foundation Home Loans, while Gatehouse Bank focuses on Shariah-compliant financing.

+ ARES MANAGEMENT [3 News That Matters]

+ ARES MANAGEMENT L.P. (NYSE:ARES) FACILITATES GROWTH THROUGH DIRECT LENDING WITH ENCORE FIRE PROTECTION LOENBRO AND ARCHIMED

Ares Management L.P. (NYSE:ARES) Posts $9.5 Billion in U.S. Direct Lending Across 70 Transactions in Q1 2026. The update dated 4th of May 2026 Reports demand for flexible capital solutions across sectors. The firm also highlights notable Q1 activity.Over the 12 months ended March 31, 2026, Ares Closes approximately $53.0 billion across 348 transactions, underscoring the breadth of its direct lending platform. In Q1, Ares acted as administrative agent for Permira's growth plans for Encore Fire Protection, led Kohlberg's acquisition of Loenbro, and served as lead arranger for ARCHIMED's credit facility backing the North American Science Association.As of March 31, 2026, Ares Management L.P.'s global platform Totals over $644 billion in assets under management across North America, South America, Europe, Asia Pacific and the Middle East

+ ARES MANAGEMENT L.P. (NYSE:ARES) SECURES $1.6 BILLION FOR FIFTH JAPAN LOGISTICS DEVELOPMENT FUND WITH BROAD INVESTMENT MOMENTUM

Ares Management L.P. (NYSE:ARES) Posts $1.6 billion for its fifth Japan-focused industrial real estate development fund, signaling continued investor appetite for Japanese logistics assets in 2026. The first close is expected in spring, as noted during an earnings call by chief executive Michael AroughetiThe fund follows more than four years after GLP closed its fourth Japan development fund at 412 billion yen ($3.7 billion). Ares completed the acquisition of GLP's international business in 2025, which has since been rebranded as GCP InternationalThe firm’s broader capital activity includes Ada Infrastructure, which has raised $2.4 billion for a data center-focused vehicle with seed assets including a $1.3 billion contribution from the Canada Pension Plan Investment Board. Blair Jacobson, Ares' co-president, said the fundraising aims to address seed assets and the robust pipeline. In the first quarter of 2026, Ares also raised $500 million for its Asia Pacific credit strategy, supported by commitments to SSG Fund VIINotable returning investors in the SSG franchise include the Virginia Retirement System ($300 million) and the New York State Common Retirement Fund ($200 million). IFC is considering a potential investment of up to $150 million for a separately managed account with a mandate aligned to the SSG series, targeting non performing loans and single-asset distress across Southeast Asia

+ ARES MANAGEMENT (NYSE:ARES) APPOINTS ASIA LEADERS AND ACQUIRES WHITESTONE REIT TO DIVERSIFY REAL ASSETS

Ares Management (NYSE:ARES) to acquire Whitestone REIT in a US$1.7 billion transaction, taking private a retail-focused REIT and expanding essential consumer-focused centers across U.S. metros. On Apr. 14, 2026, E.G. Morse was named Partner and Head of Asia Credit, with Dinesh Goel and Gabriel Fong leading the Asia Special Situations team, underscoring Ares' intent to bolster Asia-focused credit strategies. The Whitestone acquisition broadens Ares' real estate, private credit, and real assets exposure and supports diversification across geographies. Investors should monitor integration progress and Asia fundraising as the leadership changes take shape, while considering risks such as margin pressure and elevated debt; the 5.38% yield is not fully covered by earnings

+ QATAR AIRWAYS [3 News That Matters]

+ QATAR AIRWAYS CARGO EXPANDS IRAQ CORRIDOR WITH FREIGHTER AND BELLY-HOLD CAPACITY

Qatar Airways Cargo has resumed freight services to Iraq, restoring the Doha to Baghdad link with both freighter and belly-hold capacity. Effective 7th May 2026, a weekly Boeing 777 freighter will serve Baghdad, complemented by twice-weekly passenger flights on the route from 10th May to provide additional belly-hold capacity.Together, these services will deliver a combined cargo capacity of more than 115 tonnes from Doha to Baghdad from 10th May.Qatar Airways Cargo notes the broader expansion of its network: the freighter network has grown to more than 60 destinations as of 1st May, while the passenger network is scheduled to reach over 150 destinations from 16th June as operations scale up. Bookings are available through the Digital Lounge and local sales teams; inquiries can be directed to cargohelp@qatarairways.com.qa

+ QATAR AIRWAYS BRINGS BACK ERBIL SERVICE TO BOOST REGIONAL CONNECTIVITY

Qatar Airways has announced the resumption of passenger flights to Erbil on 10th May 2026, as part of its broader return to Iraq. The carrier will restart flights to Erbil, Baghdad and Basra in a phased rollout to rebuild the regional network.Freighter operations to Baghdad will resume on 7th May, complementing passenger services and boosting cargo ties. By continuing to restore routes, the airline is expanding its footprint across the Middle East as conditions allow.By mid-June 2026, the airline aims to serve more than 150 destinations, with Erbil travellers able to access the Doha hub via Hamad International Airport and reach six continents.

+ QATAR AIRWAYS BROADENS AUSTRALIA ROUTES AMID TOURISM REBOUND AND ADVISORY CAUTIONS

Qatar Airways is expanding its Doha –Australia network, reinstating daily flights to Sydney, Melbourne and Perth from May 2026, with Brisbane four weekly flights starting 16th May and rising to daily by 16th June 2026.The carrier says the expanded schedule will improve access for international travelers from Europe, the Middle East, and Asia and support Australia’s tourism recovery after the pandemic.Analysts expect the extra frequencies to lift tourism demand and tourism bodies will gain more marketing opportunities; this comes amid a broader push to position Australia as an accessible, diverse destination.Travel advisories remain a factor; Smart Traveller notes a Do Not Travel advisory for Qatar for regional security reasons, applying to transit via Hamad International Airport. Despite this, Qatar Airways expresses confidence in its safety and operational efficiency.

urn:contentItem:6JHD-03Y3-S69W-V54B-00000-00Siris Completes Acquisition of TAKKION2026-05-06T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:46:15ZLexisNexisMERGERS AND ACQUISITIONSPRODUCT / SERVICES ANNOUNCEMENTSiris Completes Acquisition of TAKKION

WEST PALM BEACH, Fla. and CENTENNIAL, Colo., May 06, 2026 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) -- Siris (together with its affiliates, “Siris”), a leading private equity firm targeting services companies that support critical technology infrastructure, today announced the completion of its previously announced acquisition of a majority stake in TAKKION, a premier integrated services provider to the renewable energy industry, from funds managed by Apollo (NYSE:APO) (the “Apollo Funds”). Through this partnership, Siris and TAKKION will look to further optimize the company’s operations, expand its proven service capabilities into adjacent renewable sectors, and pursue both organic and inorganic growth opportunities to drive long-term value creation.

“The closing of this transaction is an important milestone for TAKKION as we look to capitalize on the increasing demand for renewable energy,” said Pete Bierden, Chief Executive Officer of TAKKION. “Siris is the ideal partner for our next chapter and, with the team’s support, we look forward to expanding our capabilities into logical adjacencies and continuing to support our customers’ evolving energy needs.”

TD Securities and Wells Fargo served as financial advisors and Sidley Austin served as legal advisor to Siris on the transaction. Harris Williams acted as financial advisor and Vinson & Elkins LLP served as legal counsel to the Apollo Funds.

About Siris

Siris is a leading private equity firm focused on control investments in North American middle-market services companies that support critical technology infrastructure underpinned by secular tailwinds, such as artificial intelligence, cybersecurity and digital transformation. Based in West Palm Beach, Florida, the firm has invested approximately $9 billion since its inception as of December 31, 2025. www.siris.com 

About TAKKION

TAKKION is a leading independent service provider delivering integrated solutions for renewable energy projects across North America. The company supports the full asset lifecycle—from logistics and transportation to construction, operations, maintenance, remanufacturing, and electrical services. With a nationwide network of skilled professionals and specialized equipment, TAKKION brings the scale and technical expertise to execute complex projects safely, efficiently, and to the highest quality standards. Guided by its core values of Safety, Integrity, and Transparency, TAKKION helps customers maximize performance and long-term asset value. Learn more at www.takkion.com.

About Apollo Global Management, Inc.

Apollo is a high-growth, global alternative asset manager. In our asset management business, we seek to provide our clients excess return at every point along the risk-reward spectrum from investment grade to private equity. For more than three decades, our investing expertise across our fully integrated platform has served the financial return needs of our clients and provided businesses with innovative capital solutions for growth. Through Athene, our retirement services business, we specialize in helping clients achieve financial security by providing a suite of retirement savings products and acting as a solutions provider to institutions. Our patient, creative, and knowledgeable approach to investing aligns our clients, businesses we invest in, our employees, and the communities we impact, to expand opportunity and achieve positive outcomes. As of December 31, 2025, Apollo had approximately $938 billion of assets under management. To learn more, please visit www.apollo.com.

Media Contacts

Dana Gorman / Mallory GriffinH/Advisors Abernathydana.gorman@h-advisors.global  / mallory.griffin@h-advisors.global

Through this partnership, Siris and TAKKION will look to further optimize the company's operations, expand its proven service capabilities into adjacent renewable sectors, and pursue both organic and inorganic growth opportunities to drive long-term value creation. “The closing of this transaction is an important milestone for TAKKION as we look to capitalize on the increasing demand for renewable energy,” said Pete Bierden, Chief Executive Officer of TAKKION.“Siris is the ideal partner for our next chapter and, with the team's support, we look forward to expanding our capabilities into logical adjacencies and continuing to support our customers' evolving energy needs.”
TD Securities and Wells Fargo served as financial advisors and Sidley Austin served as legal advisor to Siris on the transaction. Harris Williams acted as financial advisor and Vinson & Elkins LLP served as legal counsel to the Apollo Funds.

Guided by its core values of Safety, Integrity, and Transparency, TAKKION helps customers maximize performance and long-term asset value. Learn more at .

urn:contentItem:6JHJ-02F3-S18X-D01Y-00000-00FORTRESS INVESTMENT GROUP LLC - INDUSTRY AND PEER INSIGHTS: UP-TO-DATE2026-05-06T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:46:15ZLexisNexisSTOCKFORTRESS INVESTMENT GROUP LLC - INDUSTRY AND PEER INSIGHTS: UP-TO-DATE

DASHBOARD: FORTRESS INVESTMENT GROUP LLC

PermID4295901538Websitehttps://www.fortress.com/IndustryAlternative InvestmentsAddress1345 Avenue OF The Americas Florida 46 New York, NY, 10105-4302 United States

ACTIVITIES:

Fortress Investment Group LLC, founded in 1998, is a global investment firm headquartered in New York City. Employing over 900 people, it manages $48 billion in assets across private equity, credit, and real estate. Acquired by SoftBank in 2017, Fortress operates independently, focusing on distressed assets and infrastructure. In 2024, it invested $2 billion in renewable energy projects. Its culture emphasizes risk management, innovation, and agility, with training in asset valuation and fintech. Competing with Apollo Global, Fortress stands out for its credit expertise and opportunistic investments. Recent initiatives include blockchain-based real estate funds and ESG-focused portfolios, aligning with investment trends. Fortress diversified strategy ensures resilience in volatile markets.

+ FORTRESS INVESTMENT GROUP LLC [3 News That Matters]

+ PURE SALMON JAPAN ADVANCES FLAGSHIP LAND-BASED FACILITY AS FORTRESS COMMITS USD 180 MILLION

Pure Salmon Japan secured over USD 180 million in preferred-note financing led by Fortress Investment Group affiliates. The deal closed on 1st April 2026 and will fund the build-out of the company’s land-based salmon facility in Japan.The investment signals robust institutional support for a project targeting 10,000 tonnes of annual production and aiming to become Japan’s largest land-based salmon operation. The facility will implement recirculating aquaculture system (RAS) technology to help reduce imports and strengthen domestic seafood supply.CEO Erol Emed commented, "We are deeply encouraged by the dedication and conviction shown by our investors in supporting Pure Salmon Japan through this financing. Their commitment underscores the growing relevance of sustainable domestic seafood production in Japan." Fortress Investment Group, with a long-standing presence in Japan, brings both capital and strategic know-how. Eric Golden, Co-Head of Asia at Fortress, highlighted the project’s role in enhancing Japan's food production infrastructure.

+ FORTRESS INVESTMENT GROUP EXPANDS PALM COAST SENIOR HOUSING WITH TUSCAN GARDENS ACQUISITION

Fortress Investment Group LLC has announced the acquisition of Tuscan Gardens of Palm Coast, a Class A seniors housing community in Palm Coast, Florida, with the deal dated 12th March 2026.Tuscan Gardens has 130 units and offers a full continuum of care, including assisted living, enhanced assisted living and memory care, on an 11-acre site about one mile from the Atlantic Ocean.Established in 2018, the Palm Coast community serves an affluent and growing senior population along Florida's northeast coast, and occupancy has improved under AgeWell Solvere Living since 2023.Fortress notes the asset's location and quality position it for long-term growth in the senior housing sector, aligning with the firm's strategy to invest in well-located properties.

+ FORTRESS CLOSES $96 MILLION LOAN TO SUPPORT JASPER'S CLASS A APARTMENT COMMUNITY IN DOWNTOWN LOS ANGELES

Fortress Investment Group closed a $96 million whole loan for Jasper, a 296-unit Class A multifamily community in Downtown Los Angeles. The financing was finalized on Feb. 9, 2026, as Fortress continues to back high-quality urban housing developments near major university campuses.Jasper is developed by Cityview and situated near the University of Southern California. The property comprises studios, and one- and two-bedroom units with upscale finishes and a broad amenity package that includes affordable housing units. Proceeds will refinance construction debt and support leasing progress for the new asset.Cityview's Damian Gancman praised Fortress for a smooth, solution-driven process and seamless execution from start to finish. Fortress's Noah Shore emphasized Jasper's fit with the firm's focus on backing well-capitalized, newly built multifamily projects with experienced sponsors, reflecting Fortress's broader investment approach.
