urn:contentItem:6JHB-59C3-S8C5-137C-00000-00FORTRESS INVESTMENT GROUP LLC - INDUSTRY AND PEER TRENDS: THE RECENT2026-05-05T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:46:15ZLexisNexisSTOCKFORTRESS INVESTMENT GROUP LLC - INDUSTRY AND PEER TRENDS: THE RECENT

DASHBOARD: FORTRESS INVESTMENT GROUP LLC

PermID4295901538Websitehttps://www.fortress.com/IndustryAlternative InvestmentsAddress1345 Avenue OF The Americas Florida 46 New York, NY, 10105-4302 United States

ACTIVITIES:

Fortress Investment Group LLC, founded in 1998, is a global investment firm headquartered in New York City. Employing over 900 people, it manages $48 billion in assets across private equity, credit, and real estate. Acquired by SoftBank in 2017, Fortress operates independently, focusing on distressed assets and infrastructure. In 2024, it invested $2 billion in renewable energy projects. Its culture emphasizes risk management, innovation, and agility, with training in asset valuation and fintech. Competing with Apollo Global, Fortress stands out for its credit expertise and opportunistic investments. Recent initiatives include blockchain-based real estate funds and ESG-focused portfolios, aligning with investment trends. Fortress diversified strategy ensures resilience in volatile markets.

+ FORTRESS INVESTMENT GROUP LLC [3 News That Matters]

+ PURE SALMON JAPAN ADVANCES FLAGSHIP LAND-BASED FACILITY AS FORTRESS COMMITS USD 180 MILLION

Pure Salmon Japan secured over USD 180 million in preferred-note financing led by Fortress Investment Group affiliates. The deal closed on 1st April 2026 and will fund the build-out of the company’s land-based salmon facility in Japan.The investment signals robust institutional support for a project targeting 10,000 tonnes of annual production and aiming to become Japan’s largest land-based salmon operation. The facility will implement recirculating aquaculture system (RAS) technology to help reduce imports and strengthen domestic seafood supply.CEO Erol Emed commented, "We are deeply encouraged by the dedication and conviction shown by our investors in supporting Pure Salmon Japan through this financing. Their commitment underscores the growing relevance of sustainable domestic seafood production in Japan." Fortress Investment Group, with a long-standing presence in Japan, brings both capital and strategic know-how. Eric Golden, Co-Head of Asia at Fortress, highlighted the project’s role in enhancing Japan's food production infrastructure.

+ FORTRESS INVESTMENT GROUP EXPANDS PALM COAST SENIOR HOUSING WITH TUSCAN GARDENS ACQUISITION

Fortress Investment Group LLC has announced the acquisition of Tuscan Gardens of Palm Coast, a Class A seniors housing community in Palm Coast, Florida, with the deal dated 12th March 2026.Tuscan Gardens has 130 units and offers a full continuum of care, including assisted living, enhanced assisted living and memory care, on an 11-acre site about one mile from the Atlantic Ocean.Established in 2018, the Palm Coast community serves an affluent and growing senior population along Florida's northeast coast, and occupancy has improved under AgeWell Solvere Living since 2023.Fortress notes the asset's location and quality position it for long-term growth in the senior housing sector, aligning with the firm's strategy to invest in well-located properties.

+ FORTRESS CLOSES $96 MILLION LOAN TO SUPPORT JASPER'S CLASS A APARTMENT COMMUNITY IN DOWNTOWN LOS ANGELES

Fortress Investment Group closed a $96 million whole loan for Jasper, a 296-unit Class A multifamily community in Downtown Los Angeles. The financing was finalized on Feb. 9, 2026, as Fortress continues to back high-quality urban housing developments near major university campuses.Jasper is developed by Cityview and situated near the University of Southern California. The property comprises studios, and one- and two-bedroom units with upscale finishes and a broad amenity package that includes affordable housing units. Proceeds will refinance construction debt and support leasing progress for the new asset.Cityview's Damian Gancman praised Fortress for a smooth, solution-driven process and seamless execution from start to finish. Fortress's Noah Shore emphasized Jasper's fit with the firm's focus on backing well-capitalized, newly built multifamily projects with experienced sponsors, reflecting Fortress's broader investment approach.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ KKR & CO [3 News That Matters]

+ KKR & CO. INC. (NYSE:KKR) TIES WITH ALTERRA TO EXPAND CLIMATE TRANSITION FUNDING

KKR & Co. Inc. (NYSE:KKR) Ties With ALTERRA to Expand Climate Transition FundingKKR & Co. Inc. (NYSE:KKR) partners with ALTERRA, the UAE’s $30 billion blended finance vehicle, to support its climate transition strategy. The announcement was made on Apr. 22, 2026. ALTERRA launched at COP28 and aims to mobilize up to $250 billion in climate finance by 2030, with a significant portion directed to developing markets. ALTERRA already counts BlackRock, Brookfield Asset Management and TPG as partners.KKR’s climate strategy, co-managed by Charlie Gailliot and Emmanuel Lagarrigue, covers renewables, energy efficiency and sustainable fuels. KKR manages over $100 billion in assets and has invested more than $44 billion in climate and environmental sustainability projects since 2011. Notable investments include Zenobe, EGC, Avantus and IGNIS P2X. The collaboration with ALTERRA could help scale technologies and improve energy security and decarbonization.Gailliot and Lagarrigue see strong potential to combine ALTERRA’s reach with KKR’s infrastructure know-how to advance decarbonization across sectors.

+ KKR & CO. INC. (NYSE:KKR) FUNDS STARITAS AS IT BECOMES STANDALONE GROWTH VEHICLE

Apr. 22, 2026 — KKR & Co. Inc. (NYSE:KKR) Invests in Staritas through Accel-KKR to support the spin-out from ECR as it pursues growth opportunities in digital technology markets. Staritas will operate as an independent company, receiving resources and guidance from Accel-KKR and KKR to accelerate development and market entry in the digital space. The move highlights investor interest in technology-driven firms able to adapt quickly and scale within competitive markets.

+ KKR & CO. INC. (NYSE:KKR) APPOINTS JOEL TRAUT TO LEAD GLOBAL REAL ESTATE CREDIT

KKR & Co. Inc. (NYSE:KKR) Appoints Joel Traut to Lead Global Real Estate CreditTraut takes on the role of global head of direct lending within KKR's real estate division, effective Apr. 20, 2026, as the firm scales its real estate credit platform. Traut has led the unit since 2015, growing it from a seed investment of $400 million to nearly $45 billion in assets under management by end-2025.Traut's leadership comes as KKR invests nearly $15 billion annually across loans and securities in the U.S. and Europe and expands its European real estate credit business with support from Ali Imraan and a dedicated team of 25 investment and asset management professionals.Paul Fine is named head of originations, succeeding Traut. Fine, a former managing director, will work to deepen client relationships and widen KKR's capital pools. Since 2017 the firm has expanded its capital pools from two to eight, including the $850 million closed for the KKR Opportunistic Real Estate Credit Fund II in February 2025.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ CARLYLE GROUP [3 News That Matters]

+ CARLYLE GROUP L.P. (NASDAQ:CG) APPOINTS TRIO AS CO-PRESIDENTS TO DRIVE UNIFIED DECISION-MAKING

Carlyle Group L.P. (NASDAQ:CG) appoints three senior executives as co-presidents under Chief Executive Officer Harvey Schwartz, effective after 22nd April 2026, to accelerate decision-making and align operations with its evolving strategy.The co-presidents will share oversight of investment priorities, fundraising, and day-to-day management across Carlyle Group L.P. (NASDAQ:CG)'s businesses, with the goal of improving execution as market conditions shift.Schwartz, who assumed leadership during a transitional period, seeks clearer accountability and faster decisions, a model that aligns with practices at other major firms to maintain momentum.The firm will assess the impact on investment activity and financial performance in forthcoming quarters.

+ CARLYLE STRENGTHENS WEALTH-MANAGEMENT FOOTPRINT WITH MAI CAPITAL ACQUISITION

Carlyle Group is set to expand its wealth-management footprint by acquiring a majority stake in MAI Capital Management from Galway Holdings, Harvest Partners and Oak Hill Capital, valuing MAI at over $2.8 billion. The deal was announced on 31st March 2026.As part of the agreement, MAI employees will retain a meaningful minority stake, keeping MAI independent under Rick Buoncore, who leads as chairman and chief executive.The investment aims to bolster MAI's capital base and growth in financial planning, investment management and family-office services. MAI reports $72.6 billion in assets under management or administration as of Jan 1 across 40 U.S. offices.Closing is expected in the second quarter of 2026, subject to standard regulatory approvals, with Carlyle leveraging its global platform to support continuity for clients and advisors.

+ MAI CAPITAL TO SELL MAJORITY OWNERSHIP TO CARLYLE FOR ABOUT $2.8 BILLION

Apr. 1, 2026 — Carlyle Group L.P. has reached an agreement to acquire a majority stake in MAI Capital Management, valuing MAI at more than $2.8 billion. The move will give Carlyle majority ownership as Galway Holdings, Harvest Partners and Oak Hill Capital exit, while MAI employees retain a significant minority stake. Carlyle had previously invested in MAI in 2021 via Galway Holdings. As of Jan. 1, 2026, MAI oversees $72.6 billion in assets, operates 40 U.S. offices and employs over 700 people. The deal underscores Carlyle's continued expansion even as its stock has been volatile; the firm reports strong liquidity and a 15-year dividend history at about 3%. Analysts rate Carlyle’s overall financial health as FAIR. Rick Buoncore, MAI’s chairman and CEO, said, "As we looked for a long-term partner to help us achieve that vision, Carlyle stood out for its alignment with our culture and values along with their deep industry knowledge and expertise." The transaction is expected to close in the second quarter of 2026, subject to customary closing conditions and regulatory approvals, including consent from the Division of Banking in South Dakota. Harvest Partners, MAI’s former sponsor, added, "We are confident that MAI is well positioned for the next stage of growth with Carlyle’s backing."

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ OAKTREE CAPITAL MANAGEMENT LP [3 News That Matters]

+ BIOXCEL THERAPEUTICS ANNOUNCES NINTH AMENDMENT TO CREDIT AGREEMENT AND NEW WARRANT ISSUANCE

BioXcel Therapeutics, Inc. said it has expanded its financing framework through a Fourth Amended and Restated Registration Rights Agreement with Oaktree Capital Management and other institutional holders. The new agreement, disclosed in a Form 8-K, was announced with the filing date of 18th April 2026 and encompasses changes to prior terms as well as the issuance of additional equity warrants to lenders, reflecting the company’s ongoing capital management strategy. Separately, the Ninth Amendment to BioXcel's Credit Agreement was executed on 27th March 2026, and on 15th April 2026 the company issued unregistered warrants in a private placement. The warrants give lenders the right to acquire more BioXcel common stock, and the private placement means the warrants were not registered under the Securities Act of 1933. BioXcel also stated it will file registration statements with the SEC to enable potential resale of the underlying shares, a move intended to provide liquidity and investor transparency. Lenders have said they will hold the securities for investment purposes, which could reduce immediate selling pressure but may still lead to a higher total share count if the warrants are exercised and the shares registered for resale. The company notes it is not an emerging growth company and will continue to comply with full regulatory reporting. With participation from multiple Oaktree entities, the deal illustrates a continued commitment to securities compliance and to monitoring upcoming filings and announcements that could influence BioXcel’s stock performance.

+ OAKTREE, NORTH MILL EXECUTE $355.8 MILLION ASSET-BACKED SECURITIZATION

Oaktree Capital Management and North Mill Equipment Finance have closed a $355.8 million asset-backed securitization, OAKEF 2026-1, on 8th April 2026, reflecting healthy investor demand for quality equipment finance assets.The securitized assets originated from Midland Equipment Finance in a November 2025 deal. The notes were rated AAA through A and drew strong demand, about 6.3 times subscriptions, while delivering a weighted average spread of 99 basis points and a 4.7% yield.Truist Securities led structuring with Deutsche Bank as co-manager. Brendan Beer of Oaktree noted the market reception as evidence of investor confidence in the strategy and in North Mill's role.North Mill's David Lee emphasized asset quality and the servicing platform, reiterating that NMEF will continue as the primary servicer for the assets, ensuring ongoing management.

+ OAKTREE NAVIGATES PRIVATE CREDIT PRESSURES BY FULFILLING $7.7B REDEMPTIONS AND CUTTING DIVIDEND

On 29th March 2026, Oaktree Capital Management, a Brookfield Asset Management subsidiary, said it would fulfill all redemption requests from its private credit fund, totaling about $7.7 billion, allowing withdrawals up to 8.5% of net assets.Brookfield will contribute roughly $80 million to support the redemptions.The Oaktree Strategic Credit Fund will repurchase 6.8% of its shares, with Brookfield buying an additional 1.7% from a single investor; the fund's dividend will be cut by two cents to 16 cents.As of 23rd March 2026, liquidity stood at $1.8 billion from cash and undrawn facilities, while the firm has sold publicly traded loans and bonds to reduce exposure to software investments. The broader private credit sector remains under pressure, with peers such as Blackstone raising buyback terms to 7%.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ APOLLO GLOBAL MANAGEMENT [3 News That Matters]

+ APOLLO GLOBAL MANAGEMENT LLC (NYSE:APO) REPORTS $1.25 BILLION CONVERTIBLE PREFERRED EQUITY INVESTMENT FOR 13% MCKESSON MEDICAL-SURGICAL SOLUTIONS STAKE

On Apr. 20, 2026, Apollo Global Management LLC (NYSE:APO) Invests $1.25 Billion in McKesson Corporation (NYSE:MCK) for a 13% stake in its Medical-Surgical Solutions division via convertible preferred equity, valuing the unit at approximately $13 billion. The capital supports McKesson's planned separation of the Medical-Surgical Solutions unit and funds strategic initiatives within the business. The transaction reflects a strategic alignment between Apollo and McKesson as the latter moves toward separating its Medical-Surgical Solutions segment and concentrating on core operations. Apollo Global Management oversees about $938.4 billion in assets under management and has a market capitalization near $72.06 billion, with a trailing P/E ratio of 22.58, indicating market expectations for continued growth. Insider activity raises caution, with six insider sells versus one insider buy over the past year, and Apollo's financial strength is rated 6 out of 10. Market participants will watch the arrangement for potential impacts on McKesson's separation plan.

+ MEDICAL SURGICAL SOLUTIONS, LLC AT $9.62 BILLION POST-MONEY VALUATION WITH $1.25 BILLION FROM APOLLO GLOBAL MANAGEMENT

Medical Surgical Solutions, LLC secures $1.25 billion from Apollo Funds, managed by Apollo Global Management, Inc., delivering a 13% stake via convertible preferred shares. The investment was formalized on April 20, 2026 and is contingent on regulatory approvals and customary closing conditions. The post-money valuation stands at about $9.62 billion, and the capital will be used to bolster operations and expand the company’s market footprint as Apollo provides strategic backing through its funds.

+ APOLLO BROADENS UK HOUSING FOOTPRINT VIA GATEHOUSE LIVING GROUP PURCHASE

Apollo Global Management Inc. has acquired Gatehouse Living Group from Gatehouse Bank, completed on 1st April 2026, bringing Gatehouse Investment Management and Ascend Properties into its UK housing platform.Financial terms were not disclosed.GLG is a Build-to-Rent single-family housing leader with more than 10,000 properties and 5,000 homes acquired, and will continue to be led by CEO Paul Stockwell with Ascend Properties handling external portfolio management.The deal strengthens Apollo's UK strategy and follows its existing interests in Miller Homes and Foundation Home Loans, while Gatehouse Bank focuses on Shariah-compliant financing.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ ARES MANAGEMENT [3 News That Matters]

+ ARES MANAGEMENT (NYSE:ARES) APPOINTS ASIA LEADERS AND ACQUIRES WHITESTONE REIT TO DIVERSIFY REAL ASSETS

Ares Management (NYSE:ARES) to acquire Whitestone REIT in a US$1.7 billion transaction, taking private a retail-focused REIT and expanding essential consumer-focused centers across U.S. metros. On Apr. 14, 2026, E.G. Morse was named Partner and Head of Asia Credit, with Dinesh Goel and Gabriel Fong leading the Asia Special Situations team, underscoring Ares' intent to bolster Asia-focused credit strategies. The Whitestone acquisition broadens Ares' real estate, private credit, and real assets exposure and supports diversification across geographies. Investors should monitor integration progress and Asia fundraising as the leadership changes take shape, while considering risks such as margin pressure and elevated debt; the 5.38% yield is not fully covered by earnings

+ WHITESTONE REIT TO BECOME PRIVATE AS ARES COMPLETES $1.7B ACQUISITION

On Apr. 10, 2026, Ares Management announced plans to acquire Whitestone REIT in an all-cash deal valued at about $1.7 billion, at $19.00 per share or unit. The transaction will render Whitestone private and remove it from the NYSE. Whitestone’s 56 properties total around 4.9 million square feet across Sun Belt markets such as Phoenix, Austin, Dallas–Fort Worth, Houston and San Antonio. The move aims to broaden Ares Real Estate’s footprint with necessity-based retail centers in high-demand Arizona and Texas markets. Whitestone CEO Dave Holeman stated the company’s strategy centers on high-return, smaller spaces with a diversified tenant base, adding that the agreement with Ares validates this approach for shareholders. Ares’ global head of real estate strategy and growth, David Roth, emphasized the appeal of New Economy real estate and convenient consumer experiences, highlighting the growing demand for grocery, pharmacy, healthcare, fitness and dining options.

+ ARES MANAGEMENT ANNOUNCES DEFINITIVE MERGER TO BUY WHITESTONE REIT FOR $1.7 BILLION

Ares Management has agreed to acquire Whitestone REIT for $1.7 billion, valuing Whitestone at $19 per share and taking the company private. The deal is targeted to close in the third quarter of 2026.On April 9, 2026, Ares described Whitestone’s portfolio as a fit for its growth strategy, focused on necessary retail centers in Arizona and Texas. Whitestone owns 56 properties totaling about 3.9 million square feet, with locations in Phoenix, Austin, Dallas-Fort Worth, Houston and San Antonio.Whitestone’s board unanimously approved the deal, which remains subject to closing conditions including shareholder approval. Whitestone’s advisers are BofA Securities and JLL Securities; Ares is advised by Citi and Morgan Stanley. Whitestone stock traded at about $16.94 on April 8, 2026, up from $12.57 a year earlier. Ares has about $623 billion in assets under management as of December 2025, and its shares traded near $104.83 on April 8, 2026, down from 137.75 the prior year.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ QATAR AIRWAYS [3 News That Matters]

+ QATAR AIRWAYS CARGO RESTARTS FREIGHTER AND PASSENGER LINKS TO UAE ON EXPANDED NETWORK

Qatar Airways Cargo has resumed both freighter and belly operations to and from Doha after safety concerns. The new schedule confirms freighter flights to Dubai World Central (DWC) twice weekly from 26th April and to Sharjah (SHJ) three times weekly from 1st May, while daily passenger services to Dubai (DXB) and Sharjah (SHJ) also restart, with freighter reach planned to exceed 50 destinations by 1st May and the overall passenger network expected to top 150 by 16th June.Urgent freight restrictions have been lifted on routes such as Victoria, Australia, with the carrier introducing special handling codes QRLIV for fish, mammals and diplomatic cargo, and LHO for live human organs and blood, effective 1st May.Reservations remain accessible via the Digital Lounge and Qatar Airways Cargo sales teams, alongside standard sales channels. The resumption is described as a key milestone for regional logistics and supports ongoing expansion.Industry stakeholders are watching the pace of growth, as the UAE freight market benefits from improved connectivity and service levels.

+ QATAR AIRWAYS INTRODUCES NEXT-GENERATION QSUITE, AIMING TO SUSTAIN PREMIUM EDGE

Qatar Airways announced the Qsuite Next Generation, with a launch planned later this year on the Airbus A350-1000 after delays with the 777-9, as of 23rd April 2026. The Next Generation builds on the iconic Qsuite introduced in 2017, maintaining sliding doors, double beds, and modular seating while adding a movable 4K OLED screen and stronger privacy options. The initiative reinforces Qatar Airways' premium strategy and its Doha hub, where exclusive check-in, fast-track security, and premium lounges contribute to a seamless travel experience. By investing in product development, the airline seeks to attract high-yield corporate travelers and affluent leisure customers as competition in luxury cabins intensifies.

+ QATAR AIRWAYS SETS OUT PHASED EMBARGO LIFTS DURING RECOVERY

Qatar Airways is steadily expanding its operations as it rebuilds its network, with passenger routes restored to more than 150 destinations and freighter coverage over 50 locations as of April 20, 2026.The restoration is part of a wider plan to increase capacity amid rising air freight demand. Some shipments remain embargoed, with AVF, AVM, and DOC handling codes restricted, and live organs and blood not accepted.QRLIV shipments can be booked under different codes, as long as Doha transit time stays within 24 hours. Other products remain bookable via standard sales channels, and the carrier emphasizes adaptability and a commitment to service as it expands.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

urn:contentItem:6JGB-S5D3-RTDH-2455-00000-00FORTRESS INVESTMENT GROUP LLC - UPDATED NEWS FROM KKR & CO. INC. AND PEERS2026-05-01T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:46:15ZLexisNexisSTOCKFORTRESS INVESTMENT GROUP LLC - UPDATED NEWS FROM KKR & CO. INC. AND PEERS

DASHBOARD: FORTRESS INVESTMENT GROUP LLC

PermID4295901538Websitehttps://www.fortress.com/IndustryAlternative InvestmentsAddress1345 Avenue OF The Americas Florida 46 New York, NY, 10105-4302 United States

ACTIVITIES:

Fortress Investment Group LLC, founded in 1998, is a global investment firm headquartered in New York City. Employing over 900 people, it manages $48 billion in assets across private equity, credit, and real estate. Acquired by SoftBank in 2017, Fortress operates independently, focusing on distressed assets and infrastructure. In 2024, it invested $2 billion in renewable energy projects. Its culture emphasizes risk management, innovation, and agility, with training in asset valuation and fintech. Competing with Apollo Global, Fortress stands out for its credit expertise and opportunistic investments. Recent initiatives include blockchain-based real estate funds and ESG-focused portfolios, aligning with investment trends. Fortress diversified strategy ensures resilience in volatile markets.

+ FORTRESS INVESTMENT GROUP LLC [3 News That Matters]

+ FORTRESS-LED FINANCING INJECTS OVER USD 180 MILLION INTO PURE SALMON JAPAN’S LAND-BASED SALMON PROJECT

Pure Salmon Japan has secured a preferred note investment exceeding USD 180 million, led by affiliates of Fortress Investment Group. The financing, completed on 1st April 2026, is earmarked to support the construction of Pure Salmon Japan’s land-based farming facility and to help meet rising domestic demand for premium seafood.The deal underscores institutional backing for the project, which envisions a facility capable of producing up to 10,000 tonnes and potentially becoming Japan’s largest land-based salmon operation. It relies on advanced recirculating aquaculture system (RAS) technology to bolster food security by reducing import dependency.CEO Erol Emed said, "We are deeply encouraged by the dedication and conviction shown by our investors in supporting Pure Salmon Japan through this financing. Their commitment underscores the growing relevance of sustainable domestic seafood production in Japan." Fortress Investment Group, which has a longstanding presence in Japan, brings capital and expertise to the project. Eric Golden, Co-Head of Asia at Fortress, described the initiative as a meaningful step for strengthening Japan's food-production infrastructure.

+ FORTRESS INVESTMENT GROUP EXPANDS SENIOR HOUSING PORTFOLIO WITH TUSCAN GARDENS PALM COAST ACQUISITION

Fortress Investment Group LLC has announced the acquisition of Tuscan Gardens of Palm Coast, a Class A seniors housing community located in Palm Coast, Florida. The deal, completed on 12th March 2026, involves funds managed by Fortress affiliates, with AgeWell Solvere Living continuing to operate the property after managing it since 2023.Tuscan Gardens comprises 130 units and provides a full continuum of care, including assisted living, enhanced assisted living, and memory care. Established in 2018 on an 11-acre campus about one mile from the Atlantic Ocean, the property serves a growing, affluent senior population along Florida's northeast coast. Under AgeWell's leadership, occupancy and performance have improved, positioning the community as a leading option in the Palm Coast market.

+ FORTRESS INVESTMENT GROUP ARRANGES $96 MILLION FINANCING FOR JASPER DOWNTOWN LOS ANGELES MULTIFAMILY PROJECT

Fortress Investment Group has closed a $96 million whole loan for Jasper, a newly developed 296-unit Class A multifamily community in Downtown Los Angeles. The financing, facilitated by Fortress affiliates, was completed on Feb. 9, 2026, highlighting Fortress's ongoing commitment to supporting high-quality real estate developments.Jasper is owned and developed by Cityview and sits near the University of Southern California campus. The property comprises studios, one- and two-bedroom residences featuring upscale finishes and a comprehensive amenity package, including affordable housing units. Proceeds from the loan will refinance existing construction debt and back the ongoing leasing efforts for the new community.Cityview's Damian Gancman described Fortress's involvement as efficient and solution-oriented, noting a seamless execution from start to finish. Noah Shore, Global Co-Head of Real Estate Debt at Fortress, said the project aligns with the firm's focus on backing high-quality, newly built multifamily housing with significant sponsor equity. Fortress, founded in 1998, manages about $54 billion in assets as of Sept. 30, 2025, and continues to invest across credit, real estate, private equity and permanent capital strategies.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ KKR & CO [3 News That Matters]

+ KKR & CO. INC. (NYSE:KKR) PARTNERS WITH ALTERRA TO ACCELERATE GLOBAL CLIMATE INVESTMENTS

KKR & Co. Inc. (NYSE:KKR) Partners With ALTERRA to Accelerate Global Climate InvestmentsKKR & Co. Inc. (NYSE:KKR) secured a commitment from the UAE's ALTERRA, a $30 billion blended finance vehicle, to bolster its climate transition strategy. The announcement was made Apr. 22, 2026, as KKR seeks to mobilize private capital for sustainable energy projects. The terms of the commitment were not disclosed.ALTERRA, launched at COP28 with UAE backing, aims to de-risk climate investments and has already formed partnerships with BlackRock, Brookfield Asset Management and TPG. With KKR’s inclusion, ALTERRA now counts a broad set of investment expertise and targets up to $250 billion in mobilized climate finance by 2030, a substantial share of which is expected to flow to developing markets.KKR’s Global Climate Transition Strategy, co-managed by Charlie Gailliot and Emmanuel Lagarrigue, spans renewables, energy efficiency and sustainable fuels. The firm manages more than $100 billion in assets and has invested over $44 billion in climate and environmental sustainability projects since 2011. Notable bets include Zenobe in the United Kingdom, EGC in Germany, Avantus in the United States and IGNIS P2X for industrial decarbonization. Gailliot and Lagarrigue highlighted that ALTERRA’s global reach combined with KKR’s infrastructure expertise could scale proven technologies and build more resilient energy systems.

+ KKR & CO. INC. (NYSE:KKR) INVESTS IN STARITAS THROUGH ACCEL-KKR SPIN-OUT

Apr. 22, 2026 — KKR & Co. Inc. (NYSE:KKR) Invests in Staritas through its Accel-KKR unit to back the spin-out from ECR as it seeks growth in digital technology markets. The announcement signals KKR's continued focus on technology investments through Accel-KKR and aligns with Staritas' standalone growth plan. Staritas will operate independently from ECR to accelerate product development and market penetration, aided by the resources and expertise of Accel-KKR and KKR & Co. Inc. (NYSE:KKR). The collaboration aims to position Staritas to respond quickly to evolving customer demands in the digital space. Investors will watch how the Staritas partnership unfolds in a competitive tech landscape, as the management team plans to expand offerings and strengthen service capabilities with this new capital and guidance.

+ KKR & CO. INC. (NYSE:KKR) APPOINTS JOEL TRAUT GLOBAL HEAD OF DIRECT LENDING

KKR & Co. Inc. (NYSE:KKR) Appoints Joel Traut Global Head of Direct LendingTraut takes on the role of global head of direct lending, effective Apr. 20, 2026. He has led KKR Real Estate's credit business, growing the platform from a seed investment of $400 million in 2015 to nearly $45 billion in assets under management by the end of 2025, and will oversee growth across a broader geographic footprint.Paul Fine, a 2015 hire, is named head of originations to succeed Traut. Fine, previously a managing director, will focus on strengthening client relationships and expanding the firm's capital base. The real estate credit team has grown from 10 employees in 2015 to 130 today, with Ali Imraan leading a dedicated team of 25 investment and asset management professionals.The leadership transition signals a continued focus on growth and innovation in global real estate credit as KKR looks to capture opportunities across the U.S. and Europe.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ CARLYLE GROUP [3 News That Matters]

+ CARLYLE GROUP L.P. (NASDAQ:CG) ELEVATES THREE EXECUTIVES TO CO-PRESIDENTS TO STRENGTHEN LEADERSHIP

Carlyle Group L.P. (NASDAQ:CG) elevates three senior executives to co-presidents under Chief Executive Officer Harvey Schwartz, effective from 22nd April 2026. The move aims to bolster decision-making and streamline operations as Carlyle refines its strategy in a challenging economic environment.The three co-presidents will share oversight of investment priorities, fundraising, and operational management across Carlyle Group L.P. (NASDAQ:CG)'s franchises, positioning the firm to respond more quickly to market dynamics. By concentrating authority around Schwartz, Carlyle Group seeks greater execution discipline and improved performance in the coming quarters.

+ CARLYLE TO ACQUIRE MAJORITY STAKE IN MAI CAPITAL FOR $2.8 BILLION, EXPANDING WEALTH-MANAGEMENT PLATFORM

Carlyle Group Inc. confirmed on 31st March 2026 that it will acquire a majority stake in MAI Capital Management from Galway Holdings, Harvest Partners and Oak Hill Capital, valuing MAI at more than $2.8 billion. The deal will transfer majority ownership to Carlyle while MAI employees retain a meaningful minority stake and the firm will continue to operate independently under Rick Buoncore, the chairman and chief executive.The transaction is designed to strengthen MAI's capital base and support growth in its financial planning, investment management and family-office capabilities. As of Jan 1, MAI and its affiliates oversee $72.6 billion in assets across 40 offices in the United States, a scale Carlyle will leverage with its global resources to maintain continuity for clients and advisors.The deal is expected to close in the second quarter of 2026, subject to customary regulatory approvals. Carlyle has a history of originating, structuring and leading equity investments in wealth-management platforms. While some investors may see AI stocks offering greater upside, a report on undervalued AI stocks is available.

+ CARLYLE TO ACQUIRE MAJORITY STAKE IN MAI CAPITAL IN $2.8 BILLION DEAL

Apr. 1, 2026 — Carlyle Group L.P. has signed an agreement to acquire a majority stake in MAI Capital Management, valuing the registered investment adviser at more than $2.8 billion. Under the terms, Carlyle will assume majority ownership, while existing stakeholders including Galway Holdings, Harvest Partners and Oak Hill Capital will exit; MAI employees will retain a meaningful minority stake. Carlyle previously invested in MAI in 2021 through Galway Holdings, which acquired the firm that year. As of Jan. 1, 2026, MAI manages $72.6 billion in assets, operates 40 offices across the United States and has a workforce of more than 700. Despite volatility in Carlyle's stock, the parent firm maintains solid fundamentals, with liquid assets exceeding its near-term obligations and a 15-year streak of dividend payments, currently yielding around 3%. Financial analyses rate Carlyle's overall health as FAIR. Rick Buoncore, Chairman and CEO of MAI, said, "As we looked for a long-term partner to help us achieve that vision, Carlyle stood out for its alignment with our culture and values along with their deep industry knowledge and expertise." The deal is expected to close in the second quarter of 2026, subject to customary closing conditions and regulatory approvals, including consent from the Division of Banking in South Dakota. Harvest Partners, which facilitated MAI's growth through more than 30 strategic acquisitions during its ownership, said, "We are confident that MAI is well positioned for the next stage of growth with Carlyle’s backing."

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ OAKTREE CAPITAL MANAGEMENT LP [3 News That Matters]

+ BIOXCEL THERAPEUTICS EXPANDS FINANCING FRAMEWORK THROUGH OAKTREE AGREEMENT

BioXcel Therapeutics, Inc. has entered into a Fourth Amended and Restated Registration Rights Agreement with Oaktree Capital Management and other institutional holders. This development, announced in a Form 8-K filing, marks a milestone in the company's approach to managing its capital structure. The agreement was disclosed on 18th April 2026 and includes both amendments to existing terms and the issuance of new equity warrants to lenders. The Ninth Amendment to BioXcel Therapeutics' existing Credit Agreement was executed on 27th March 2026, and it introduced provisions that led to the issuance of equity warrants. On 15th April 2026, the company issued these unregistered warrants, granting lenders the right to acquire additional shares of common stock. The issuance was completed as a private placement and was not registered under the Securities Act of 1933, underscoring the company's financing approach. In conjunction with this agreement, BioXcel committed to filing registration statements with the SEC to facilitate potential resale of the shares underlying the warrants. This step aims to support liquidity and transparency for investors, with lenders indicating they will hold the securities for investment purposes only. The arrangements could lead to dilution if warrants are exercised and may increase the share count once registered for resale. BioXcel notes it is not an emerging growth company and remains subject to full reporting requirements; the registration rights agreement, involving several Oaktree entities among other holders, signals continued adherence to securities regulations. Investors are advised to monitor forthcoming filings and announcements, as these developments could influence BioXcel's financial position and market performance.

+ OAKTREE AND NORTH MILL CLOSE $355.8 MILLION ASSET-BACKED SECURITIZATION

Oaktree Capital Management has closed a $355.8 million asset-backed securitization with North Mill Equipment Finance, known as OAKEF 2026-1, on 8th April 2026. The deal underscores investor appetite for high-quality equipment finance assets.The securitization pools assets originally acquired from Midland Equipment Finance in a deal finalized in November 2025. The offering was rated from AAA to single A and drew peak subscriptions of about 6.3 times, enabling efficient execution across the capital stack with a weighted average spread of 99 basis points and a weighted average yield of 4.7%. Truist Securities acted as lead structuring agent and Deutsche Bank served as co-manager.

+ OAKTREE TO HONOR ALL REDEMPTIONS ON ABOUT $7.7 BILLION PRIVATE CREDIT FUND

On 29th March 2026, Oaktree Capital Management, a Brookfield Asset Management subsidiary, said it would honor all redemption requests from its private credit fund, totaling about $7.7 billion. Investors will be allowed to redeem up to 8.5% of net assets, or roughly $400 million, with Brookfield contributing about $80 million to support the redemptions.The Oaktree Strategic Credit Fund, which targets retail investors, is set to repurchase 6.8% of its own shares, with Brookfield agreeing to buy an additional 1.7% from a single investor, according to a letter to fund holders. The firm is also reducing its quarterly dividend by two cents, bringing the total to 16 cents.As of 23rd March 2026, the fund reported $1.8 billion in liquidity from cash and undrawn facilities. Oaktree has also been selling portions of publicly traded loans and bonds this year to mitigate exposure to software investments, reflecting a broader portfolio-management push during a volatile period. The deal comes amid rising scrutiny of private credit markets, where peers such as Blackstone have faced higher-than-usual repurchase requests and have had to adjust buyback terms. Blackstone's $82 billion private credit fund faced repurchase requests exceeding the typical 5% threshold, prompting the board to raise the buyback to 7%.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ APOLLO GLOBAL MANAGEMENT [3 News That Matters]

+ MEDICAL SURGICAL SOLUTIONS, LLC SECURES $1.25 BILLION INVESTMENT FROM APOLLO GLOBAL MANAGEMENT (NYSE:APO)

Medical Surgical Solutions, LLC has secured a $1.25 billion investment from Apollo Funds, managed by Apollo Global Management, Inc. The deal grants Apollo a 13% stake and was formalized on April 20, 2026 through the issuance of convertible preferred shares, subject to regulatory approvals and customary closing conditions. The funding values Medical Surgical Solutions, LLC at a post-money valuation of approximately $9.62 billion and aims to strengthen operations and expand market presence. Apollo Global Management, Inc., via Apollo Funds, brings strategic backing to the company as demand for surgical solutions grows, with analysts watching how the partnership could influence growth and shareholder value as the transaction proceeds through regulatory review

+ APOLLO GLOBAL MANAGEMENT LLC (NYSE:APO) INVESTS $1.25 BILLION IN MCKESSON CORPORATION (NYSE:MCK) FOR 13% STAKE IN MEDICAL-SURGICAL SOLUTIONS DIVISION

On Apr. 20, 2026, Apollo Global Management LLC (NYSE:APO) Invests $1.25 Billion in McKesson Corporation (NYSE:MCK) for a 13% stake in its Medical-Surgical Solutions division via convertible preferred equity, valuing the unit at approximately $13 billion. The investment supports McKesson's plan to separate the Medical-Surgical Solutions division and is expected to bolster operational efficiencies across the group. McKesson intends to separate the Medical-Surgical Solutions division, and the capital from Apollo is expected to bolster the strategic plan and fund initiatives across the business. The investment signals a strategic alignment between Apollo and McKesson as the company pursues the separation and optimization of its core operations. Apollo Global Management LLC (NYSE:APO) oversees about $938.4 billion in assets under management and has a market capitalization near $72.06 billion, with a trailing P/E ratio of 22.58, indicating investors anticipate growth. Insider activity raises caution, with six insider sells versus one insider buy over the past year, and Apollo's financial strength is rated 6 out of 10. Market participants will monitor the deal as it unfolds and its impact on McKesson's separation plans.

+ APOLLO GLOBAL MANAGEMENT EXPANDS UK PRESENCE WITH GATEHOUSE LIVING GROUP ACQUISITION

Apollo Global Management Inc. has expanded its UK footprint with the acquisition of Gatehouse Living Group from Gatehouse Bank, a deal completed on 1st April 2026 that brings Gatehouse Investment Management and Ascend Properties under Apollo.Financial terms were not disclosed. The transaction aligns with Apollo's strategy to invest in high-potential sectors and complements its existing UK housing interests, including Miller Homes and Foundation Home Loans. GLG is a national leader in Build-to-Rent single-family housing, managing a portfolio of more than 10,000 properties and having acquired over 5,000 homes. Under Apollo ownership, GLG will be led by CEO Paul Stockwell and the current management team, with Ascend Properties continuing to provide management services for external institutional portfolios, enabling GLG to leverage both Apollo capital and third-party partners to accelerate development of high-quality rental housing. Gatehouse Bank's divestiture will allow it to focus on core retail growth and Shariah-compliant home finance products.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ ARES MANAGEMENT [3 News That Matters]

+ ARES MANAGEMENT (NYSE:ARES) TO ACQUIRE WHITESTONE REIT IN US$1.7 BILLION TRANSACTION

Ares Management (NYSE:ARES) to acquire Whitestone REIT in a transaction valued at approximately US$1.7 billion, taking the retail-focused REIT private and expanding the firm’s footprint in necessity-based retail centers across rapidly growing metropolitan areas in the United States. The deal supports Ares' strategy to diversify across geographies and asset types and align with its emphasis on private credit, real assets, and special situations. On Apr. 14, 2026, Ares Management named E.G. Morse Partner and Head of Asia Credit, while Dinesh Goel and Gabriel Fong will lead the Asia Special Situations team, underscoring the firm’s focus on strengthening credit and special situations capabilities in Asia. The Whitestone purchase broadens Ares’ exposure to essential retail real estate and supports its strategy to diversify capital allocation across geographies and asset types. Investors should heed risks including profit margin compression, high debt levels, and a dividend yield of 5.38% that is not fully supported by earnings

+ ARES MANAGEMENT TO ACQUIRE WHITESTONE REIT IN $1.7 BILLION ALL-CASH DEAL

The announcement, made on Apr. 10, 2026, confirms that certain Ares Real Estate funds will acquire all common shares and operating partnership units of Whitestone REIT in an all-cash transaction valued at approximately $1.7 billion, at $19.00 per share or unit. Whitestone’s portfolio comprises 56 convenience-focused retail properties totaling about 4.9 million square feet in Sun Belt markets such as Phoenix, Austin, Dallas–Fort Worth, Houston and San Antonio. After the deal closes, Whitestone will be private and will cease trading on the NYSE. Whitestone CEO Dave Holeman said, "We believe Whitestone has shown the value of high-return smaller spaces occupied by a well-diversified mix of tenants. Our investment strategy is designed to allow businesses to fuel connection and convenience within thriving, dynamic communities. We believe this transaction with Ares is a testament to the value that strategy has created for our business and, ultimately, for our shareholders." David Roth, global head of real estate strategy and growth at Ares Real Estate, added, "This transaction reflects our high conviction in New Economy real estate as today’s consumers are increasingly seeking convenient experiences for their grocery, pharmacy, healthcare, fitness, and dining needs." Ares Management said it expects to support and expand Whitestone's portfolio to create value for communities and investors.

+ ARES MANAGEMENT RAISES $5.4 BILLION FOR VALUE-ADD REAL ESTATE RECOVERY

Ares Management Corporation (NYSE:ARES) has raised approximately $5.4 billion for value-add real estate investments in the United States and Europe, as of 9th April 2026. The fundraising underscores the firm's focus on opportunities in supply-constrained markets, with emphasis on logistics, multifamily, and self-storage assets that could rebound in the near term.The $5.4 billion package comprises about $1.9 billion for the EPEP IV fund and roughly $3.5 billion aimed at Ares' U.S. value-add strategy. Both lines have already deployed or identified around $1.1 billion in initial investments, signaling a proactive stance toward potential growth sectors.Analysts are broadly constructive, with about 75% of coverage bullish and a consensus price target near $161, implying a potential upside of roughly 60%. In a related note, on 24th March 2026, BMO Capital trimmed its price target for Ares from $140 to $112 and maintained a Market Perform rating amid concerns about BDC redemptions, asset-based credit issues, and market volatility. Ares, founded as a global alternative investment manager, also counts BlackRock among its significant backers, with a stake of about 7.12% as of the end of 2025. While Ares shows promise, some investors may find greater upside in certain AI stocks and other sectors in a rapidly shifting market.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ QATAR AIRWAYS [3 News That Matters]

+ QATAR AIRWAYS CARGO RESUMES UAE FREIGHT OPERATIONS FOLLOWING SAFETY REVIEW

Qatar Airways Cargo has resumed its freighter and belly operations to and from Doha after a temporary halt due to safety concerns. As of 28th April 2026, the carrier published its new schedule, which includes the resumption of freighter flights to Dubai World Central (DWC) twice weekly, starting from 26th April. In addition, freighter services to Sharjah (SHJ) will operate three times weekly beginning on 1st May, while daily passenger flights to Dubai (DXB) and Sharjah (SHJ) also restart, with plans to expand freighter destinations to more than 50 by 1st May. The passenger network is expected to grow to over 150 destinations from 16th June.Shipment restrictions have also been lifted on certain urgent freight services to routes such as Victoria, Australia, with Qatar Airways Cargo offering products under new handling codes starting 1st May. The QRLIV codes cover commodities such as fish, mammals and diplomatic cargo, while LHO codes cover the transport of live human organs and blood.Reservations remain accessible through the Digital Lounge and local Qatar Airways Cargo sales teams, in addition to the standard sales channels. The airline says it remains committed to maintaining robust service levels as it ramps back up its operations.

+ QATAR AIRWAYS REVEALS NEXT-GENERATION QSUITE TO ELEVATE BUSINESS CLASS

Qatar Airways has announced the upcoming Qsuite Next Generation, slated to launch later this year after delays in its Boeing 777-9 arrivals. The upgraded business-class product will first appear on the Airbus A350-1000, as of 23rd April 2026. Building on the long-running Qsuite introduced nearly a decade ago, the Next Generation preserves sliding doors, double beds, and configurable seating while integrating advanced technology and improved space management to appeal to business travelers seeking privacy and flexibility. The move underscores Qatar Airways' strategy to lead premium travel, leveraging Doha Hamad International Airport's reputation and its premium ecosystem, including dedicated check-in, fast-track security, and lounge access.

+ QATAR AIRWAYS EXPANDS NETWORK AS RESTORATIONS CONTINUE

Qatar Airways has been expanding its operations, with belly and freighter services increasing to and from Doha. As of April 20, 2026, the carrier has restored its passenger network to more than 150 destinations and expanded freighter coverage to over 50 locations worldwide.The network restoration is part of a broader plan to rebuild capacity in the air cargo market. While the network grows, some shipments remain embargoed; AVF, AVM, and DOC handling codes are restricted, and live organs and blood are not accepted. Customers can still book QRLIV shipments under alternative handling codes, provided the Doha transit time does not exceed 24 hours. All other products remain bookable through standard channels, including the Digital Lounge and local sales teams, underscoring the airline's adaptability to rising demand and shifting logistics conditions.

DASHBOARD: FORTRESS INVESTMENT GROUP LLC

PermID4295901538Websitehttps://www.fortress.com/IndustryAlternative InvestmentsAddress1345 Avenue OF The Americas Florida 46 New York, NY, 10105-4302 United States

ACTIVITIES:

Fortress Investment Group LLC, founded in 1998, is a global investment firm headquartered in New York City. Employing over 900 people, it manages $48 billion in assets across private equity, credit, and real estate. Acquired by SoftBank in 2017, Fortress operates independently, focusing on distressed assets and infrastructure. In 2024, it invested $2 billion in renewable energy projects. Its culture emphasizes risk management, innovation, and agility, with training in asset valuation and fintech. Competing with Apollo Global, Fortress stands out for its credit expertise and opportunistic investments. Recent initiatives include blockchain-based real estate funds and ESG-focused portfolios, aligning with investment trends. Fortress diversified strategy ensures resilience in volatile markets.

+ FORTRESS INVESTMENT GROUP LLC [3 News That Matters]

+ PURE SALMON JAPAN ADVANCES FLAGSHIP LAND-BASED FACILITY AS FORTRESS COMMITS USD 180 MILLION

Pure Salmon Japan secured over USD 180 million in preferred-note financing led by Fortress Investment Group affiliates. The deal closed on 1st April 2026 and will fund the build-out of the company’s land-based salmon facility in Japan.The investment signals robust institutional support for a project targeting 10,000 tonnes of annual production and aiming to become Japan’s largest land-based salmon operation. The facility will implement recirculating aquaculture system (RAS) technology to help reduce imports and strengthen domestic seafood supply.CEO Erol Emed commented, "We are deeply encouraged by the dedication and conviction shown by our investors in supporting Pure Salmon Japan through this financing. Their commitment underscores the growing relevance of sustainable domestic seafood production in Japan." Fortress Investment Group, with a long-standing presence in Japan, brings both capital and strategic know-how. Eric Golden, Co-Head of Asia at Fortress, highlighted the project’s role in enhancing Japan's food production infrastructure.

+ FORTRESS INVESTMENT GROUP EXPANDS PALM COAST SENIOR HOUSING WITH TUSCAN GARDENS ACQUISITION

Fortress Investment Group LLC has announced the acquisition of Tuscan Gardens of Palm Coast, a Class A seniors housing community in Palm Coast, Florida, with the deal dated 12th March 2026.Tuscan Gardens has 130 units and offers a full continuum of care, including assisted living, enhanced assisted living and memory care, on an 11-acre site about one mile from the Atlantic Ocean.Established in 2018, the Palm Coast community serves an affluent and growing senior population along Florida's northeast coast, and occupancy has improved under AgeWell Solvere Living since 2023.Fortress notes the asset's location and quality position it for long-term growth in the senior housing sector, aligning with the firm's strategy to invest in well-located properties.

+ FORTRESS CLOSES $96 MILLION LOAN TO SUPPORT JASPER'S CLASS A APARTMENT COMMUNITY IN DOWNTOWN LOS ANGELES

Fortress Investment Group closed a $96 million whole loan for Jasper, a 296-unit Class A multifamily community in DowntownLos Angeles. The financing was finalized on Feb. 9, 2026, as Fortress continues to back high-quality urban housing developments near major university campuses.Jasper is developed by Cityview and situated near the University of Southern California. The property comprises studios, and one- and two-bedroom units with upscale finishes and a broad amenity package that includes affordable housing units. Proceeds will refinance construction debt and support leasing progress for the new asset.Cityview's Damian Gancman praised Fortress for a smooth, solution-driven process and seamless execution from start to finish. Fortress's Noah Shore emphasized Jasper's fit with the firm's focus on backing well-capitalized, newly built multifamily projects with experienced sponsors, reflecting Fortress's broader investment approach.

+ KKR & CO [3 News That Matters]

+ KKR & CO. INC. (NYSE:KKR) TIES WITH ALTERRA TO EXPAND CLIMATE TRANSITION FUNDING

KKR & Co. Inc. (NYSE:KKR) Ties With ALTERRA to Expand Climate Transition FundingKKR & Co. Inc. (NYSE:KKR) partners with ALTERRA, the UAE’s $30 billion blended finance vehicle, to support its climate transition strategy. The announcement was made on Apr. 22, 2026. ALTERRA launched at COP28 and aims to mobilize up to $250 billion in climate finance by 2030, with a significant portion directed to developing markets. ALTERRA already counts BlackRock, Brookfield Asset Management and TPG as partners.KKR’s climate strategy, co-managed by Charlie Gailliot and Emmanuel Lagarrigue, covers renewables, energy efficiency and sustainable fuels. KKR manages over $100 billion in assets and has invested more than $44 billion in climate and environmental sustainability projects since 2011. Notable investments include Zenobe, EGC, Avantus and IGNIS P2X. The collaboration with ALTERRA could help scale technologies and improve energy security and decarbonization.Gailliot and Lagarrigue see strong potential to combine ALTERRA’s reach with KKR’s infrastructure know-how to advance decarbonization across sectors.

+ KKR & CO. INC. (NYSE:KKR) FUNDS STARITAS AS IT BECOMES STANDALONE GROWTH VEHICLE

Apr. 22, 2026 — KKR & Co. Inc. (NYSE:KKR) Invests in Staritas through Accel-KKR to support the spin-out from ECR as it pursues growth opportunities in digital technology markets. Staritas will operate as an independent company, receiving resources and guidance from Accel-KKR and KKR to accelerate development and market entry in the digital space. The move highlights investor interest in technology-driven firms able to adapt quickly and scale within competitive markets.

+ KKR & CO. INC. (NYSE:KKR) APPOINTS JOEL TRAUT TO LEAD GLOBAL REAL ESTATE CREDIT

KKR & Co. Inc. (NYSE:KKR) Appoints Joel Traut to Lead Global Real Estate CreditTraut takes on the role of global head of direct lending within KKR's real estate division, effective Apr. 20, 2026, as the firm scales its real estate credit platform. Traut has led the unit since 2015, growing it from a seed investment of $400 million to nearly $45 billion in assets under management by end-2025.Traut's leadership comes as KKR invests nearly $15 billion annually across loans and securities in the U.S. and Europe and expands its European real estate credit business with support from Ali Imraan and a dedicated team of 25 investment and asset management professionals.Paul Fine is named head of originations, succeeding Traut. Fine, a former managing director, will work to deepen client relationships and widen KKR's capital pools. Since 2017 the firm has expanded its capital pools from two to eight, including the $850 million closed for the KKR Opportunistic Real Estate Credit Fund II in February 2025.

+ CARLYLE GROUP [3 News That Matters]

+ CARLYLE GROUP L.P. (NASDAQ:CG) APPOINTS TRIO AS CO-PRESIDENTS TO DRIVE UNIFIED DECISION-MAKING

Carlyle Group L.P. (NASDAQ:CG) appoints three senior executives as co-presidents under Chief Executive Officer Harvey Schwartz, effective after 22nd April 2026, to accelerate decision-making and align operations with its evolving strategy.The co-presidents will share oversight of investment priorities, fundraising, and day-to-day management across Carlyle Group L.P. (NASDAQ:CG)'s businesses, with the goal of improving execution as market conditions shift.Schwartz, who assumed leadership during a transitional period, seeks clearer accountability and faster decisions, a model that aligns with practices at other major firms to maintain momentum.The firm will assess the impact on investment activity and financial performance in forthcoming quarters.

+ CARLYLE STRENGTHENS WEALTH-MANAGEMENT FOOTPRINT WITH MAI CAPITAL ACQUISITION

Carlyle Group is set to expand its wealth-management footprint by acquiring a majority stake in MAI Capital Management from Galway Holdings, Harvest Partners and Oak Hill Capital, valuing MAI at over $2.8 billion. The deal was announced on 31st March 2026.As part of the agreement, MAI employees will retain a meaningful minority stake, keeping MAI independent under Rick Buoncore, who leads as chairman and chief executive.The investment aims to bolster MAI's capital base and growth in financial planning, investment management and family-office services. MAI reports $72.6 billion in assets under management or administration as of Jan 1 across 40 U.S. offices.Closing is expected in the second quarter of 2026, subject to standard regulatory approvals, with Carlyle leveraging its global platform to support continuity for clients and advisors.

+ MAI CAPITAL TO SELL MAJORITY OWNERSHIP TO CARLYLE FOR ABOUT $2.8 BILLION

Apr. 1, 2026 — Carlyle Group L.P. has reached an agreement to acquire a majority stake in MAI Capital Management, valuing MAI at more than $2.8 billion. The move will give Carlyle majority ownership as Galway Holdings, Harvest Partners and Oak Hill Capital exit, while MAI employees retain a significant minority stake. Carlyle had previously invested in MAI in 2021 via Galway Holdings. As of Jan. 1, 2026, MAI oversees $72.6 billion in assets, operates 40 U.S. offices and employs over 700 people. The deal underscores Carlyle's continued expansion even as its stock has been volatile; the firm reports strong liquidity and a 15-year dividend history at about 3%. Analysts rate Carlyle’s overall financial health as FAIR. Rick Buoncore, MAI’s chairman and CEO, said, "As we looked for a long-term partner to help us achieve that vision, Carlyle stood out for its alignment with our culture and values along with their deep industry knowledge and expertise." The transaction is expected to close in the second quarter of 2026, subject to customary closing conditions and regulatory approvals, including consent from the Division of Banking in South Dakota. Harvest Partners, MAI’s former sponsor, added, "We are confident that MAI is well positioned for the next stage of growth with Carlyle’s backing."

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ OAKTREE CAPITAL MANAGEMENT LP [3 News That Matters]

+ BIOXCEL THERAPEUTICS ANNOUNCES NINTH AMENDMENT TO CREDIT AGREEMENT AND NEW WARRANT ISSUANCE

BioXcel Therapeutics, Inc. said it has expanded its financing framework through a Fourth Amended and Restated Registration Rights Agreement with Oaktree Capital Management and other institutional holders. The new agreement, disclosed in a Form 8-K, was announced with the filing date of 18th April 2026 and encompasses changes to prior terms as well as the issuance of additional equity warrants to lenders, reflecting the company’s ongoing capital management strategy. Separately, the Ninth Amendment to BioXcel's Credit Agreement was executed on 27th March 2026, and on 15th April 2026 the company issued unregistered warrants in a private placement. The warrants give lenders the right to acquire more BioXcel common stock, and the private placement means the warrants were not registered under the Securities Act of 1933. BioXcel also stated it will file registration statements with the SEC to enable potential resale of the underlying shares, a move intended to provide liquidity and investor transparency. Lenders have said they will hold the securities for investment purposes, which could reduce immediate selling pressure but may still lead to a higher total share count if the warrants are exercised and the shares registered for resale. The company notes it is not an emerging growth company and will continue to comply with full regulatory reporting. With participation from multiple Oaktree entities, the deal illustrates a continued commitment to securities compliance and to monitoring upcoming filings and announcements that could influence BioXcel’s stock performance.

+ OAKTREE, NORTH MILL EXECUTE $355.8 MILLION ASSET-BACKED SECURITIZATION

Oaktree Capital Management and North Mill Equipment Finance have closed a $355.8 million asset-backed securitization, OAKEF 2026-1, on 8th April 2026, reflecting healthy investor demand for quality equipment finance assets.The securitized assets originated from Midland Equipment Finance in a November 2025 deal. The notes were rated AAA through A and drew strong demand, about 6.3 times subscriptions, while delivering a weighted average spread of 99 basis points and a 4.7% yield.Truist Securities led structuring with Deutsche Bank as co-manager. Brendan Beer of Oaktree noted the market reception as evidence of investor confidence in the strategy and in North Mill's role.North Mill's David Lee emphasized asset quality and the servicing platform, reiterating that NMEF will continue as the primary servicer for the assets, ensuring ongoing management.

+ OAKTREE NAVIGATES PRIVATE CREDIT PRESSURES BY FULFILLING $7.7B REDEMPTIONS AND CUTTING DIVIDEND

On 29th March 2026, Oaktree Capital Management, a Brookfield Asset Management subsidiary, said it would fulfill all redemption requests from its private credit fund, totaling about $7.7 billion, allowing withdrawals up to 8.5% of net assets.Brookfield will contribute roughly $80 million to support the redemptions.The Oaktree Strategic Credit Fund will repurchase 6.8% of its shares, with Brookfield buying an additional 1.7% from a single investor; the fund's dividend will be cut by two cents to 16 cents.As of 23rd March 2026, liquidity stood at $1.8 billion from cash and undrawn facilities, while the firm has sold publicly traded loans and bonds to reduce exposure to software investments. The broader private credit sector remains under pressure, with peers such as Blackstone raising buyback terms to 7%.

+ APOLLO GLOBAL MANAGEMENT [3 News That Matters]

+ MEDICAL SURGICAL SOLUTIONS, LLC AT $9.62 BILLION POST-MONEY VALUATION WITH $1.25 BILLION FROM APOLLO GLOBAL MANAGEMENT

Medical Surgical Solutions, LLC secures $1.25 billion from Apollo Funds, managed by Apollo Global Management, Inc., delivering a 13% stake via convertible preferred shares. The investment was formalized on April 20, 2026 and is contingent on regulatory approvals and customary closing conditions. The post-money valuation stands at about $9.62 billion, and the capital will be used to bolster operations and expand the company’s market footprint as Apollo provides strategic backing through its funds.

+ APOLLO GLOBAL MANAGEMENT LLC (NYSE:APO) REPORTS $1.25 BILLION CONVERTIBLE PREFERRED EQUITY INVESTMENT FOR 13% MCKESSON MEDICAL-SURGICAL SOLUTIONS STAKE

On Apr. 20, 2026, Apollo Global Management LLC (NYSE:APO) Invests $1.25 Billion in McKesson Corporation (NYSE:MCK) for a 13% stake in its Medical-Surgical Solutions division via convertible preferred equity, valuing the unit at approximately $13 billion. The capital supports McKesson's planned separation of the Medical-Surgical Solutions unit and funds strategic initiatives within the business. The transaction reflects a strategic alignment between Apollo and McKesson as the latter moves toward separating its Medical-Surgical Solutions segment and concentrating on core operations. Apollo Global Management oversees about $938.4 billion in assets under management and has a market capitalization near $72.06 billion, with a trailing P/E ratio of 22.58, indicating market expectations for continued growth. Insider activity raises caution, with six insider sells versus one insider buy over the past year, and Apollo's financial strength is rated 6 out of 10. Market participants will watch the arrangement for potential impacts on McKesson's separation plan.

+ APOLLO BROADENS UK HOUSING FOOTPRINT VIA GATEHOUSE LIVING GROUP PURCHASE

Apollo Global Management Inc. has acquired Gatehouse Living Group from Gatehouse Bank, completed on 1st April 2026, bringing Gatehouse Investment Management and Ascend Properties into its UK housing platform.Financial terms were not disclosed.GLG is a Build-to-Rent single-family housing leader with more than 10,000 properties and 5,000 homes acquired, and will continue to be led by CEO Paul Stockwell with Ascend Properties handling external portfolio management.The deal strengthens Apollo's UK strategy and follows its existing interests in Miller Homes and Foundation Home Loans, while Gatehouse Bank focuses on Shariah-compliant financing.

+ ARES MANAGEMENT [3 News That Matters]

+ ARES MANAGEMENT (NYSE:ARES) APPOINTS ASIA LEADERS AND ACQUIRES WHITESTONE REIT TO DIVERSIFY REAL ASSETS

Ares Management (NYSE:ARES) to acquire Whitestone REIT in a US$1.7 billion transaction, taking private a retail-focused REIT and expanding essential consumer-focused centers across U.S. metros. On Apr. 14, 2026, E.G. Morse was named Partner and Head of Asia Credit, with Dinesh Goel and Gabriel Fong leading the Asia Special Situations team, underscoring Ares' intent to bolster Asia-focused credit strategies. The Whitestone acquisition broadens Ares' real estate, private credit, and real assets exposure and supports diversification across geographies. Investors should monitor integration progress and Asia fundraising as the leadership changes take shape, while considering risks such as margin pressure and elevated debt; the 5.38% yield is not fully covered by earnings

+ WHITESTONE REIT TO BECOME PRIVATE AS ARES COMPLETES $1.7B ACQUISITION

On Apr. 10, 2026, Ares Management announced plans to acquire Whitestone REIT in an all-cash deal valued at about $1.7 billion, at $19.00 per share or unit. The transaction will render Whitestone private and remove it from the NYSE. Whitestone’s 56 properties total around 4.9 million square feet across Sun Belt markets such as Phoenix, Austin, Dallas–Fort Worth, Houston and San Antonio. The move aims to broaden Ares Real Estate’s footprint with necessity-based retail centers in high-demand Arizona and Texas markets. Whitestone CEO Dave Holeman stated the company’s strategy centers on high-return, smaller spaces with a diversified tenant base, adding that the agreement with Ares validates this approach for shareholders. Ares’ global head of real estate strategy and growth, David Roth, emphasized the appeal of New Economy real estate and convenient consumer experiences, highlighting the growing demand for grocery, pharmacy, healthcare, fitness and dining options.

+ ARES MANAGEMENT DEPLOYS $5.4 BILLION TO ACCELERATE VALUE-ADD REAL ESTATE RECOVERY

Ares Management Corporation has secured about $5.4 billion for value-add real estate investments in the United States and Europe, a move aimed at capitalizing on supply-constrained markets likely to rebound soon, with a focus on logistics, multifamily, and self-storage sectors.The fundraising includes $1.9 billion for the EPEP IV fund and roughly $3.5 billion for the U.S. value-add program, with initial investments identified or deployed totaling around $1.1 billion on each track.Analysts are broadly supportive; about 75% are bullish, and the consensus price target sits near $161, suggesting roughly 60% upside. However, on 24th March 2026, BMO Capital reduced its target to $112 from $140 and issued a Market Perform rating amid concerns about BDC redemptions and broader market volatility.Ares, founded as a global alternative investment manager, had BlackRock as a notable backer with approximately 7.12% stake at end-2025, underscoring strong institutional support. The note also reminds investors that AI-related equities may offer greater upside in some scenarios, reinforcing the case for diversification.

+ QATAR AIRWAYS [3 News That Matters]

+ QATAR AIRWAYS CARGO RESTARTS FREIGHTER AND PASSENGER LINKS TO UAE ON EXPANDED NETWORK

Qatar Airways Cargo has resumed both freighter and belly operations to and from Doha after safety concerns. The new schedule confirms freighter flights to Dubai World Central (DWC) twice weekly from 26th April and to Sharjah (SHJ) three times weekly from 1st May, while daily passenger services to Dubai (DXB) and Sharjah (SHJ) also restart, with freighter reach planned to exceed 50 destinations by 1st May and the overall passenger network expected to top 150 by 16th June.Urgent freight restrictions have been lifted on routes such as Victoria, Australia, with the carrier introducing special handling codes QRLIV for fish, mammals and diplomatic cargo, and LHO for live human organs and blood, effective 1st May.Reservations remain accessible via the Digital Lounge and Qatar Airways Cargo sales teams, alongside standard sales channels. The resumption is described as a key milestone for regional logistics and supports ongoing expansion.Industry stakeholders are watching the pace of growth, as the UAE freight market benefits from improved connectivity and service levels.

+ QATAR AIRWAYS INTRODUCES NEXT-GENERATION QSUITE, AIMING TO SUSTAIN PREMIUM EDGE

Qatar Airways announced the Qsuite Next Generation, with a launch planned later this year on the Airbus A350-1000 after delays with the 777-9, as of 23rd April 2026. The Next Generation builds on the iconic Qsuite introduced in 2017, maintaining sliding doors, double beds, and modular seating while adding a movable 4K OLED screen and stronger privacy options. The initiative reinforces Qatar Airways' premium strategy and its Doha hub, where exclusive check-in, fast-track security, and premium lounges contribute to a seamless travel experience. By investing in product development, the airline seeks to attract high-yield corporate travelers and affluent leisure customers as competition in luxury cabins intensifies.

+ QATAR AIRWAYS SETS OUT PHASED EMBARGO LIFTS DURING RECOVERY

Qatar Airways is steadily expanding its operations as it rebuilds its network, with passenger routes restored to more than 150 destinations and freighter coverage over 50 locations as of April 20, 2026.The restoration is part of a wider plan to increase capacity amid rising air freight demand. Some shipments remain embargoed, with AVF, AVM, and DOC handling codes restricted, and live organs and blood not accepted.QRLIV shipments can be booked under different codes, as long as Doha transit time stays within 24 hours. Other products remain bookable via standard sales channels, and the carrier emphasizes adaptability and a commitment to service as it expands.

value.Title: May 05, 2026: Crowley Maritime Achieves Safe Transit of CS Anthem Through Strait of Hormuz

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JJW-DVG3-S2B7-82H9-00000-00May 05, 2026: Crowley Maritime Achieves Safe Transit of CS Anthem Through Strait of Hormuz2026-05-13T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:46:15ZLexisNexisANNOUNCEMENTSMay 05, 2026: Crowley Maritime Achieves Safe Transit of CS Anthem Through Strait of Hormuz

Crowley Maritime has confirmed that its managed vessel, CS Anthem, has successfully transited the Strait of Hormuz, providing crucial visibility into the limited number of U.S.-flagged ships that have exited the Persian Gulf since the conflict began. On the 5th of May, 2026, the company stated that CS Anthem has safely completed its transit through this vital maritime chokepoint.

In its statement, Crowley emphasized that the safety of its crews and vessels, as well as the continuity of service for its customers, remain top priorities. The company also acknowledged the professionalism of U.S. Navy and government partners who work globally to support maritime safety. However, Crowley refrained from disclosing further operational details, citing company policy against discussing specifics of vessel routing or security arrangements.

CS Anthem is now one of only two U.S.-flagged commercial vessels confirmed to have transited the Strait of Hormuz under the Trump administration's newly initiated 'Project Freedom' operation. This operation aims to facilitate the evacuation of ships via Oman’s territorial waters within an enhanced U.S.-backed security area. Alongside CS Anthem, A.P. Moller – Maersk's U.S.-flagged vessel, Alliance Fairfax, also successfully exited the Persian Gulf under U.S. military escort, marking a significant milestone in Washington’s efforts to guide stranded vessels out of the region.

Despite these successful transits, the overall shipping environment through the Strait of Hormuz remains precarious. Persistent risks, including threats from mines, drone activities, and an expanded military presence, continue to deter a broader return of commercial traffic in this crucial waterway. Industry observers caution that these movements are indicative of highly coordinated, one-off transits rather than a sign of a broader reopening for commercial shipping.

Both the CS Anthem and Alliance Fairfax belong to a small group of U.S.-flagged vessels effectively stranded in the Gulf since late February. They are part of the Maritime Security Program (MSP) or the Tanker Security Program (TSP), ensuring the availability of commercially operated U.S.-flagged vessels for military logistics during crises. The CS Anthem, a 49,990-metric-ton medium-range tanker, is managed by Crowley under a bareboat charter arrangement, further illustrating the company's strategic positioning in a highly complex maritime landscape.

INDEX

SECTION 1 CROWLEY MARITIME PROFILE

SECTION 2 CROWLEY MARITIME TOP MANAGEMENT

SECTION 3 CROWLEY MARITIME RANKING

SECTION 1 CROWLEY MARITIME PROFILE

1.1 ACTIVITIES

Crowley Maritime Corporation, founded in 1892, is a family-owned logistics and maritime services company headquartered in Jacksonville, Florida. Employing over 5,300 people, it operates in shipping, energy, and supply chain management, generating over $2 billion annually. Crowley provides ocean transport, LNG distribution, and logistics for government and commercial clients. In 2024, it expanded renewable energy logistics, supporting offshore wind projects. Its culture emphasizes safety, innovation, and sustainability, with robust employee training. Competing with Maersk, Crowley stands out for its U.S.-focused operations and government contracts. Recent initiatives include decarbonization efforts and digital tracking systems, aligning with green logistics trends.
