value.Title: WEEKLY CORPORATE NEWS TRACKER: YALE UNIVERSITY NEWS THIS PAST WEEK MAY 10, 2026

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JJG-9DP3-RRPY-Y53M-00000-00WEEKLY CORPORATE NEWS TRACKER: YALE UNIVERSITY NEWS THIS PAST WEEK MAY 10, 20262026-05-11T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:40:23ZLexisNexisWEEKLY CORPORATE NEWS TRACKER: YALE UNIVERSITY NEWS THIS PAST WEEK MAY 10, 2026

+ YALE UNIVERSITY [3 News That Matters]

+ YALE UNIVERSITY REVEALS UPDATED CLIMATE OPINION MAPS FOR 2025

Yale University has released its updated Climate Opinion Maps, providing a detailed view of U.S. public sentiment regarding climate change and energy policy. This latest version, published on the 5th of May 2026, reveals a significant shift in attitudes toward renewable energy sources across the nation, particularly in support for solar and wind energy projects. The updated Yale Climate Opinion Maps introduce four critical measures: support for local solar farms, local wind farms, opposition to fossil fuel drilling on public land, and preference for candidates who advocate for climate action. Notably, over half of the states in the U.S. show majority support for solar (44 states) and wind farms (29 states), indicating a clear trend towards renewable energy solutions in local communities.

Further analysis from Yale University indicates that a substantial 57% of Americans are inclined to vote for candidates who prioritize action on global warming. However, this preference varies significantly across different regions, from as low as 40% in West Virginia to as high as 87% in the District of Columbia. This local disparity highlights the complex relationship between geographic location and climate policy support. Despite federal efforts to limit renewable energy projects, the momentum for solar and wind energy continues to grow, with notable increases in solar generation (up 37%) and wind energy (up 12%) from the previous year. States like California, Texas, and Florida lead the way in public support for local solar initiatives, reinforcing the trend of local advocacy for clean energy solutions. The comprehensive data provided by the Yale Climate Opinion Maps serves as a vital resource for understanding public attitudes toward climate change and energy policies. As the country navigates a pivotal moment in addressing climate issues, Yale University's findings will be instrumental for policymakers, educators, and stakeholders aiming to align with the evolving public sentiment around renewable energy.

+ SOPHIE BUCHOWSKI JOINS YALE UNIVERSITY AS A PROMISING SCHOLAR ATHLETE

Sophie Buchowski, a standout athlete from John Burroughs School, has earned the title of the 2026 Post-Dispatch Scholar Athlete. This recognition highlights her exceptional performance both in academics and athletics, particularly in field hockey and lacrosse. Buchowski, who has a unique hobby of creating her own version of slime, will be taking her talents to Yale University, where she plans to continue her field hockey career while pursuing a degree in medicine. On the 5th of May, 2026, it was announced that Buchowski would join Yale University, a prestigious institution known for its rigorous academic programs and strong athletic programs. The Scholar Athlete initiative acknowledges students across various high schools who demonstrate excellence in sports as well as in the classroom. Buchowski's achievements in athletics and her commitment to academic excellence make her a fitting representative of this program. Buchowski's journey is not only marked by her athletic prowess but also by her academic aspirations. As she prepares to transition to Yale University, she aims to balance her demanding schedule of sports and studies. Her dedication to both her athletic and academic pursuits reflects a growing trend among student-athletes who aspire to excel in multiple domains. This achievement is a testament to her hard work and a source of inspiration for her peers. The Yale University athletic department has expressed excitement over Buchowski's commitment, emphasizing the value of student-athletes in fostering a culture of excellence. With a focus on developing athletes who are also scholars, Yale encourages its students to pursue their passions and goals, both on and off the field. Buchowski's dual commitment to sports and medicine aligns perfectly with this mission. As Buchowski embarks on this new chapter at Yale University, she joins a roster of talented student-athletes who have similarly balanced their academic and athletic careers. Her story serves as a reminder of the opportunities available to those who are dedicated and driven. The future looks bright for Sophie Buchowski as she steps onto the field at Yale, eager to make her mark both as an athlete and a future medical professional.

+ YALE UNIVERSITY UNVEILS NEW MISSION STATEMENT AMID TRUST CONCERNS

Yale University has introduced a new mission statement aimed at restoring public trust in higher education. The revised statement, which reads: 'Yale’s core mission is to create, disseminate, and preserve knowledge through research and teaching,' comes in the wake of a university-commissioned report released on 15th April 2026 that outlined key recommendations for enhancing the institution's credibility. The change marks a significant departure from Yale's previous mission statement, which was more expansive and included phrases such as 'improving the world today' and 'educating aspiring leaders worldwide.' Yale's new statement is notably concise, reflecting the report's emphasis on clarity and purpose in higher education. The committee of ten professors responsible for the report highlighted 'widespread uncertainty about the fundamental purpose and mission of higher education,' urging a more focused approach. Yale's President, Maurie McInnis, who initiated the committee's formation last year, acknowledged the report as an 'honest assessment' of the university's role in the decline of public trust. In her end-of-year message to the community, she indicated that the university had already begun implementing many of the committee's recommendations, including the adoption of this new mission statement. The report specifically criticized the previous mission statement's complexity, arguing that while its goals were commendable, they detracted from the core purpose of a university. The committee asserted that without a clear mission, it becomes challenging to measure whether educational institutions are fulfilling their commitments. The new statement aligns closely with a model referenced in the report, which aims to rebuild trust in Yale and similar institutions. In her message, McInnis emphasized the importance of public confidence in higher education, stating that the university's work must resonate with societal needs. She reaffirmed Yale's commitment to addressing global challenges and enhancing educational accessibility, including initiatives like free tuition for more students. As Yale continues to navigate the evolving landscape of higher education, McInnis plans to provide further updates in the coming months, reinforcing the institution's mission and priorities.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

urn:contentItem:6JJD-NJC3-S2NH-M2TH-00000-00WEEKLY INDUSTRY ACTIVITY SNAPSHOT: YALE UNIVERSITY NEWS THIS PAST WEEK MAY 10, 20262026-05-11T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:40:23ZLexisNexisWEEKLY INDUSTRY ACTIVITY SNAPSHOT: YALE UNIVERSITY NEWS THIS PAST WEEK MAY 10, 2026

+ YALE UNIVERSITY [3 News That Matters]

+ YALE UNIVERSITY REVEALS UPDATED CLIMATE OPINION MAPS FOR 2025

Yale University has released its updated Climate Opinion Maps, providing a detailed view of U.S. public sentiment regarding climate change and energy policy. This latest version, published on the 5th of May 2026, reveals a significant shift in attitudes toward renewable energy sources across the nation, particularly in support for solar and wind energy projects. The updated Yale Climate Opinion Maps introduce four critical measures: support for local solar farms, local wind farms, opposition to fossil fuel drilling on public land, and preference for candidates who advocate for climate action. Notably, over half of the states in the U.S. show majority support for solar (44 states) and wind farms (29 states), indicating a clear trend towards renewable energy solutions in local communities.

Further analysis from Yale University indicates that a substantial 57% of Americans are inclined to vote for candidates who prioritize action on global warming. However, this preference varies significantly across different regions, from as low as 40% in West Virginia to as high as 87% in the District of Columbia. This local disparity highlights the complex relationship between geographic location and climate policy support. Despite federal efforts to limit renewable energy projects, the momentum for solar and wind energy continues to grow, with notable increases in solar generation (up 37%) and wind energy (up 12%) from the previous year. States like California, Texas, and Florida lead the way in public support for local solar initiatives, reinforcing the trend of local advocacy for clean energy solutions. The comprehensive data provided by the Yale Climate Opinion Maps serves as a vital resource for understanding public attitudes toward climate change and energy policies. As the country navigates a pivotal moment in addressing climate issues, Yale University's findings will be instrumental for policymakers, educators, and stakeholders aiming to align with the evolving public sentiment around renewable energy.

+ SOPHIE BUCHOWSKI JOINS YALE UNIVERSITY AS A PROMISING SCHOLAR ATHLETE

Sophie Buchowski, a standout athlete from John Burroughs School, has earned the title of the 2026 Post-Dispatch Scholar Athlete. This recognition highlights her exceptional performance both in academics and athletics, particularly in field hockey and lacrosse. Buchowski, who has a unique hobby of creating her own version of slime, will be taking her talents to Yale University, where she plans to continue her field hockey career while pursuing a degree in medicine. On the 5th of May, 2026, it was announced that Buchowski would join Yale University, a prestigious institution known for its rigorous academic programs and strong athletic programs. The Scholar Athlete initiative acknowledges students across various high schools who demonstrate excellence in sports as well as in the classroom. Buchowski's achievements in athletics and her commitment to academic excellence make her a fitting representative of this program. Buchowski's journey is not only marked by her athletic prowess but also by her academic aspirations. As she prepares to transition to Yale University, she aims to balance her demanding schedule of sports and studies. Her dedication to both her athletic and academic pursuits reflects a growing trend among student-athletes who aspire to excel in multiple domains. This achievement is a testament to her hard work and a source of inspiration for her peers. The Yale University athletic department has expressed excitement over Buchowski's commitment, emphasizing the value of student-athletes in fostering a culture of excellence. With a focus on developing athletes who are also scholars, Yale encourages its students to pursue their passions and goals, both on and off the field. Buchowski's dual commitment to sports and medicine aligns perfectly with this mission. As Buchowski embarks on this new chapter at Yale University, she joins a roster of talented student-athletes who have similarly balanced their academic and athletic careers. Her story serves as a reminder of the opportunities available to those who are dedicated and driven. The future looks bright for Sophie Buchowski as she steps onto the field at Yale, eager to make her mark both as an athlete and a future medical professional.

+ YALE UNIVERSITY UNVEILS NEW MISSION STATEMENT AMID TRUST CONCERNS

Yale University has introduced a new mission statement aimed at restoring public trust in higher education. The revised statement, which reads: 'Yale’s core mission is to create, disseminate, and preserve knowledge through research and teaching,' comes in the wake of a university-commissioned report released on 15th April 2026 that outlined key recommendations for enhancing the institution's credibility. The change marks a significant departure from Yale's previous mission statement, which was more expansive and included phrases such as 'improving the world today' and 'educating aspiring leaders worldwide.' Yale's new statement is notably concise, reflecting the report's emphasis on clarity and purpose in higher education. The committee of ten professors responsible for the report highlighted 'widespread uncertainty about the fundamental purpose and mission of higher education,' urging a more focused approach. Yale's President, Maurie McInnis, who initiated the committee's formation last year, acknowledged the report as an 'honest assessment' of the university's role in the decline of public trust. In her end-of-year message to the community, she indicated that the university had already begun implementing many of the committee's recommendations, including the adoption of this new mission statement. The report specifically criticized the previous mission statement's complexity, arguing that while its goals were commendable, they detracted from the core purpose of a university. The committee asserted that without a clear mission, it becomes challenging to measure whether educational institutions are fulfilling their commitments. The new statement aligns closely with a model referenced in the report, which aims to rebuild trust in Yale and similar institutions. In her message, McInnis emphasized the importance of public confidence in higher education, stating that the university's work must resonate with societal needs. She reaffirmed Yale's commitment to addressing global challenges and enhancing educational accessibility, including initiatives like free tuition for more students. As Yale continues to navigate the evolving landscape of higher education, McInnis plans to provide further updates in the coming months, reinforcing the institution's mission and priorities.

+ YALE UNIVERSITY [3 News That Matters]

+ YALE UNIVERSITY REVEALS UPDATED CLIMATE OPINION MAPS FOR 2025

Yale University has released its updated Climate Opinion Maps, providing a detailed view of U.S. public sentiment regarding climate change and energy policy. This latest version, published on the 5th of May 2026, reveals a significant shift in attitudes toward renewable energy sources across the nation, particularly in support for solar and wind energy projects. The updated Yale Climate Opinion Maps introduce four critical measures: support for local solar farms, local wind farms, opposition to fossil fuel drilling on public land, and preference for candidates who advocate for climate action. Notably, over half of the states in the U.S. show majority support for solar (44 states) and wind farms (29 states), indicating a clear trend towards renewable energy solutions in local communities.

Further analysis from Yale University indicates that a substantial 57% of Americans are inclined to vote for candidates who prioritize action on global warming. However, this preference varies significantly across different regions, from as low as 40% in West Virginia to as high as 87% in the District of Columbia. This local disparity highlights the complex relationship between geographic location and climate policy support. Despite federal efforts to limit renewable energy projects, the momentum for solar and wind energy continues to grow, with notable increases in solar generation (up 37%) and wind energy (up 12%) from the previous year. States like California, Texas, and Florida lead the way in public support for local solar initiatives, reinforcing the trend of local advocacy for clean energy solutions. The comprehensive data provided by the Yale Climate Opinion Maps serves as a vital resource for understanding public attitudes toward climate change and energy policies. As the country navigates a pivotal moment in addressing climate issues, Yale University's findings will be instrumental for policymakers, educators, and stakeholders aiming to align with the evolving public sentiment around renewable energy.

value.Title: May 05, 2026: Yale University Reveals Updated Climate Opinion Maps for 2025

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JHT-DBX3-RXH7-2457-00000-00May 05, 2026: Yale University Reveals Updated Climate Opinion Maps for 20252026-05-08T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:40:23ZLexisNexisANNOUNCEMENTSMay 05, 2026: Yale University Reveals Updated Climate Opinion Maps for 2025

Yale University has released its updated Climate Opinion Maps, providing a detailed view of U.S. public sentiment regarding climate change and energy policy. This latest version, published on the 5th of May 2026, reveals a significant shift in attitudes toward renewable energy sources across the nation, particularly in support for solar and wind energy projects.

The updated Yale Climate Opinion Maps introduce four critical measures: support for local solar farms, local wind farms, opposition to fossil fuel drilling on public land, and preference for candidates who advocate for climate action. Notably, over half of the states in the U.S. show majority support for solar (44 states) and wind farms (29 states), indicating a clear trend towards renewable energy solutions in local communities.

Further analysis from Yale University indicates that a substantial 57% of Americans are inclined to vote for candidates who prioritize action on global warming. However, this preference varies significantly across different regions, from as low as 40% in West Virginia to as high as 87% in the District of Columbia. This local disparity highlights the complex relationship between geographic location and climate policy support.

Despite federal efforts to limit renewable energy projects, the momentum for solar and wind energy continues to grow, with notable increases in solar generation (up 37%) and wind energy (up 12%) from the previous year. States like California, Texas, and Florida lead the way in public support for local solar initiatives, reinforcing the trend of local advocacy for clean energy solutions.

The comprehensive data provided by the Yale Climate Opinion Maps serves as a vital resource for understanding public attitudes toward climate change and energy policies. As the country navigates a pivotal moment in addressing climate issues, Yale University's findings will be instrumental for policymakers, educators, and stakeholders aiming to align with the evolving public sentiment around renewable energy.

INDEX

SECTION 1 YALE UNIVERSITY PROFILE

SECTION 2 YALE UNIVERSITY TOP MANAGEMENT

SECTION 1 YALE UNIVERSITY PROFILE

1.1 ACTIVITIES

Yale University, founded in 1701, is an Ivy League institution in New Haven, Connecticut. With around 15,000 employees and 12,000 students, it generates approximately $4.5 billion in annual revenue. Yale excels in humanities, sciences, and professional schools like law and medicine, with notable alumni including five U.S. presidents. In 2024, it expanded sustainability research. Competing with Harvard and Princeton, Yales mission is to educate leaders and advance discovery through rigorous scholarship and global outreach.

Mission

Yale's mission is to improve the world through exceptional research, education, and community engagement. By fostering a diverse, ethical, and collaborative environment, the university educates aspiring leaders dedicated to serving society across all sectors.

Vision

Yale aims to be the most student-focused research university globally. This vision is realized through faculty dedication to teaching, close engagement with students, and ensuring that all students benefit from Yale's extraordinary resources and programs.

Core Values

Excellence: Commitment to the highest standards in all endeavors.

Integrity: Ethical principles guide every decision and action.

Diversity and Inclusion: A welcoming environment that celebrates diverse perspectives and backgrounds.

Community: A collaborative culture that supports mutual growth and respect.

Service: Empowering members to positively impact society.

Research and Innovation

As a leader in research, Yale drives discovery across disciplines, preserving cultural and scientific heritage while inspiring innovation for future generations.

From its storied past to its ongoing contributions to society, Yale University continues to illuminate the world with its commitment to Lux et Veritas.

1.2 SUMMARY

value.Title: CROWLEY MARITIME - COMPREHENSIVE NEWS FROM INDUSTRY AND ITS PEERS

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JHN-7S43-SK5D-33H0-00000-00CROWLEY MARITIME - COMPREHENSIVE NEWS FROM INDUSTRY AND ITS PEERS2026-05-07T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:36:34ZLexisNexisSTOCKCROWLEY MARITIME - COMPREHENSIVE NEWS FROM INDUSTRY AND ITS PEERS

DASHBOARD: CROWLEY MARITIME

PermID4295904276Websitehttp://www.crowley.com/IndustryMaritime & Shipping Logistics

ACTIVITIES:

Crowley Maritime Corporation, founded in 1892, is a family-owned logistics and maritime services company headquartered in Jacksonville, Florida. Employing over 5,300 people, it operates in shipping, energy, and supply chain management, generating over $2 billion annually. Crowley provides ocean transport, LNG distribution, and logistics for government and commercial clients. In 2024, it expanded renewable energy logistics, supporting offshore wind projects. Its culture emphasizes safety, innovation, and sustainability, with robust employee training. Competing with Maersk, Crowley stands out for its U.S.-focused operations and government contracts. Recent initiatives include decarbonization efforts and digital tracking systems, aligning with green logistics trends.

+ CROWLEY MARITIME [3 News That Matters]

+ CROWLEY MARITIME ADVANCES ALASKA GROWTH WITH PORT MCKENZIE FUEL TERMINAL FEASIBILITY

Crowley Maritime has announced a plan to conduct a feasibility study for a potential fuel terminal at Port McKenzie, signaling a broader push to expand its Alaska footprint. The permit was granted on January 7, 2026, as rising regional activity underscores demand for improved logistics and transportation capabilities.Officials say the terminal would provide a reliable fuel-supply chain for local marine and land operations, supporting growing commercial activity in the Matanuska-Susitna Borough and the broader Alaska economy.Crowley's strategy centers on leveraging Port McKenzie's location to enhance logistics and attract other tenants. If developed, the project could streamline Crowley's operations and help catalyze additional investment in the area.With the permit in place, Crowley moves forward with feasibility assessments and engagement with local stakeholders, marking a potential milestone for the company and for Port McKenzie as they explore future growth opportunities.

+ CROWLEY MARITIME ANNOUNCES DUAL-DIVISION REALIGNMENT TO ENHANCE OPERATIONAL AGILITY

Crowley Maritime announced on January 6, 2026 the formation of two new divisions—Shipping and Logistics led by James C. Fowler, and Energy led by Kollin Fencil—as part of a strategic consolidation to improve execution and accelerate growth across its shipping, logistics and energy activities.The Shipping and Logistics Division combines Crowley's logistics, land transportation and global ship management to serve commercial and government customers with ocean shipping, inland trucking, multimodal transportation and warehousing.Fowler said the reorganized structure will enable cohesive management of the entire transportation value chain, driving growth through safe and efficient supply-chain solutions.The Energy Division consolidates Crowley Fuels in Alaska, LNG services and advanced energy solutions, enabling greater innovation and scale in both traditional and emerging energy markets.Fencil noted the realignment will help Crowley meet evolving customer demands for traditional fuels and advanced energy solutions, and the leaders have delivered improvements in financial performance and operational efficiency since joining the company in 2023. Chief Operating Officer Megan Davidson said the realignment will speed decision-making and improve the company's ability to respond to market dynamics, reinforcing Crowley's status as a leader in maritime, energy and logistics. Crowley is privately held and U.S.-owned, with a footprint spanning 36 nations and territories and more than 130 years in business.

+ FAIRWATER HOLDINGS EMERGES AS NEW JONES ACT SERVICE PROVIDER FROM CROWLEY-SEACOR ALLIANCE

Crowley Maritime and SEACOR Holdings have created Fairwater Holdings, a new joint venture intended to integrate their liquid energy and chemical transportation assets and to serve as an independent U.S. Jones Act service provider.The venture will combine the strengths of Crowley and SEACOR to offer transportation solutions across the petroleum and chemical trades, plus third-party ship management.Fairwater will manage a fleet of 20 ocean-going articulated tug-barges and 11 tankers, largely under long-term charters, and will provide crewing and technical management for an additional 21 vessels owned by third parties.Daniel Thorogood, currently CEO of Seabulk, will take the helm as CEO of Fairwater once the deal closes.Crowley's Tom Crowley described Seabulk as an ideal partner with a proven track record, adding the venture supports Crowley's growth strategy and strengthens its position as the largest American-flag petroleum and chemical tank vessel fleet, with capacity exceeding 12 million barrels.The companies expect to begin operations after the customary closing conditions and regulatory approvals, with Fairwater slated to commence in the first quarter of 2024.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ CMA CGM LOGISTICS [3 News That Matters]

+ SAUDI PORTS AUTHORITY LAUNCHES CMA CGM ROUTE TO STRENGTHEN TRADE LINKS

RIYADH: Mawani has added CMACGM's Ocean Rise service to Jeddah Islamic Port, expanding maritime connectivity to Asia and Europe. The service commenced on the 3rd of May 2026 and links Jeddah with Kobe, Nagoya, and Yokohama in Japan; Xiamen, Yantian, and Nansha in China; Rotterdam in the Netherlands; Hamburg in Germany; and Southampton in the UK.Mawani said the service has a handling capacity of up to 3,001 TEUs, supporting Saudi exports and imports in cooperation with international shipping lines. The addition supports Mawani's ongoing push to boost the Kingdom's global logistics capabilities.The move aligns with the National Transport and Logistics Strategy, which aims to reinforce Saudi Arabia's standing as a global logistics hub connecting three continents.During this period Mawani has launched 18 maritime shipping services with a combined estimated capacity of 123,552 TEUs.Port activity figures show momentum, with March cargo throughput up 6.66 percent year on year and 667,882 TEUs handled in February, a 20.89 percent rise from a year earlier. Jeddah Islamic Port remains a critical gateway supported by advanced facilities to bolster international trade.

+ CMA CGM TRANSFERS TWO MORE SHIPS TO INDIA, LIFTS INDIAN-FLAG FLEET TO SIX

CMACGM has moved two more containerships into the Indian registry, expanding its Indian-flag fleet to six as the group solidifies its presence in the Indian market. The development follows earlier transfers of CMA CGM Victoria, CMA CGM Mendelssohn, CMA CGM Diamond and CMA CGM Manaus.The Vila do Conde, a 33,434-dwt vessel built in 2009 with a capacity of 2,556 TEU, was re-flagged from Malta to India and has been part of CMACGM's fleet since 2023. Papers have also been filed for the transfer of the Semarang, built in 2007 with a deadweight of 38,000 and a 2,700 TEU capacity.These two new transfers bring CMA CGM to six Indian-flag vessels, a noteworthy step after it led the way among major carriers in moving ships to the Indian registry. Maersk and MSC have since followed suit, reflecting India’s tightened cabotage regime.India's new rules restrict foreign-flag vessels from carrying domestic cargo, and since January 2026 the Ports Ministry has rolled back a prior exemption for certain transshipment. CMA CGM has already started employing Indian seafarers on its Indian-flag vessels—about 1,000 now, with plans to reach 1,500 by year-end. Additionally, CMA CGM signed a February 2026 contract with Cochin Shipyard for LNG-powered newbuilds, underscoring its growth and sustainability push in the region.

+ INDIA EMERGES AS GROWTH ENGINE AS CMA CGM PUSHES AI AND ECOSYSTEM PARTNERSHIPS IN LOGISTICS

The logistics sector is undergoing a transformation as supply chains become more volatile and customer expectations rise. CMA CGM Group outlines India's emergence as both a strategic growth market and a global innovation hub, driven by AI, ecosystem partnerships, and digital platforms that are reshaping port operations and customer experience. The move signals a shift from reactive to predictive, intelligent, and increasingly autonomous supply chains.On 28th April 2026, CMA CGM underscored its pledge to harness artificial intelligence to improve operational efficiency in logistics. The company is building AI-powered solutions to optimize supply chain management and curb disruptions caused by unpredictable market dynamics, leveraging analytics and machine learning to create a more resilient, responsive network for customers.CMACGM's plan involves forging partnerships with Indian tech companies to build a robust ecosystem that enables AI integration across logistics operations. This collaborative model not only accelerates innovation but also positions India as a pivotal player in the global logistics landscape. The firm envisions AI as a core element of its operating framework, driving smarter decision-making and enhanced customer service.The company is also investing in digital platforms that boost transparency and real-time shipment tracking. This capability helps businesses make informed decisions and improves overall performance, with the aim of delivering a seamless client experience and accelerating growth in e-commerce and manufacturing.With CMACGM leading the charge, the industry stands on the cusp of significant change. Its AI-first strategy highlights how ongoing innovation is essential to maintaining a competitive edge, and CMACGM's India-focused initiatives could serve as a model for others navigating modern, complex supply chains.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

value.Title: CROWLEY MARITIME - THE RECENT ON INDUSTRY AND PEER DEVELOPMENTS

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JHF-8HY3-S51B-P1GG-00000-00CROWLEY MARITIME - THE RECENT ON INDUSTRY AND PEER DEVELOPMENTS2026-05-06T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:36:34ZLexisNexisSTOCKCROWLEY MARITIME - THE RECENT ON INDUSTRY AND PEER DEVELOPMENTS

DASHBOARD: CROWLEY MARITIME

PermID4295904276Websitehttp://www.crowley.com/IndustryMaritime & Shipping Logistics

ACTIVITIES:

Crowley Maritime Corporation, founded in 1892, is a family-owned logistics and maritime services company headquartered in Jacksonville, Florida. Employing over 5,300 people, it operates in shipping, energy, and supply chain management, generating over $2 billion annually. Crowley provides ocean transport, LNG distribution, and logistics for government and commercial clients. In 2024, it expanded renewable energy logistics, supporting offshore wind projects. Its culture emphasizes safety, innovation, and sustainability, with robust employee training. Competing with Maersk, Crowley stands out for its U.S.-focused operations and government contracts. Recent initiatives include decarbonization efforts and digital tracking systems, aligning with green logistics trends.

+ CROWLEY MARITIME [3 News That Matters]

+ CROWLEY MARITIME ADVANCES ALASKA GROWTH WITH PORT MCKENZIE FUEL TERMINAL FEASIBILITY

Crowley Maritime has announced a plan to conduct a feasibility study for a potential fuel terminal at Port McKenzie, signaling a broader push to expand its Alaska footprint. The permit was granted on January 7, 2026, as rising regional activity underscores demand for improved logistics and transportation capabilities.Officials say the terminal would provide a reliable fuel-supply chain for local marine and land operations, supporting growing commercial activity in the Matanuska-Susitna Borough and the broader Alaska economy.Crowley's strategy centers on leveraging Port McKenzie's location to enhance logistics and attract other tenants. If developed, the project could streamline Crowley's operations and help catalyze additional investment in the area.With the permit in place, Crowley moves forward with feasibility assessments and engagement with local stakeholders, marking a potential milestone for the company and for Port McKenzie as they explore future growth opportunities.

+ CROWLEY MARITIME ANNOUNCES DUAL-DIVISION REALIGNMENT TO ENHANCE OPERATIONAL AGILITY

Crowley Maritime announced on January 6, 2026 the formation of two new divisions—Shipping and Logistics led by James C. Fowler, and Energy led by Kollin Fencil—as part of a strategic consolidation to improve execution and accelerate growth across its shipping, logistics and energy activities.The Shipping and Logistics Division combines Crowley's logistics, land transportation and global ship management to serve commercial and government customers with ocean shipping, inland trucking, multimodal transportation and warehousing.Fowler said the reorganized structure will enable cohesive management of the entire transportation value chain, driving growth through safe and efficient supply-chain solutions.The Energy Division consolidates Crowley Fuels in Alaska, LNG services and advanced energy solutions, enabling greater innovation and scale in both traditional and emerging energy markets.Fencil noted the realignment will help Crowley meet evolving customer demands for traditional fuels and advanced energy solutions, and the leaders have delivered improvements in financial performance and operational efficiency since joining the company in 2023. Chief Operating Officer Megan Davidson said the realignment will speed decision-making and improve the company's ability to respond to market dynamics, reinforcing Crowley's status as a leader in maritime, energy and logistics. Crowley is privately held and U.S.-owned, with a footprint spanning 36 nations and territories and more than 130 years in business.

+ FAIRWATER HOLDINGS EMERGES AS NEW JONES ACT SERVICE PROVIDER FROM CROWLEY-SEACOR ALLIANCE

Crowley Maritime and SEACOR Holdings have created Fairwater Holdings, a new joint venture intended to integrate their liquid energy and chemical transportation assets and to serve as an independent U.S. Jones Act service provider.The venture will combine the strengths of Crowley and SEACOR to offer transportation solutions across the petroleum and chemical trades, plus third-party ship management.Fairwater will manage a fleet of 20 ocean-going articulated tug-barges and 11 tankers, largely under long-term charters, and will provide crewing and technical management for an additional 21 vessels owned by third parties.Daniel Thorogood, currently CEO of Seabulk, will take the helm as CEO of Fairwater once the deal closes.Crowley's Tom Crowley described Seabulk as an ideal partner with a proven track record, adding the venture supports Crowley's growth strategy and strengthens its position as the largest American-flag petroleum and chemical tank vessel fleet, with capacity exceeding 12 million barrels.The companies expect to begin operations after the customary closing conditions and regulatory approvals, with Fairwater slated to commence in the first quarter of 2024.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ CMA CGM LOGISTICS [3 News That Matters]

+ CMA CGM EXPANDS INDIAN FLAG REGISTRY WITH FIFTH VESSEL

MUMBAI: CMA CGM Group announced the reflagging of its fifth container vessel in India, saying the move will streamline services on key routes. The company noted the reflagging occurred on the 24th of April 2026, reinforcing its long-term commitment to India.The reflagging changes the Vila do Conde's registration to the Indian flag and involves a 2,592 TEU capacity vessel.This effort fits a broader government push to grow an Indian-flag fleet, with Prime Minister Modi emphasizing self-sufficiency in shipping.CMA CGM has already reflagged five vessels since last year and plans a sixth by the end of May.Earlier this year, the company ordered six dual-fuel vessels from Cochin Shipyard for about $350 million and announced a planned $300 million investment in Maharashtra's logistics infrastructure. Executives are also exploring inland waterways and proposed using Gujarat International Finance Tec-City, or GIFT City, as a vessel-registration hub to strengthen maritime security and competitiveness.

+ CMACGM'S GEMALINK EXPANSION SET TO DOUBLE VIETNAM PORT CAPACITY BY 2027

CMA CGM Group has announced Phase 2 of the Gemalink terminal expansion in Cai Mep port, southern Vietnam, with the work beginning on 20th April 2026. The move reinforces CMA CGM's commitment to expanding port capacity in Southeast Asia and supporting robust, sustainable supply chains in the region. When finished, capacity will nearly double from 1.7 million TEUs to around 3 million TEUs, with full operation expected by 2027. The plan adds 450 meters of quay in two segments, expands the container yard from 32 to 44 hectares, and introduces five additional Ship-to-Shore cranes, bringing the total to thirteen. Gemalink has quickly established itself as one of the region's most efficient terminals since its 2021 opening, currently operating at full capacity and handling 1.7 million TEUs annually. Its location along key trade lanes makes it a strategic hub within CMACGM's network in Vung Tau. Christine Cabau Woehrel, CMA CGM Group Executive Vice President, said the expansion reflects a long-term partnership with Gemadept and aligns with Vietnam's strategy to become a leading maritime logistics hub in Southeast Asia by 2050.

+ CMA CGM ENHANCES TRANSATLANTIC OFFERING WITH DIRECT CORK CALLS TO NEW YORK AND SAVANNAH

CMA CGM Group has announced enhancements to its Transatlantic PAD service, adding Cork as a direct weekly link to New York and Savannah, effective 28th April 2026.The changes include deployment of an additional vessel and optimized port rotations to improve schedule reliability and overall predictability for supply chains relying on these routes.The PAD service now features direct calls at Dunkerque and Zeebrugge, a distinguishing feature not offered by other services, and connects with CMA CGM's Northern Europe network including Le Havre and Rotterdam to facilitate feedering to Baltic and Scandinavian markets.In the United States, the service benefits from CMA CGM Port Liberty terminals, providing priority berthing to help relieve port congestion and bolster service reliability.The PAD service is capable of handling pharmaceuticals, beverages, and general cargo, appealing to time sensitive and high value shipments and offering a competitive alternative to air freight. The Cork addition strengthens Ireland's access to major U.S. markets, complementing CMA CGM's two-decade presence in Ireland across three ports and up to six weekly sailings, linking the country to more than 420 ports worldwide and supporting key sectors such as pharmaceuticals, agri-food, and manufacturing.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

SECTION 1 RECENT NEWS

Fri Apr 24, 2026: Duke Energy Secures 20-Year License for Robinson Nuclear Plant

Duke Energy Corporation has received a significant boost with the U.S. Nuclear Regulatory Commission's (NRC) decision to grant a 20-year extension for the operating license of its Robinson Nuclear Plant. This renewal allows the facility to continue producing energy until the year 2050, a crucial development for the utility giant. The announcement was made on April 23rd, 2026, and signifies Duke Energy's commitment to providing stable and sustainable energy to the region.

The Robinson Nuclear Plant, located in Hartsville, South Carolina, plays a vital role in Duke Energy's operations, supplying electricity to approximately 570,000 households. This license extension not only secures the plant's operational future but also aligns with Duke Energy's broader strategy of maintaining a diverse energy portfolio. The company is focused on integrating nuclear, natural gas, and renewable energy sources to meet the growing energy demands of its 8 million customers across various states, including the Carolinas, Indiana, Florida, Ohio, and Kentucky.

Duke Energy operates within the regulated utilities sector, boasting a market capitalization of approximately $99.64 billion. The company is segmented into electric utilities and infrastructure, gas utilities and infrastructure, and commercial renewables. Currently, the company's P/E ratio stands at 20.26, which is close to its five-year median, indicating that the stock may be fairly valued in the current market context. Investors are encouraged to consider this valuation alongside other financial metrics when evaluating the stock's potential.

The GF Score(TradeMark) for Duke Energy is 77 out of 100, reflecting strong performance across key metrics that can lead to higher long-term returns. The company's profitability rank is notably strong at 7 out of 10, underscoring its operational efficiency. However, concerns regarding financial strength persist, with a rating of just 3 out of 10, suggesting potential issues related to debt levels and overall financial health. Investors should carefully weigh these factors when considering Duke Energy as part of their investment portfolio.

In recent months, insider trading activity has raised eyebrows, with insiders selling $5.6 million worth of shares and no recorded insider buying. This trend may lead to questions about insider confidence in the company's future performance. Overall, Duke Energy presents a mixed outlook for investors. While the recent license renewal for the Robinson Nuclear Plant strengthens its operational foundation, its financial challenges could impact long-term growth. Investors are encouraged to explore further insights about Duke Energy and its current market position.

Recent News 2 of 3, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 271 words

Wed Apr 22, 2026: Duke Energy Honored with Arbor Day Foundation’s Tree Line USA Award for 20th Year

Duke Energy Corporation has received national recognition for its tree care practices for the 20th consecutive year. The Arbor Day Foundation named Duke Energy Florida a 'Tree Line USA' utility, citing the company's rigorous tree pruning standards, comprehensive employee training, and public education efforts aimed at reducing power outages and safeguarding electrical infrastructure.

On 22nd April 2026, the Arbor Day Foundation lauded Duke Energy for meeting industry standards for tree care, supporting tree planting initiatives, and engaging in annual arbor education programs. The designation reflects Duke Energy's commitment to sustainable practices, as well as its proactive approach to maintaining the health of urban trees in proximity to electrical lines.

One of Duke Energy's ongoing initiatives includes an annual free tree giveaway for customers through the Arbor Day Foundation's Energy-Saving Trees program. In 2026, the company distributed 1,000 trees to Florida customers, bringing the total number of free trees given away statewide to over 16,000 since 2017. These trees are shipped directly to customers with detailed planting instructions designed to encourage proper placement away from power lines.

Company officials noted that proper tree placement is essential for conserving energy and minimizing the risk of outages during storm and hurricane seasons. Duke Energy also conducts year-round vegetation maintenance near power lines and ramps up these efforts ahead of hurricane season, ensuring a reliable power supply for its customers.

Additionally, Duke Energy reminds customers not to trim trees near power lines themselves and encourages them to contact Sunshine 811 before digging near underground utilities. More information about responsible tree planting near electrical equipment can be found through Duke Energy's Right Tree Right Place program.

Recent News 3 of 3, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 395 words

Wed Apr 22, 2026: Duke Energy Foundation Launches America250 Grants for South Carolina Green Spaces

GREENVILLE, S.C.– To strengthen communities across South Carolina as the nation approaches its 250th anniversary, Duke Energy Foundation is awarding $275,000 to nonprofit organizations through a first round of funding as part of its America250 initiative. This funding, announced on 22nd April 2026, celebrates Earth Day and aims to enhance local parks, tree planting efforts, and trail maintenance projects throughout the Palmetto State.

The grants, which form part of a total investment exceeding $550,000 in South Carolina, are aimed at revitalizing parks and shared green spaces, supporting tree planting, and litter prevention initiatives. "As we look back on our country's 250-year anniversary, it's an opportunity to reflect on our nation's history while working to support the next generation," said Tim Pearson, Duke Energy South Carolina president. "We are proud to partner with local organizations who can help care for the Palmetto State now, so future generations can enjoy its beauty."

Duke Energy Foundation has awarded a total of 11 grants in this first round of funding. Notable recipients include the South Carolina Wildlife Federation, Foothills Trail Conservancy, and the Upstate Greenways & Trails Alliance. Each organization is expected to utilize the funding to enhance local environments and foster community engagement in conservation efforts. For instance, Upstate Greenways & Trails Alliance plans to create a new trail that highlights the scenic beauty of Lake Keowee while educating visitors about the Revolutionary War history in the area.

The positive responses from grant recipients highlight the importance of such initiatives. Matt Flege, Executive Director of Upstate Greenways & Trails Alliance, expressed gratitude for the support, stating, "The America250 grant will allow us to create a new trail showcasing the beauty of Lake Keowee to South Carolina residents and visitors alike." Similarly, Susan H. Wild, Chair of Sumter Green, emphasized how the investment strengthens their commitment to beautifying the community, ensuring that efforts made today will benefit future generations.

These grants mark the first awards under Duke Energy Foundation's America250 initiative, which represents a commitment of over $1 million towards community-driven projects across the company's six-state service territories. Additional grant recipients will be announced later this spring, including initiatives focused on veterans' workforce pathways and enhancing access to history and civics education. Duke Energy Foundation continues to provide nearly $30 million annually in philanthropic support to the communities it serves, reflecting its dedication to environmental stewardship and community development.

SECTION 2 TODAY'S BULLISH SIGNALS

2.1 Uptrend

Today's Volatility:

The 5% discount to 12-month high of $134.49 against the 19% premium to 12-month low of $107.42 suggests the stock continues to push for new highs.

Price/Moving Average Price of 1.04:

- The Price/MAP 200 for Duke Energy Corporation is 1.04. Being higher than 1 is a bullish indicator. The stock is trading above both its MAPs and the 50-day MAP of $133.05 is higher than the 200-day MAP of $122.54, a second bullish indicator. The 200-day MAP has increased to $122.54, a third bullish indicator.

Past Quarter:

- In the last three months the stock has hit a new 52-week high twelve times, pointing to a significant uptrend.

2.2 Other Bullish Signals

- Return on Equity of 9.0% versus sector average of 5.8%.

MCap/Total Assets:

- Tobin's Q Ratio, defined as MCap divided by Total Assets, is 0.5. Compared with the rest of the market the stock is undervalued.

- Net profit margin has averaged 14.4% in the last 3 years. This is better than the sector average of 11.8% and suggests a high margin of safety.

- As per the Du Pont analysis, Return on Equity of 9.0% is better than sector average of 5.8%. This is computed as net profit margin of 15.2% times asset turnover [sales/assets] of 0.16 times leverage factor [total assets/shareholders' equity] of 3.6.

Source: Duke Energy Corporation's, 10-K filing dated February 27, 2025, available at the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission website: https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/17797/000132616025000072/duk-20241231.htm and Duke Energy Corporation's website: https://investors.duke-energy.com/overview/default.aspx. company (Duke Energy Corporation), Ticker(DUK)

Source: New York Stock Exchange: https://www.nasdaq.com/market-activity/stocks/DUK, company (Duke Energy Corporation), Ticker(DUK)

SECTION 3 ONGOING BULLISH PARAMETERS

3.1 Rule of 40

The stock scores a favorable score exceeding 40 when using the Rule of 40 (Revenue Growth plus EBITDA margin). Y.o.y revenue growth of 4%, EBITDA margin is 36.2% and the sum of the two 40.7% needs to exceed 40%.

Source: Duke Energy Corporation's, 10-K filing dated February 27, 2025, available at the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission website: https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/17797/000132616025000072/duk-20241231.htm and Duke Energy Corporation's website: https://investors.duke-energy.com/overview/default.aspx. company (Duke Energy Corporation), Ticker(DUK)

3.2 Created Market Value [CMV] past 17 yrs of $78.6 billion

- Market Capitalization has increased by $80.7 billion from $18.4 billion to $99.1 billion in the last 17 years. This increase comprises cumulative retained earnings (RETE) of $2 billion and Created Market Value of $78.6 billion. The Created Market Value multiple, defined by the change in MCap for every $1 of retained earnings is exemplary at $39.68.

3.3 Satisfies four criteria of Benjamin Graham

- The P/E of 22.3 is 0.4 times the highest average P/E of 57.7 in the last five years. This is a value criterion, according to Benjamin Graham who described as a value criterion "A P/E ratio down to less than four-tenth of the highest average P/E ratio the stock attained in the most recent five years".

-""Earnings growth over the most recent ten years of 7% compounded - that is a doubling of earnings in a five-year period""; the earnings per share have more than doubled in the last 5 years.

-""Stability of growth in earnings over the last 5 years, defined as no more than two declines of 5% or greater in year-end earnings""; there have been 2 declines in earnings in the last 5 years.

Source: Duke Energy Corporation's, 10-K filing dated February 27, 2025, available at the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission website: https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/17797/000132616025000072/duk-20241231.htm and Duke Energy Corporation's website: https://investors.duke-energy.com/overview/default.aspx. company (Duke Energy Corporation), Ticker(DUK)

3.4 Satisfies 5 out of 9 criteria of Joseph Piotroski [pass mark 5]:

- Positive net income.

- Positive operating cashflow.

- Return on Assets improvement [from 2.4% to 2.5%].

- Good quality of earnings [operating cashflow exceeds net income].

- Improvement in gross margin [from 98.0% to 98.1%].

But does not meet the following 4 criteria of Joseph Piotroski:

- Improvement in long-term debt to total assets.

- Improvement in current ratio.

- Total shares on issue unchanged (or reduction in total shares on issue).

- Improvement in asset turnover.

Source: Duke Energy Corporation's, 10-K filing dated February 27, 2025, available at the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission website: https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/17797/000132616025000072/duk-20241231.htm and Duke Energy Corporation's website: https://investors.duke-energy.com/overview/default.aspx. company (Duke Energy Corporation), Ticker(DUK)

SECTION 5 PEER COMPARISON & RANKING OF DUK

5.1 LONG-TERM FUNDAMENTAL RANKING: 3 OUT OF 5 [5 is best]

Duke Energy Corporation is ranked number 1 out of 32 listed utility companies in the United States with a market capitalization of $99.1 billion.

Within its sector it has a relatively low P/E of 22.3 and low P/E/G of 0.36.

It has a moderate relative ROE of 9.0% and ROA of 2.5%. The company paid a dividend of $3.19 in the last twelve months. The dividend yield is moderate at 3.3%. Finally, its earnings growth in the past 12 months has been a comparatively high 61.3%.

Stocks are scored on a set of parameters reflecting fundamental analytical tools involving valuation, size and financial performance. They are ranked according to the average values of those parameters. The highest ranking is 5 and the lowest ranking is 1.

Fri Apr 24, 2026: Duke Energy Secures 20-Year License for Robinson Nuclear Plant

Duke Energy Corporation has received a significant boost with the U.S. Nuclear Regulatory Commission's (NRC) decision to grant a 20-year extension for the operating license of its Robinson Nuclear Plant. This renewal allows the facility to continue producing energy until the year 2050, a crucial development for the utility giant. The announcement was made on April 23rd, 2026, and signifies Duke Energy's commitment to providing stable and sustainable energy to the region.

The Robinson Nuclear Plant, located in Hartsville, South Carolina, plays a vital role in Duke Energy's operations, supplying electricity to approximately 570,000 households. This license extension not only secures the plant's operational future but also aligns with Duke Energy's broader strategy of maintaining a diverse energy portfolio. The company is focused on integrating nuclear, natural gas, and renewable energy sources to meet the growing energy demands of its 8 million customers across various states, including the Carolinas, Indiana, Florida, Ohio, and Kentucky.

Duke Energy operates within the regulated utilities sector, boasting a market capitalization of approximately $99.64 billion. The company is segmented into electric utilities and infrastructure, gas utilities and infrastructure, and commercial renewables. Currently, the company's P/E ratio stands at 20.26, which is close to its five-year median, indicating that the stock may be fairly valued in the current market context. Investors are encouraged to consider this valuation alongside other financial metrics when evaluating the stock's potential.

The GF Score(TradeMark) for Duke Energy is 77 out of 100, reflecting strong performance across key metrics that can lead to higher long-term returns. The company's profitability rank is notably strong at 7 out of 10, underscoring its operational efficiency. However, concerns regarding financial strength persist, with a rating of just 3 out of 10, suggesting potential issues related to debt levels and overall financial health. Investors should carefully weigh these factors when considering Duke Energy as part of their investment portfolio.

In recent months, insider trading activity has raised eyebrows, with insiders selling $5.6 million worth of shares and no recorded insider buying. This trend may lead to questions about insider confidence in the company's future performance. Overall, Duke Energy presents a mixed outlook for investors. While the recent license renewal for the Robinson Nuclear Plant strengthens its operational foundation, its financial challenges could impact long-term growth. Investors are encouraged to explore further insights about Duke Energy and its current market position.

Recent News 2 of 3, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 395 words

Wed Apr 22, 2026: Duke Energy Foundation Launches America250 Grants for South Carolina Green Spaces

GREENVILLE, S.C.– To strengthen communities across South Carolina as the nation approaches its 250th anniversary, Duke Energy Foundation is awarding $275,000 to nonprofit organizations through a first round of funding as part of its America250 initiative. This funding, announced on 22nd April 2026, celebrates Earth Day and aims to enhance local parks, tree planting efforts, and trail maintenance projects throughout the Palmetto State.

The grants, which form part of a total investment exceeding $550,000 in South Carolina, are aimed at revitalizing parks and shared green spaces, supporting tree planting, and litter prevention initiatives. "As we look back on our country's 250-year anniversary, it's an opportunity to reflect on our nation's history while working to support the next generation," said Tim Pearson, Duke Energy South Carolina president. "We are proud to partner with local organizations who can help care for the Palmetto State now, so future generations can enjoy its beauty."

Duke Energy Foundation has awarded a total of 11 grants in this first round of funding. Notable recipients include the South Carolina Wildlife Federation, Foothills Trail Conservancy, and the Upstate Greenways & Trails Alliance. Each organization is expected to utilize the funding to enhance local environments and foster community engagement in conservation efforts. For instance, Upstate Greenways & Trails Alliance plans to create a new trail that highlights the scenic beauty of Lake Keowee while educating visitors about the Revolutionary War history in the area.

The positive responses from grant recipients highlight the importance of such initiatives. Matt Flege, Executive Director of Upstate Greenways & Trails Alliance, expressed gratitude for the support, stating, "The America250 grant will allow us to create a new trail showcasing the beauty of Lake Keowee to South Carolina residents and visitors alike." Similarly, Susan H. Wild, Chair of Sumter Green, emphasized how the investment strengthens their commitment to beautifying the community, ensuring that efforts made today will benefit future generations.

These grants mark the first awards under Duke Energy Foundation's America250 initiative, which represents a commitment of over $1 million towards community-driven projects across the company's six-state service territories. Additional grant recipients will be announced later this spring, including initiatives focused on veterans' workforce pathways and enhancing access to history and civics education. Duke Energy Foundation continues to provide nearly $30 million annually in philanthropic support to the communities it serves, reflecting its dedication to environmental stewardship and community development.

Recent News 3 of 3, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 271 words

Wed Apr 22, 2026: Duke Energy Honored with Arbor Day Foundation’s Tree Line USA Award for 20th Year

Duke Energy Corporation has received national recognition for its tree care practices for the 20th consecutive year. The Arbor Day Foundation named Duke Energy Florida a 'Tree Line USA' utility, citing the company's rigorous tree pruning standards, comprehensive employee training, and public education efforts aimed at reducing power outages and safeguarding electrical infrastructure.

On 22nd April 2026, the Arbor Day Foundation lauded Duke Energy for meeting industry standards for tree care, supporting tree planting initiatives, and engaging in annual arbor education programs. The designation reflects Duke Energy's commitment to sustainable practices, as well as its proactive approach to maintaining the health of urban trees in proximity to electrical lines.

One of Duke Energy's ongoing initiatives includes an annual free tree giveaway for customers through the Arbor Day Foundation's Energy-Saving Trees program. In 2026, the company distributed 1,000 trees to Florida customers, bringing the total number of free trees given away statewide to over 16,000 since 2017. These trees are shipped directly to customers with detailed planting instructions designed to encourage proper placement away from power lines.

Company officials noted that proper tree placement is essential for conserving energy and minimizing the risk of outages during storm and hurricane seasons. Duke Energy also conducts year-round vegetation maintenance near power lines and ramps up these efforts ahead of hurricane season, ensuring a reliable power supply for its customers.

Additionally, Duke Energy reminds customers not to trim trees near power lines themselves and encourages them to contact Sunshine 811 before digging near underground utilities. More information about responsible tree planting near electrical equipment can be found through Duke Energy's Right Tree Right Place program.

3.3 Satisfies four criteria of Benjamin Graham

- The P/E of 22.3 is 0.4 times the highest average P/E of 57.7 in the last five years. This is a value criterion, according to Benjamin Graham who described as a value criterion "A P/E ratio down to less than four-tenth of the highest average P/E ratio the stock attained in the most recent five years".

-""Earnings growth over the most recent ten years of 7% compounded - that is a doubling of earnings in a five-year period""; the earnings per share have more than doubled in the last 5 years.

-""Stability of growth in earnings over the last 5 years, defined as no more than two declines of 5% or greater in year-end earnings""; there have been 2 declines in earnings in the last 5 years.
