value.Title: FORTRESS INVESTMENT GROUP LLC - RECENT DEVELOPMENTS: KKR & CO. INC. AND PEERS

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JH4-65J3-RRKY-D3TF-00000-00FORTRESS INVESTMENT GROUP LLC - RECENT DEVELOPMENTS: KKR & CO. INC. AND PEERS2026-05-04T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:46:15ZLexisNexisSTOCKFORTRESS INVESTMENT GROUP LLC - RECENT DEVELOPMENTS: KKR & CO. INC. AND PEERS

DASHBOARD: FORTRESS INVESTMENT GROUP LLC

PermID4295901538Websitehttps://www.fortress.com/IndustryAlternative InvestmentsAddress1345 Avenue OF The Americas Florida 46 New York, NY, 10105-4302 United States

ACTIVITIES:

Fortress Investment Group LLC, founded in 1998, is a global investment firm headquartered in New York City. Employing over 900 people, it manages $48 billion in assets across private equity, credit, and real estate. Acquired by SoftBank in 2017, Fortress operates independently, focusing on distressed assets and infrastructure. In 2024, it invested $2 billion in renewable energy projects. Its culture emphasizes risk management, innovation, and agility, with training in asset valuation and fintech. Competing with Apollo Global, Fortress stands out for its credit expertise and opportunistic investments. Recent initiatives include blockchain-based real estate funds and ESG-focused portfolios, aligning with investment trends. Fortress diversified strategy ensures resilience in volatile markets.

+ FORTRESS INVESTMENT GROUP LLC [3 News That Matters]

+ PURE SALMON JAPAN ADVANCES FLAGSHIP LAND-BASED FACILITY AS FORTRESS COMMITS USD 180 MILLION

Pure Salmon Japan secured over USD 180 million in preferred-note financing led by Fortress Investment Group affiliates. The deal closed on 1st April 2026 and will fund the build-out of the company’s land-based salmon facility in Japan.The investment signals robust institutional support for a project targeting 10,000 tonnes of annual production and aiming to become Japan’s largest land-based salmon operation. The facility will implement recirculating aquaculture system (RAS) technology to help reduce imports and strengthen domestic seafood supply.CEO Erol Emed commented, "We are deeply encouraged by the dedication and conviction shown by our investors in supporting Pure Salmon Japan through this financing. Their commitment underscores the growing relevance of sustainable domestic seafood production in Japan." Fortress Investment Group, with a long-standing presence in Japan, brings both capital and strategic know-how. Eric Golden, Co-Head of Asia at Fortress, highlighted the project’s role in enhancing Japan's food production infrastructure.

+ FORTRESS INVESTMENT GROUP EXPANDS PALM COAST SENIOR HOUSING WITH TUSCAN GARDENS ACQUISITION

Fortress Investment Group LLC has announced the acquisition of Tuscan Gardens of Palm Coast, a Class A seniors housing community in Palm Coast, Florida, with the deal dated 12th March 2026.Tuscan Gardens has 130 units and offers a full continuum of care, including assisted living, enhanced assisted living and memory care, on an 11-acre site about one mile from the Atlantic Ocean.Established in 2018, the Palm Coast community serves an affluent and growing senior population along Florida's northeast coast, and occupancy has improved under AgeWell Solvere Living since 2023.Fortress notes the asset's location and quality position it for long-term growth in the senior housing sector, aligning with the firm's strategy to invest in well-located properties.

+ FORTRESS CLOSES $96 MILLION LOAN TO SUPPORT JASPER'S CLASS A APARTMENT COMMUNITY IN DOWNTOWN LOS ANGELES

Fortress Investment Group closed a $96 million whole loan for Jasper, a 296-unit Class A multifamily community in DowntownLos Angeles. The financing was finalized on Feb. 9, 2026, as Fortress continues to back high-quality urban housing developments near major university campuses.Jasper is developed by Cityview and situated near the University of Southern California. The property comprises studios, and one- and two-bedroom units with upscale finishes and a broad amenity package that includes affordable housing units. Proceeds will refinance construction debt and support leasing progress for the new asset.Cityview's Damian Gancman praised Fortress for a smooth, solution-driven process and seamless execution from start to finish. Fortress's Noah Shore emphasized Jasper's fit with the firm's focus on backing well-capitalized, newly built multifamily projects with experienced sponsors, reflecting Fortress's broader investment approach.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ KKR & CO [3 News That Matters]

+ KKR & CO. INC. (NYSE:KKR) TIES WITH ALTERRA TO EXPAND CLIMATE TRANSITION FUNDING

KKR & Co. Inc. (NYSE:KKR) Ties With ALTERRA to Expand Climate Transition FundingKKR & Co. Inc. (NYSE:KKR) partners with ALTERRA, the UAE’s $30 billion blended finance vehicle, to support its climate transition strategy. The announcement was made on Apr. 22, 2026. ALTERRA launched at COP28 and aims to mobilize up to $250 billion in climate finance by 2030, with a significant portion directed to developing markets. ALTERRA already counts BlackRock, Brookfield Asset Management and TPG as partners.KKR’s climate strategy, co-managed by Charlie Gailliot and Emmanuel Lagarrigue, covers renewables, energy efficiency and sustainable fuels. KKR manages over $100 billion in assets and has invested more than $44 billion in climate and environmental sustainability projects since 2011. Notable investments include Zenobe, EGC, Avantus and IGNIS P2X. The collaboration with ALTERRA could help scale technologies and improve energy security and decarbonization.Gailliot and Lagarrigue see strong potential to combine ALTERRA’s reach with KKR’s infrastructure know-how to advance decarbonization across sectors.

+ KKR & CO. INC. (NYSE:KKR) FUNDS STARITAS AS IT BECOMES STANDALONE GROWTH VEHICLE

Apr. 22, 2026 — KKR & Co. Inc. (NYSE:KKR) Invests in Staritas through Accel-KKR to support the spin-out from ECR as it pursues growth opportunities in digital technology markets. Staritas will operate as an independent company, receiving resources and guidance from Accel-KKR and KKR to accelerate development and market entry in the digital space. The move highlights investor interest in technology-driven firms able to adapt quickly and scale within competitive markets.

+ KKR & CO. INC. (NYSE:KKR) APPOINTS JOEL TRAUT TO LEAD GLOBAL REAL ESTATE CREDIT

KKR & Co. Inc. (NYSE:KKR) Appoints Joel Traut to Lead Global Real Estate CreditTraut takes on the role of global head of direct lending within KKR's real estate division, effective Apr. 20, 2026, as the firm scales its real estate credit platform. Traut has led the unit since 2015, growing it from a seed investment of $400 million to nearly $45 billion in assets under management by end-2025.Traut's leadership comes as KKR invests nearly $15 billion annually across loans and securities in the U.S. and Europe and expands its European real estate credit business with support from Ali Imraan and a dedicated team of 25 investment and asset management professionals.Paul Fine is named head of originations, succeeding Traut. Fine, a former managing director, will work to deepen client relationships and widen KKR's capital pools. Since 2017 the firm has expanded its capital pools from two to eight, including the $850 million closed for the KKR Opportunistic Real Estate Credit Fund II in February 2025.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ CARLYLE GROUP [3 News That Matters]

+ CARLYLE GROUP L.P. (NASDAQ:CG) APPOINTS TRIO AS CO-PRESIDENTS TO DRIVE UNIFIED DECISION-MAKING

Carlyle Group L.P. (NASDAQ:CG) appoints three senior executives as co-presidents under Chief Executive Officer Harvey Schwartz, effective after 22nd April 2026, to accelerate decision-making and align operations with its evolving strategy.The co-presidents will share oversight of investment priorities, fundraising, and day-to-day management across Carlyle Group L.P. (NASDAQ:CG)'s businesses, with the goal of improving execution as market conditions shift.Schwartz, who assumed leadership during a transitional period, seeks clearer accountability and faster decisions, a model that aligns with practices at other major firms to maintain momentum.The firm will assess the impact on investment activity and financial performance in forthcoming quarters.

+ CARLYLE STRENGTHENS WEALTH-MANAGEMENT FOOTPRINT WITH MAI CAPITAL ACQUISITION

Carlyle Group is set to expand its wealth-management footprint by acquiring a majority stake in MAI Capital Management from Galway Holdings, Harvest Partners and Oak Hill Capital, valuing MAI at over $2.8 billion. The deal was announced on 31st March 2026.As part of the agreement, MAI employees will retain a meaningful minority stake, keeping MAI independent under Rick Buoncore, who leads as chairman and chief executive.The investment aims to bolster MAI's capital base and growth in financial planning, investment management and family-office services. MAI reports $72.6 billion in assets under management or administration as of Jan 1 across 40 U.S. offices.Closing is expected in the second quarter of 2026, subject to standard regulatory approvals, with Carlyle leveraging its global platform to support continuity for clients and advisors.

+ MAI CAPITAL TO SELL MAJORITY OWNERSHIP TO CARLYLE FOR ABOUT $2.8 BILLION

Apr. 1, 2026 — Carlyle Group L.P. has reached an agreement to acquire a majority stake in MAI Capital Management, valuing MAI at more than $2.8 billion. The move will give Carlyle majority ownership as Galway Holdings, Harvest Partners and Oak Hill Capital exit, while MAI employees retain a significant minority stake. Carlyle had previously invested in MAI in 2021 via Galway Holdings. As of Jan. 1, 2026, MAI oversees $72.6 billion in assets, operates 40 U.S. offices and employs over 700 people. The deal underscores Carlyle's continued expansion even as its stock has been volatile; the firm reports strong liquidity and a 15-year dividend history at about 3%. Analysts rate Carlyle’s overall financial health as FAIR. Rick Buoncore, MAI’s chairman and CEO, said, "As we looked for a long-term partner to help us achieve that vision, Carlyle stood out for its alignment with our culture and values along with their deep industry knowledge and expertise." The transaction is expected to close in the second quarter of 2026, subject to customary closing conditions and regulatory approvals, including consent from the Division of Banking in South Dakota. Harvest Partners, MAI’s former sponsor, added, "We are confident that MAI is well positioned for the next stage of growth with Carlyle’s backing."

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ OAKTREE CAPITAL MANAGEMENT LP [3 News That Matters]

+ BIOXCEL THERAPEUTICS ANNOUNCES NINTH AMENDMENT TO CREDIT AGREEMENT AND NEW WARRANT ISSUANCE

BioXcel Therapeutics, Inc. said it has expanded its financing framework through a Fourth Amended and Restated Registration Rights Agreement with Oaktree Capital Management and other institutional holders. The new agreement, disclosed in a Form 8-K, was announced with the filing date of 18th April 2026 and encompasses changes to prior terms as well as the issuance of additional equity warrants to lenders, reflecting the company’s ongoing capital management strategy. Separately, the Ninth Amendment to BioXcel's Credit Agreement was executed on 27th March 2026, and on 15th April 2026 the company issued unregistered warrants in a private placement. The warrants give lenders the right to acquire more BioXcel common stock, and the private placement means the warrants were not registered under the Securities Act of 1933. BioXcel also stated it will file registration statements with the SEC to enable potential resale of the underlying shares, a move intended to provide liquidity and investor transparency. Lenders have said they will hold the securities for investment purposes, which could reduce immediate selling pressure but may still lead to a higher total share count if the warrants are exercised and the shares registered for resale. The company notes it is not an emerging growth company and will continue to comply with full regulatory reporting. With participation from multiple Oaktree entities, the deal illustrates a continued commitment to securities compliance and to monitoring upcoming filings and announcements that could influence BioXcel’s stock performance.

+ OAKTREE, NORTH MILL EXECUTE $355.8 MILLION ASSET-BACKED SECURITIZATION

Oaktree Capital Management and North Mill Equipment Finance have closed a $355.8 million asset-backed securitization, OAKEF 2026-1, on 8th April 2026, reflecting healthy investor demand for quality equipment finance assets.The securitized assets originated from Midland Equipment Finance in a November 2025 deal. The notes were rated AAA through A and drew strong demand, about 6.3 times subscriptions, while delivering a weighted average spread of 99 basis points and a 4.7% yield.Truist Securities led structuring with Deutsche Bank as co-manager. Brendan Beer of Oaktree noted the market reception as evidence of investor confidence in the strategy and in North Mill's role.North Mill's David Lee emphasized asset quality and the servicing platform, reiterating that NMEF will continue as the primary servicer for the assets, ensuring ongoing management.

+ OAKTREE NAVIGATES PRIVATE CREDIT PRESSURES BY FULFILLING $7.7B REDEMPTIONS AND CUTTING DIVIDEND

On 29th March 2026, Oaktree Capital Management, a Brookfield Asset Management subsidiary, said it would fulfill all redemption requests from its private credit fund, totaling about $7.7 billion, allowing withdrawals up to 8.5% of net assets.Brookfield will contribute roughly $80 million to support the redemptions.The Oaktree Strategic Credit Fund will repurchase 6.8% of its shares, with Brookfield buying an additional 1.7% from a single investor; the fund's dividend will be cut by two cents to 16 cents.As of 23rd March 2026, liquidity stood at $1.8 billion from cash and undrawn facilities, while the firm has sold publicly traded loans and bonds to reduce exposure to software investments. The broader private credit sector remains under pressure, with peers such as Blackstone raising buyback terms to 7%.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ APOLLO GLOBAL MANAGEMENT [3 News That Matters]

+ MEDICAL SURGICAL SOLUTIONS, LLC AT $9.62 BILLION POST-MONEY VALUATION WITH $1.25 BILLION FROM APOLLO GLOBAL MANAGEMENT

Medical Surgical Solutions, LLC secures $1.25 billion from Apollo Funds, managed by Apollo Global Management, Inc., delivering a 13% stake via convertible preferred shares. The investment was formalized on April 20, 2026 and is contingent on regulatory approvals and customary closing conditions. The post-money valuation stands at about $9.62 billion, and the capital will be used to bolster operations and expand the company’s market footprint as Apollo provides strategic backing through its funds.

+ APOLLO GLOBAL MANAGEMENT LLC (NYSE:APO) REPORTS $1.25 BILLION CONVERTIBLE PREFERRED EQUITY INVESTMENT FOR 13% MCKESSON MEDICAL-SURGICAL SOLUTIONS STAKE

On Apr. 20, 2026, Apollo Global Management LLC (NYSE:APO) Invests $1.25 Billion in McKesson Corporation (NYSE:MCK) for a 13% stake in its Medical-Surgical Solutions division via convertible preferred equity, valuing the unit at approximately $13 billion. The capital supports McKesson's planned separation of the Medical-Surgical Solutions unit and funds strategic initiatives within the business. The transaction reflects a strategic alignment between Apollo and McKesson as the latter moves toward separating its Medical-Surgical Solutions segment and concentrating on core operations. Apollo Global Management oversees about $938.4 billion in assets under management and has a market capitalization near $72.06 billion, with a trailing P/E ratio of 22.58, indicating market expectations for continued growth. Insider activity raises caution, with six insider sells versus one insider buy over the past year, and Apollo's financial strength is rated 6 out of 10. Market participants will watch the arrangement for potential impacts on McKesson's separation plan.

+ APOLLO BROADENS UK HOUSING FOOTPRINT VIA GATEHOUSE LIVING GROUP PURCHASE

Apollo Global Management Inc. has acquired Gatehouse Living Group from Gatehouse Bank, completed on 1st April 2026, bringing Gatehouse Investment Management and Ascend Properties into its UK housing platform.Financial terms were not disclosed.GLG is a Build-to-Rent single-family housing leader with more than 10,000 properties and 5,000 homes acquired, and will continue to be led by CEO Paul Stockwell with Ascend Properties handling external portfolio management.The deal strengthens Apollo's UK strategy and follows its existing interests in Miller Homes and Foundation Home Loans, while Gatehouse Bank focuses on Shariah-compliant financing.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ ARES MANAGEMENT [3 News That Matters]

+ ARES MANAGEMENT (NYSE:ARES) APPOINTS ASIA LEADERS AND ACQUIRES WHITESTONE REIT TO DIVERSIFY REAL ASSETS

Ares Management (NYSE:ARES) to acquire Whitestone REIT in a US$1.7 billion transaction, taking private a retail-focused REIT and expanding essential consumer-focused centers across U.S. metros. On Apr. 14, 2026, E.G. Morse was named Partner and Head of Asia Credit, with Dinesh Goel and Gabriel Fong leading the Asia Special Situations team, underscoring Ares' intent to bolster Asia-focused credit strategies. The Whitestone acquisition broadens Ares' real estate, private credit, and real assets exposure and supports diversification across geographies. Investors should monitor integration progress and Asia fundraising as the leadership changes take shape, while considering risks such as margin pressure and elevated debt; the 5.38% yield is not fully covered by earnings

+ WHITESTONE REIT TO BECOME PRIVATE AS ARES COMPLETES $1.7B ACQUISITION

On Apr. 10, 2026, Ares Management announced plans to acquire Whitestone REIT in an all-cash deal valued at about $1.7 billion, at $19.00 per share or unit. The transaction will render Whitestone private and remove it from the NYSE. Whitestone’s 56 properties total around 4.9 million square feet across Sun Belt markets such as Phoenix, Austin, Dallas–Fort Worth, Houston and San Antonio. The move aims to broaden Ares Real Estate’s footprint with necessity-based retail centers in high-demand Arizona and Texas markets. Whitestone CEO Dave Holeman stated the company’s strategy centers on high-return, smaller spaces with a diversified tenant base, adding that the agreement with Ares validates this approach for shareholders. Ares’ global head of real estate strategy and growth, David Roth, emphasized the appeal of New Economy real estate and convenient consumer experiences, highlighting the growing demand for grocery, pharmacy, healthcare, fitness and dining options.

+ ARES MANAGEMENT DEPLOYS $5.4 BILLION TO ACCELERATE VALUE-ADD REAL ESTATE RECOVERY

Ares Management Corporation has secured about $5.4 billion for value-add real estate investments in the United States and Europe, a move aimed at capitalizing on supply-constrained markets likely to rebound soon, with a focus on logistics, multifamily, and self-storage sectors.The fundraising includes $1.9 billion for the EPEP IV fund and roughly $3.5 billion for the U.S. value-add program, with initial investments identified or deployed totaling around $1.1 billion on each track.Analysts are broadly supportive; about 75% are bullish, and the consensus price target sits near $161, suggesting roughly 60% upside. However, on 24th March 2026, BMO Capital reduced its target to $112 from $140 and issued a Market Perform rating amid concerns about BDC redemptions and broader market volatility.Ares, founded as a global alternative investment manager, had BlackRock as a notable backer with approximately 7.12% stake at end-2025, underscoring strong institutional support. The note also reminds investors that AI-related equities may offer greater upside in some scenarios, reinforcing the case for diversification.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ QATAR AIRWAYS [3 News That Matters]

+ QATAR AIRWAYS CARGO RESTARTS FREIGHTER AND PASSENGER LINKS TO UAE ON EXPANDED NETWORK

Qatar Airways Cargo has resumed both freighter and belly operations to and from Doha after safety concerns. The new schedule confirms freighter flights to Dubai World Central (DWC) twice weekly from 26th April and to Sharjah (SHJ) three times weekly from 1st May, while daily passenger services to Dubai (DXB) and Sharjah (SHJ) also restart, with freighter reach planned to exceed 50 destinations by 1st May and the overall passenger network expected to top 150 by 16th June.Urgent freight restrictions have been lifted on routes such as Victoria, Australia, with the carrier introducing special handling codes QRLIV for fish, mammals and diplomatic cargo, and LHO for live human organs and blood, effective 1st May.Reservations remain accessible via the Digital Lounge and Qatar Airways Cargo sales teams, alongside standard sales channels. The resumption is described as a key milestone for regional logistics and supports ongoing expansion.Industry stakeholders are watching the pace of growth, as the UAE freight market benefits from improved connectivity and service levels.

+ QATAR AIRWAYS INTRODUCES NEXT-GENERATION QSUITE, AIMING TO SUSTAIN PREMIUM EDGE

Qatar Airways announced the Qsuite Next Generation, with a launch planned later this year on the Airbus A350-1000 after delays with the 777-9, as of 23rd April 2026. The Next Generation builds on the iconic Qsuite introduced in 2017, maintaining sliding doors, double beds, and modular seating while adding a movable 4K OLED screen and stronger privacy options. The initiative reinforces Qatar Airways' premium strategy and its Doha hub, where exclusive check-in, fast-track security, and premium lounges contribute to a seamless travel experience. By investing in product development, the airline seeks to attract high-yield corporate travelers and affluent leisure customers as competition in luxury cabins intensifies.

+ QATAR AIRWAYS SETS OUT PHASED EMBARGO LIFTS DURING RECOVERY

Qatar Airways is steadily expanding its operations as it rebuilds its network, with passenger routes restored to more than 150 destinations and freighter coverage over 50 locations as of April 20, 2026.The restoration is part of a wider plan to increase capacity amid rising air freight demand. Some shipments remain embargoed, with AVF, AVM, and DOC handling codes restricted, and live organs and blood not accepted.QRLIV shipments can be booked under different codes, as long as Doha transit time stays within 24 hours. Other products remain bookable via standard sales channels, and the carrier emphasizes adaptability and a commitment to service as it expands.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

urn:contentItem:6JGH-RVD3-RSGT-S02T-00000-00CROWLEY MARITIME - RECENT NEWS UPDATE FROM CMA CGM LOGISTICS SA AND PEERS2026-05-02T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:36:34ZLexisNexisSTOCKCROWLEY MARITIME - RECENT NEWS UPDATE FROM CMA CGM LOGISTICS SA AND PEERS

DASHBOARD: CROWLEY MARITIME

PermID4295904276Websitehttp://www.crowley.com/IndustryMaritime & Shipping Logistics

ACTIVITIES:

Crowley Maritime Corporation, founded in 1892, is a family-owned logistics and maritime services company headquartered in Jacksonville, Florida. Employing over 5,300 people, it operates in shipping, energy, and supply chain management, generating over $2 billion annually. Crowley provides ocean transport, LNG distribution, and logistics for government and commercial clients. In 2024, it expanded renewable energy logistics, supporting offshore wind projects. Its culture emphasizes safety, innovation, and sustainability, with robust employee training. Competing with Maersk, Crowley stands out for its U.S.-focused operations and government contracts. Recent initiatives include decarbonization efforts and digital tracking systems, aligning with green logistics trends.

+ CROWLEY MARITIME [3 News That Matters]

+ CROWLEY MARITIME EXPANDS INTERESTS AT PORT MCKENZIE WITH NEW FUEL TERMINAL STUDY

Crowley Maritime is set to capitalize on the increasing interest surrounding Port McKenzie by securing a permit to study the development of a large fuel terminal. This strategic move could significantly enhance the company's operational footprint in Alaska's Matanuska-Susitna Borough.On the 7th of January 2026, Crowley Maritime announced its plans to conduct a comprehensive study aimed at establishing a fuel terminal at Port McKenzie. The initiative comes amid rising commercial activities and interest in the region, which is poised to benefit from enhanced logistics and transportation capabilities.The proposed fuel terminal at Port McKenzie is expected to play a critical role in supporting local industries and businesses by providing a reliable fuel supply chain. Crowley Maritime's investment in this infrastructure is seen as a response to the growing demand for fuel in both marine and land-based operations in Alaska, a region that has been increasingly recognized for its potential in various sectors.As part of its strategic growth plan, Crowley Maritime is focusing on expanding its services in Alaska, leveraging the advantageous location of Port McKenzie. The terminal is anticipated to not only streamline operations for Crowley but also attract other businesses looking to establish a presence in the region, further enhancing the economic landscape of the Matanuska-Susitna Borough.With the approval of this permit, Crowley Maritime is now positioned to move forward with its feasibility studies and engage with local stakeholders. The development of the fuel terminal could mark a significant milestone for both Crowley and Port McKenzie, potentially paving the way for future investments and growth in the area.

+ CROWLEY MARITIME RESTRUCTURES LEADERSHIP TO DRIVE GROWTH IN SHIPPING AND ENERGY

Crowley Maritime has announced a strategic consolidation of its business structure aimed at enhancing efficiency and value for customers. On 6th January 2026, the company revealed the establishment of two new divisions: Shipping and Logistics, led by Executive Vice President and Division President James C. Fowler, and Energy, overseen by Executive Vice President and Division President Kollin Fencil. This reorganization is designed to improve operational execution and agility, positioning Crowley Maritime for accelerated growth across its shipping, logistics, and energy sectors.The Shipping and Logistics Division, under Fowler's leadership, merges Crowley's logistics, land transportation, and global ship management businesses. This division is set to serve both commercial and government customers, providing a comprehensive array of ocean shipping, inland trucking, multimodal transportation, and warehousing solutions. Fowler emphasized that this new structure will ensure that the entire transportation value chain for their customers is managed cohesively, ultimately driving new growth through safe and efficient supply chain solutions.Meanwhile, the Energy Division, guided by Fencil, consolidates operations including Crowley Fuels in Alaska, liquefied natural gas (LNG) services, and advanced energy solutions. Fencil noted that the division's new structure enables Crowley Maritime to innovate and scale more effectively within traditional and emerging energy markets. With a long history of supporting diverse energy needs across the U.S., the realignment is expected to further enhance Crowley's commitment to meeting evolving customer demands for both traditional fuels and advanced energy solutions.Both Fowler and Fencil bring significant experience to their new roles, having joined Crowley Maritime as senior vice presidents in 2023. Under their respective leadership, the company has seen improvements in financial performance and operational efficiency within their business units. Chief Operating Officer Megan Davidson remarked that this realignment empowers faster decision-making and enhances the company's ability to adapt to market demands, ensuring that Crowley Maritime remains a leader in the maritime, energy, and logistics sectors.Crowley Maritime, a privately held U.S.-owned company, has been providing innovative transportation services for over 130 years. With operations spanning 36 nations and territories, the company is recognized as one of the leading employers of U.S. mariners. As Crowley Maritime embarks on this new chapter, stakeholders will be closely monitoring the impact of these changes on the company's growth trajectory and operational capabilities.

+ CROWLEY AND SEACOR JOIN FORCES IN NEW JONES ACT TANKER VENTURE

Crowley Maritime and SEACOR Holdings, through its subsidiary Seabulk Tankers, have formed a joint venture called Fairwater Holdings to integrate their liquid energy and chemical transportation assets. This collaboration aims to address the evolving demands of the U.S. domestic market. The agreement was finalized on 2nd January 2026, establishing Fairwater Holdings as an independent U.S. Jones Act service provider in the maritime sector.The new entity will amalgamate the strengths of both companies, providing a suite of transportation solutions across the petroleum and chemical trades, alongside third-party ship management services. Fairwater Holdings will manage a fleet comprising 20 ocean-going articulated tug-barges and 11 tankers, primarily under long-term charters. Furthermore, the joint venture will offer crewing and technical management for an additional 21 vessels owned by third parties, thereby enhancing its operational capacity and service offerings.Daniel Thorogood, currently the CEO of Seabulk, will take on the CEO role at Fairwater Holdings upon completion of the deal. With over a decade of experience leading Seabulk and managing SEACOR's short-sea shipping and logistics investments, Thorogood is expected to steer the new venture towards innovation and sustainable practices. In his statement, he emphasized that Fairwater marks a significant milestone in delivering safe and efficient maritime transportation solutions tailored to the needs of the U.S. energy sector.Tom Crowley, Chairman and CEO of Crowley Maritime, expressed confidence in the partnership, describing Seabulk as an ideal collaborator with a proven track record in the maritime industry. He noted that the joint venture aligns with Crowley's growth strategy, further solidifying its position as a leader in the U.S. maritime market, where it operates the largest American-flag petroleum and chemical tank vessel fleet, boasting a capacity exceeding 12 million barrels.As both companies work towards integrating their assets and services, the joint venture is expected to commence operations after meeting customary closing conditions and securing regulatory approvals. This integration effort is anticipated to create a unified and seamless transition for customers, ensuring uninterrupted support for their energy supply chains. Fairwater Holdings is set to begin operations in the first quarter of 2024, marking an exciting new chapter in U.S. maritime transportation.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ CMA CGM LOGISTICS [3 News That Matters]

+ CMA CGM TAKES STRATEGIC STEP WITH FIFTH VESSEL REFLAGGING IN INDIA

MUMBAI: French container shipping line CMA CGM Group announced the reflagging of its fifth container vessel in India, as it looks to enhance its service efficiency for customers across key trade routes. This announcement comes on the 24th of April 2026, marking another significant milestone in CMACGM's ongoing investment and long-term commitment to the country.The reflagging process involves changing the country of registration (flag State) of a merchant ship. CMA CGM has positioned itself as a strong growth partner for India across maritime, logistics, and infrastructure sectors. The reflagging of the 2,592 TEUs capacity vessel, Vila do Conde, underscores this commitment and aligns with the Indian government's broader objectives to increase the number of vessels registered under the Indian flag.Reflagging of vessels is gaining traction in light of the government’s initiative to bolster a larger fleet owned by India. Prime Minister Shri Narendra Modi highlighted the importance of self-sufficiency in shipping last September, citing the ₹6 trillion expenditure to foreign shipping lines in maritime trade. Following this trend, CMACGM has successfully reflagged all five vessels since last year, with plans to flag its sixth vessel in the country by the end of May as part of its expansion strategy.CMA CGM has outlined several strategic initiatives to support India’s maritime ecosystem, recognizing the country’s increasingly significant role in global trade. In February, the company became the first major international carrier to order vessels from an Indian shipyard, signing a $350 million deal with state-owned Cochin Shipyard for six dual-fuel vessels. Additionally, CMA CGM is planning a $300 million investment in logistics infrastructure in Maharashtra, as indicated by the company’s chairman Rodolphe Saade.CMACGM is also exploring opportunities in inland waterways while assessing viable business proposals. A senior executive from the company has advocated for the Indian government to leverage Gujarat International Finance Tec-City (GIFT City) as a vessel registration hub. With the government focusing on registering vessels under the Indian flag, this initiative not only offers commercial benefits but is also seen as a step towards enhancing maritime security amidst global uncertainties.

+ CMA CGM EXPANDS GEMALINK TERMINAL: A STRATEGIC MOVE FOR VIETNAM'S TRADE

CMA CGM Group has announced the launch of Phase 2 of expansion works at the Gemalink container terminal, located in Cai Mep port in southern Vietnam. This significant project, which commenced on 20th April 2026, marks a crucial step in enhancing CMACGM's port capacity in Southeast Asia and reinforces its commitment to the region's economic development and sustainable supply chains.Upon completion, the terminal's capacity is expected to nearly double from 1.7 million TEUs to approximately 3 million TEUs, with full operational capability anticipated by 2027. The expansion plan includes extending the quay length by 450 meters in two segments and increasing the container yard's area from 32 to 44 hectares. Furthermore, the addition of five new Ship-to-Shore cranes will augment the current fleet from eight to thirteen, significantly boosting the terminal's operational efficiency.Gemalink terminal has quickly established itself as one of the most efficient terminals in the Vung Tau region since its opening in 2021, currently operating at full capacity and handling 1.7 million TEUs annually. This rapid growth emphasizes the terminal's strategic importance within CMACGM's logistics network, as it is well-positioned along key maritime routes. Christine Cabau Woehrel, Executive Vice President of CMA CGM Group, highlighted the expansion as a testament to the long-term partnership with Gemadept and a reflection of the shared vision for Vietnam's role in global supply chains.The expansion of Gemalink is a critical component of CMACGM's broader strategy to enhance its presence in Vietnam. The Group has maintained operations in Vietnam since 1989, currently employing over 550 people across five offices in major cities, including Ho Chi Minh City and Hanoi. With 29 weekly services connecting seven ports in Vietnam, CMACGM aims to leverage its extensive intermodal network powered by CEVA Logistics to boost trade efficiency.This expansion is more than just an increase in capacity; it highlights CMA CGM's commitment to supporting Vietnam's vision of becoming a leading maritime logistics hub in Southeast Asia by 2050. As the global demand for freight services continues to rise, investments like the Gemalink terminal expansion will play a pivotal role in facilitating the movement of goods in the region.

+ CMA CGM ENHANCES TRANSATLANTIC SERVICE, BOOSTING PORT OF CORK

The Port of Cork is set to enhance its role in international trade as CMA CGM Group, a leading global provider of sea, land, air, and logistics solutions, announces improvements to its Transatlantic PAD (Panama Direct) service. This enhancement, effective from 28th April 2026, marks a significant step in fortifying Ireland's connectivity to vital U.S. and rapidly growing Oceania markets.With the new service, Cork will enjoy a direct weekly connection to major U.S. ports in New York and Savannah, providing shippers with competitive transit times of approximately nine days to New York and eleven days to Savannah. This initiative is poised to enhance schedule reliability through the deployment of an additional vessel and optimized port rotations, thereby improving overall predictability for supply chains reliant on these routes.CMACGM's enhanced PAD service distinguishes itself with unique market coverage, featuring direct calls at Dunkerque and Zeebrugge, which are not available through other services. This new route also integrates seamlessly with CMACGM's extensive network across Northern Europe, including key ports like Le Havre and Rotterdam, thereby facilitating efficient feedering solutions to Baltic and Scandinavian markets. In the U.S., the service capitalizes on CMACGM's Port Liberty terminals, ensuring priority berthing and bolstering reliability amidst ongoing port congestion challenges.The versatility of the PAD service allows it to cater to a wide array of commodities, including pharmaceuticals, beverages, and general cargo. This makes it particularly attractive for high-value and time-sensitive shipments, presenting a viable alternative to traditional air freight solutions. The addition of Cork to this route offers Irish exporters and importers a direct, reliable, and competitive means to access major U.S. markets, thus reducing transit times and enhancing supply chain predictability.CMA CGM has maintained a presence in Ireland for over two decades, enhancing the country's trade through regular maritime services and a dedicated local office. With operations at three Irish ports and up to six weekly sailings, the company is committed to ensuring robust connectivity and dependable service. As CMACGM continues to expand its global network, it connects Ireland to over 420 ports worldwide, supporting critical sectors of the Irish economy, including pharmaceuticals, agri-food, and manufacturing.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

urn:contentItem:6JG9-SPG3-RSND-R47S-00000-00CROWLEY MARITIME - RECENT NEWS OF CMA CGM LOGISTICS SA AND PEERS2026-05-01T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:36:35ZLexisNexisSTOCKCROWLEY MARITIME - RECENT NEWS OF CMA CGM LOGISTICS SA AND PEERS

DASHBOARD: CROWLEY MARITIME

PermID4295904276Websitehttp://www.crowley.com/IndustryMaritime & Shipping Logistics

ACTIVITIES:

Crowley Maritime Corporation, founded in 1892, is a family-owned logistics and maritime services company headquartered in Jacksonville, Florida. Employing over 5,300 people, it operates in shipping, energy, and supply chain management, generating over $2 billion annually. Crowley provides ocean transport, LNG distribution, and logistics for government and commercial clients. In 2024, it expanded renewable energy logistics, supporting offshore wind projects. Its culture emphasizes safety, innovation, and sustainability, with robust employee training. Competing with Maersk, Crowley stands out for its U.S.-focused operations and government contracts. Recent initiatives include decarbonization efforts and digital tracking systems, aligning with green logistics trends.

+ CROWLEY MARITIME [3 News That Matters]

+ CROWLEY MARITIME EXPANDS INTERESTS AT PORT MCKENZIE WITH NEW FUEL TERMINAL STUDY

Crowley Maritime is set to capitalize on the increasing interest surrounding Port McKenzie by securing a permit to study the development of a large fuel terminal. This strategic move could significantly enhance the company's operational footprint in Alaska's Matanuska-Susitna Borough.On the 7th of January 2026, Crowley Maritime announced its plans to conduct a comprehensive study aimed at establishing a fuel terminal at Port McKenzie. The initiative comes amid rising commercial activities and interest in the region, which is poised to benefit from enhanced logistics and transportation capabilities.The proposed fuel terminal at Port McKenzie is expected to play a critical role in supporting local industries and businesses by providing a reliable fuel supply chain. Crowley Maritime's investment in this infrastructure is seen as a response to the growing demand for fuel in both marine and land-based operations in Alaska, a region that has been increasingly recognized for its potential in various sectors.As part of its strategic growth plan, Crowley Maritime is focusing on expanding its services in Alaska, leveraging the advantageous location of Port McKenzie. The terminal is anticipated to not only streamline operations for Crowley but also attract other businesses looking to establish a presence in the region, further enhancing the economic landscape of the Matanuska-Susitna Borough.With the approval of this permit, Crowley Maritime is now positioned to move forward with its feasibility studies and engage with local stakeholders. The development of the fuel terminal could mark a significant milestone for both Crowley and Port McKenzie, potentially paving the way for future investments and growth in the area.

+ CROWLEY MARITIME RESTRUCTURES LEADERSHIP TO DRIVE GROWTH IN SHIPPING AND ENERGY

Crowley Maritime has announced a strategic consolidation of its business structure aimed at enhancing efficiency and value for customers. On 6th January 2026, the company revealed the establishment of two new divisions: Shipping and Logistics, led by Executive Vice President and Division President James C. Fowler, and Energy, overseen by Executive Vice President and Division President Kollin Fencil. This reorganization is designed to improve operational execution and agility, positioning Crowley Maritime for accelerated growth across its shipping, logistics, and energy sectors.The Shipping and Logistics Division, under Fowler's leadership, merges Crowley's logistics, land transportation, and global ship management businesses. This division is set to serve both commercial and government customers, providing a comprehensive array of ocean shipping, inland trucking, multimodal transportation, and warehousing solutions. Fowler emphasized that this new structure will ensure that the entire transportation value chain for their customers is managed cohesively, ultimately driving new growth through safe and efficient supply chain solutions.Meanwhile, the Energy Division, guided by Fencil, consolidates operations including Crowley Fuels in Alaska, liquefied natural gas (LNG) services, and advanced energy solutions. Fencil noted that the division's new structure enables Crowley Maritime to innovate and scale more effectively within traditional and emerging energy markets. With a long history of supporting diverse energy needs across the U.S., the realignment is expected to further enhance Crowley's commitment to meeting evolving customer demands for both traditional fuels and advanced energy solutions.Both Fowler and Fencil bring significant experience to their new roles, having joined Crowley Maritime as senior vice presidents in 2023. Under their respective leadership, the company has seen improvements in financial performance and operational efficiency within their business units. Chief Operating Officer Megan Davidson remarked that this realignment empowers faster decision-making and enhances the company's ability to adapt to market demands, ensuring that Crowley Maritime remains a leader in the maritime, energy, and logistics sectors.Crowley Maritime, a privately held U.S.-owned company, has been providing innovative transportation services for over 130 years. With operations spanning 36 nations and territories, the company is recognized as one of the leading employers of U.S. mariners. As Crowley Maritime embarks on this new chapter, stakeholders will be closely monitoring the impact of these changes on the company's growth trajectory and operational capabilities.

+ CROWLEY AND SEACOR JOIN FORCES IN NEW JONES ACT TANKER VENTURE

Crowley Maritime and SEACOR Holdings, through its subsidiary Seabulk Tankers, have formed a joint venture called Fairwater Holdings to integrate their liquid energy and chemical transportation assets. This collaboration aims to address the evolving demands of the U.S. domestic market. The agreement was finalized on 2nd January 2026, establishing Fairwater Holdings as an independent U.S. Jones Act service provider in the maritime sector.The new entity will amalgamate the strengths of both companies, providing a suite of transportation solutions across the petroleum and chemical trades, alongside third-party ship management services. Fairwater Holdings will manage a fleet comprising 20 ocean-going articulated tug-barges and 11 tankers, primarily under long-term charters. Furthermore, the joint venture will offer crewing and technical management for an additional 21 vessels owned by third parties, thereby enhancing its operational capacity and service offerings.Daniel Thorogood, currently the CEO of Seabulk, will take on the CEO role at Fairwater Holdings upon completion of the deal. With over a decade of experience leading Seabulk and managing SEACOR's short-sea shipping and logistics investments, Thorogood is expected to steer the new venture towards innovation and sustainable practices. In his statement, he emphasized that Fairwater marks a significant milestone in delivering safe and efficient maritime transportation solutions tailored to the needs of the U.S. energy sector.Tom Crowley, Chairman and CEO of Crowley Maritime, expressed confidence in the partnership, describing Seabulk as an ideal collaborator with a proven track record in the maritime industry. He noted that the joint venture aligns with Crowley's growth strategy, further solidifying its position as a leader in the U.S. maritime market, where it operates the largest American-flag petroleum and chemical tank vessel fleet, boasting a capacity exceeding 12 million barrels.As both companies work towards integrating their assets and services, the joint venture is expected to commence operations after meeting customary closing conditions and securing regulatory approvals. This integration effort is anticipated to create a unified and seamless transition for customers, ensuring uninterrupted support for their energy supply chains. Fairwater Holdings is set to begin operations in the first quarter of 2024, marking an exciting new chapter in U.S. maritime transportation.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ CMA CGM LOGISTICS [3 News That Matters]

+ CMA CGM TAKES STRATEGIC STEP WITH FIFTH VESSEL REFLAGGING IN INDIA

MUMBAI: French container shipping line CMA CGM Group announced the reflagging of its fifth container vessel in India, as it looks to enhance its service efficiency for customers across key trade routes. This announcement comes on the 24th of April 2026, marking another significant milestone in CMACGM's ongoing investment and long-term commitment to the country.The reflagging process involves changing the country of registration (flag State) of a merchant ship. CMA CGM has positioned itself as a strong growth partner for India across maritime, logistics, and infrastructure sectors. The reflagging of the 2,592 TEUs capacity vessel, Vila do Conde, underscores this commitment and aligns with the Indian government's broader objectives to increase the number of vessels registered under the Indian flag.Reflagging of vessels is gaining traction in light of the government’s initiative to bolster a larger fleet owned by India. Prime Minister Shri Narendra Modi highlighted the importance of self-sufficiency in shipping last September, citing the ₹6 trillion expenditure to foreign shipping lines in maritime trade. Following this trend, CMACGM has successfully reflagged all five vessels since last year, with plans to flag its sixth vessel in the country by the end of May as part of its expansion strategy.CMA CGM has outlined several strategic initiatives to support India’s maritime ecosystem, recognizing the country’s increasingly significant role in global trade. In February, the company became the first major international carrier to order vessels from an Indian shipyard, signing a $350 million deal with state-owned Cochin Shipyard for six dual-fuel vessels. Additionally, CMA CGM is planning a $300 million investment in logistics infrastructure in Maharashtra, as indicated by the company’s chairman Rodolphe Saade.CMACGM is also exploring opportunities in inland waterways while assessing viable business proposals. A senior executive from the company has advocated for the Indian government to leverage Gujarat International Finance Tec-City (GIFT City) as a vessel registration hub. With the government focusing on registering vessels under the Indian flag, this initiative not only offers commercial benefits but is also seen as a step towards enhancing maritime security amidst global uncertainties.

+ CMA CGM EXPANDS GEMALINK TERMINAL: A STRATEGIC MOVE FOR VIETNAM'S TRADE

CMA CGM Group has announced the launch of Phase 2 of expansion works at the Gemalink container terminal, located in Cai Mep port in southern Vietnam. This significant project, which commenced on 20th April 2026, marks a crucial step in enhancing CMACGM's port capacity in Southeast Asia and reinforces its commitment to the region's economic development and sustainable supply chains.Upon completion, the terminal's capacity is expected to nearly double from 1.7 million TEUs to approximately 3 million TEUs, with full operational capability anticipated by 2027. The expansion plan includes extending the quay length by 450 meters in two segments and increasing the container yard's area from 32 to 44 hectares. Furthermore, the addition of five new Ship-to-Shore cranes will augment the current fleet from eight to thirteen, significantly boosting the terminal's operational efficiency.Gemalink terminal has quickly established itself as one of the most efficient terminals in the Vung Tau region since its opening in 2021, currently operating at full capacity and handling 1.7 million TEUs annually. This rapid growth emphasizes the terminal's strategic importance within CMACGM's logistics network, as it is well-positioned along key maritime routes. Christine Cabau Woehrel, Executive Vice President of CMA CGM Group, highlighted the expansion as a testament to the long-term partnership with Gemadept and a reflection of the shared vision for Vietnam's role in global supply chains.The expansion of Gemalink is a critical component of CMACGM's broader strategy to enhance its presence in Vietnam. The Group has maintained operations in Vietnam since 1989, currently employing over 550 people across five offices in major cities, including Ho Chi Minh City and Hanoi. With 29 weekly services connecting seven ports in Vietnam, CMACGM aims to leverage its extensive intermodal network powered by CEVA Logistics to boost trade efficiency.This expansion is more than just an increase in capacity; it highlights CMA CGM's commitment to supporting Vietnam's vision of becoming a leading maritime logistics hub in Southeast Asia by 2050. As the global demand for freight services continues to rise, investments like the Gemalink terminal expansion will play a pivotal role in facilitating the movement of goods in the region.

+ CMA CGM ENHANCES TRANSATLANTIC SERVICE, BOOSTING PORT OF CORK

The Port of Cork is set to enhance its role in international trade as CMA CGM Group, a leading global provider of sea, land, air, and logistics solutions, announces improvements to its Transatlantic PAD (Panama Direct) service. This enhancement, effective from 28th April 2026, marks a significant step in fortifying Ireland's connectivity to vital U.S. and rapidly growing Oceania markets.With the new service, Cork will enjoy a direct weekly connection to major U.S. ports in New York and Savannah, providing shippers with competitive transit times of approximately nine days to New York and eleven days to Savannah. This initiative is poised to enhance schedule reliability through the deployment of an additional vessel and optimized port rotations, thereby improving overall predictability for supply chains reliant on these routes.CMACGM's enhanced PAD service distinguishes itself with unique market coverage, featuring direct calls at Dunkerque and Zeebrugge, which are not available through other services. This new route also integrates seamlessly with CMACGM's extensive network across Northern Europe, including key ports like Le Havre and Rotterdam, thereby facilitating efficient feedering solutions to Baltic and Scandinavian markets. In the U.S., the service capitalizes on CMACGM's Port Liberty terminals, ensuring priority berthing and bolstering reliability amidst ongoing port congestion challenges.The versatility of the PAD service allows it to cater to a wide array of commodities, including pharmaceuticals, beverages, and general cargo. This makes it particularly attractive for high-value and time-sensitive shipments, presenting a viable alternative to traditional air freight solutions. The addition of Cork to this route offers Irish exporters and importers a direct, reliable, and competitive means to access major U.S. markets, thus reducing transit times and enhancing supply chain predictability.CMA CGM has maintained a presence in Ireland for over two decades, enhancing the country's trade through regular maritime services and a dedicated local office. With operations at three Irish ports and up to six weekly sailings, the company is committed to ensuring robust connectivity and dependable service. As CMACGM continues to expand its global network, it connects Ireland to over 420 ports worldwide, supporting critical sectors of the Irish economy, including pharmaceuticals, agri-food, and manufacturing.

SECTION 1 RECENT NEWS

Thu Apr 9, 2026: Kingspan Insulation Partners with REC Solar and Dynamic Energy for 881 kW Solar Project

Kingspan Insulation, in collaboration with REC Solar and Dynamic Energy, has successfully completed an 881 kW rooftop solar installation at its manufacturing facility in Mendota, Illinois. This project was announced on 9th April 2026 and marks a significant step in Kingspan Group's commitment to sustainability through its Planet Passionate program.

As the third solar installation in North America for Kingspan, the Mendota project utilizes 50,000 square feet of industrial rooftop space and is expected to produce approximately 1 million kilowatt-hours of clean energy annually. This output will effectively offset around 870 metric tons of carbon dioxide emissions each year, aligning with Kingspan's goal of achieving 60% direct renewable energy use by 2030.

Kyle McEnroe, President of Kingspan Insulation North America, expressed pride in the project's completion, highlighting it as a tangible commitment to sustainability. The installation is part of a broader initiative to integrate renewable energy solutions into Kingspan's operational framework, thereby enhancing both environmental performance and business efficiency.

Under a 25-year power purchase agreement, REC Solar will own and operate the new solar array, a strategic move that underscores the growing trend among companies to reduce emissions and enhance sustainability in their supply chains. REC Solar has previously established a 1.05 MW solar array at Kingspan's facility in DeLand, Florida, showcasing a long-standing partnership focused on renewable energy.

Dynamic Energy, which played a key role as the engineering, procurement, and construction (EPC) partner for the Mendota project, continues to support Kingspan's sustainability objectives. With rising industrial electricity rates, both Dynamic Energy and Kingspan Insulation emphasize the importance of such renewable projects in controlling energy costs while adhering to environmental goals.

urn:contentItem:6JK3-D173-RX2K-1145-00000-00CROWLEY MARITIME - THE RECENT IN THE SECTOR2026-05-14T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:46:15ZLexisNexisSTOCKCROWLEY MARITIME - THE RECENT IN THE SECTOR

DASHBOARD: CROWLEY MARITIME

PermID4295904276Websitehttp://www.crowley.com/IndustryMaritime & Shipping Logistics

ACTIVITIES:

Crowley Maritime Corporation, founded in 1892, is a family-owned logistics and maritime services company headquartered in Jacksonville, Florida. Employing over 5,300 people, it operates in shipping, energy, and supply chain management, generating over $2 billion annually. Crowley provides ocean transport, LNG distribution, and logistics for government and commercial clients. In 2024, it expanded renewable energy logistics, supporting offshore wind projects. Its culture emphasizes safety, innovation, and sustainability, with robust employee training. Competing with Maersk, Crowley stands out for its U.S.-focused operations and government contracts. Recent initiatives include decarbonization efforts and digital tracking systems, aligning with green logistics trends.

+ CROWLEY MARITIME [3 News That Matters]

+ CROWLEY MARITIME ACHIEVES SAFE TRANSIT OF CS ANTHEM THROUGH STRAIT OF HORMUZ

Crowley Maritime has confirmed that its managed vessel, CS Anthem, has successfully transited the Strait of Hormuz, providing crucial visibility into the limited number of U.S.-flagged ships that have exited the Persian Gulf since the conflict began. On the 5th of May, 2026, the company stated that CS Anthem has safely completed its transit through this vital maritime chokepoint.In its statement, Crowley emphasized that the safety of its crews and vessels, as well as the continuity of service for its customers, remain top priorities. The company also acknowledged the professionalism of U.S. Navy and government partners who work globally to support maritime safety. However, Crowley refrained from disclosing further operational details, citing company policy against discussing specifics of vessel routing or security arrangements.CS Anthem is now one of only two U.S.-flagged commercial vessels confirmed to have transited the Strait of Hormuz under the Trump administration's newly initiated 'Project Freedom' operation. This operation aims to facilitate the evacuation of ships via Oman’s territorial waters within an enhanced U.S.-backed security area. Alongside CS Anthem, A.P. Moller – Maersk's U.S.-flagged vessel, Alliance Fairfax, also successfully exited the Persian Gulf under U.S. military escort, marking a significant milestone in Washington’s efforts to guide stranded vessels out of the region.Despite these successful transits, the overall shipping environment through the Strait of Hormuz remains precarious. Persistent risks, including threats from mines, drone activities, and an expanded military presence, continue to deter a broader return of commercial traffic in this crucial waterway. Industry observers caution that these movements are indicative of highly coordinated, one-off transits rather than a sign of a broader reopening for commercial shipping.Both the CS Anthem and Alliance Fairfax belong to a small group of U.S.-flagged vessels effectively stranded in the Gulf since late February. They are part of the Maritime Security Program (MSP) or the Tanker Security Program (TSP), ensuring the availability of commercially operated U.S.-flagged vessels for military logistics during crises. The CS Anthem, a 49,990-metric-ton medium-range tanker, is managed by Crowley under a bareboat charter arrangement, further illustrating the company's strategic positioning in a highly complex maritime landscape.

+ CROWLEY MARITIME EXPANDS INTERESTS AT PORT MCKENZIE WITH NEW FUEL TERMINAL STUDY

Crowley Maritime is set to capitalize on the increasing interest surrounding Port McKenzie by securing a permit to study the development of a large fuel terminal. This strategic move could significantly enhance the company's operational footprint in Alaska's Matanuska-Susitna Borough.On the 7th of January 2026, Crowley Maritime announced its plans to conduct a comprehensive study aimed at establishing a fuel terminal at Port McKenzie. The initiative comes amid rising commercial activities and interest in the region, which is poised to benefit from enhanced logistics and transportation capabilities.The proposed fuel terminal at Port McKenzie is expected to play a critical role in supporting local industries and businesses by providing a reliable fuel supply chain. Crowley Maritime's investment in this infrastructure is seen as a response to the growing demand for fuel in both marine and land-based operations in Alaska, a region that has been increasingly recognized for its potential in various sectors.As part of its strategic growth plan, Crowley Maritime is focusing on expanding its services in Alaska, leveraging the advantageous location of Port McKenzie. The terminal is anticipated to not only streamline operations for Crowley but also attract other businesses looking to establish a presence in the region, further enhancing the economic landscape of the Matanuska-Susitna Borough.With the approval of this permit, Crowley Maritime is now positioned to move forward with its feasibility studies and engage with local stakeholders. The development of the fuel terminal could mark a significant milestone for both Crowley and Port McKenzie, potentially paving the way for future investments and growth in the area.

+ CROWLEY MARITIME RESTRUCTURES LEADERSHIP TO DRIVE GROWTH IN SHIPPING AND ENERGY

Crowley Maritime has announced a strategic consolidation of its business structure aimed at enhancing efficiency and value for customers. On 6th January 2026, the company revealed the establishment of two new divisions: Shipping and Logistics, led by Executive Vice President and Division President James C. Fowler, and Energy, overseen by Executive Vice President and Division President Kollin Fencil. This reorganization is designed to improve operational execution and agility, positioning Crowley Maritime for accelerated growth across its shipping, logistics, and energy sectors.The Shipping and Logistics Division, under Fowler's leadership, merges Crowley's logistics, land transportation, and global ship management businesses. This division is set to serve both commercial and government customers, providing a comprehensive array of ocean shipping, inland trucking, multimodal transportation, and warehousing solutions. Fowler emphasized that this new structure will ensure that the entire transportation value chain for their customers is managed cohesively, ultimately driving new growth through safe and efficient supply chain solutions.Meanwhile, the Energy Division, guided by Fencil, consolidates operations including Crowley Fuels in Alaska, liquefied natural gas (LNG) services, and advanced energy solutions. Fencil noted that the division's new structure enables Crowley Maritime to innovate and scale more effectively within traditional and emerging energy markets. With a long history of supporting diverse energy needs across the U.S., the realignment is expected to further enhance Crowley's commitment to meeting evolving customer demands for both traditional fuels and advanced energy solutions.Both Fowler and Fencil bring significant experience to their new roles, having joined Crowley Maritime as senior vice presidents in 2023. Under their respective leadership, the company has seen improvements in financial performance and operational efficiency within their business units. Chief Operating Officer Megan Davidson remarked that this realignment empowers faster decision-making and enhances the company's ability to adapt to market demands, ensuring that Crowley Maritime remains a leader in the maritime, energy, and logistics sectors.Crowley Maritime, a privately held U.S.-owned company, has been providing innovative transportation services for over 130 years. With operations spanning 36 nations and territories, the company is recognized as one of the leading employers of U.S. mariners. As Crowley Maritime embarks on this new chapter, stakeholders will be closely monitoring the impact of these changes on the company's growth trajectory and operational capabilities.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ CMA CGM LOGISTICS [3 News That Matters]

+ CMA CGM SET TO TRANSFORM KENYA’S TRANSPORT LANDSCAPE

French shipping and logistics giant CMA CGM Group has signed a strategic partnership agreement with the Government of Kenya aimed at expanding the country’s transport and logistics infrastructure. This agreement, finalized on 11th May 2026 during the Africa Forward Summit, was witnessed by President William Ruto and French President Emmanuel Macron, and seeks to bolster the development of port capacity, inland logistics, and freight management systems in Kenya and the wider East and Central African region.The partnership will focus on identifying investment opportunities to enhance Kenya’s logistics ecosystem and strengthen regional integration into global trade routes through upgraded infrastructure. President Macron announced that CMA CGM has committed 700 million euros towards the renovation of two terminals at the Port of Mombasa, a key component of this broader logistics and transport partnership.CMA CGM aims to address the rising maritime trade and supply chain demands across East and Central Africa by improving connectivity between ports, inland corridors, and international markets. With operations in Kenya dating back to 2005, CMA CGM has been pivotal in linking the ports of Mombasa and Lamu to global markets while also supporting inland transport corridors across East Africa.The company’s Africa strategy emphasizes expanding port and inland infrastructure, improving logistics integration, and supporting the decarbonisation of transport systems across the continent. Currently managing nine container terminals throughout Africa, CMA CGM is investing in both maritime and land-based logistics hubs to enhance trade efficiency and regional connectivity.In addition to infrastructure improvements, CMA CGM has announced various social investment projects in Kenya through the CMA CGM Foundation. These initiatives include the expansion of the I.O. Me001 Innovation Center in Mombasa and the renovation of the main football field at the University of Nairobi. Headquartered in Marseille, France, CMA CGM operates in 177 countries with a fleet of over 700 vessels, transporting more than 24 million containers globally in 2025.

+ CMA CGM STRENGTHENS LOGISTICS TIES IN KENYA

CMA CGM Group has signed a high-level framework agreement for joint cooperation with Kenyan institutional authorities to develop Kenya’s logistics and port capacity. This significant move comes as part of the Africa Forward Summit, and in the presence of the Presidents of the French and Kenyan Republics, M. Emmanuel Macron and M. William Ruto.The agreement aims to accelerate solutions that support the growth of supply chain flows in and out of East and Central Africa, including maritime demand, inland logistics, and freight management. Since establishing its presence in Kenya in 2005, CMA CGM has played a vital role in the country’s logistics ecosystem, connecting the ports of Mombasa and Lamu to global markets and facilitating inland corridors throughout East Africa.CMA CGM has been active on the African continent for over 50 years, pursuing a long-term development strategy focused on securing port and inland capacities, developing regional hubs, and integrating logistics solutions. The Group is also committed to accelerating the decarbonization of transport chains, which is increasingly crucial in today’s environmentally conscious market.Currently, CMA CGM operates and develops nine container terminals across Africa. For instance, the Kribi Container Terminal in Cameroon, where CMA CGM is a major stakeholder, is enhancing its role as a strategic regional hub with ongoing expansions. Additionally, the Lekki Deep Sea Port in Nigeria is central to CMA CGM’s strategy in the Gulf of Guinea, with investments aimed at positioning it as a primary gateway for West Africa.Beyond infrastructure development, CMA CGM is committed to social responsibility across the continent. The CMA CGM Foundation recently announced two projects in Kenya aimed at strengthening education and innovation, further solidifying the Group’s presence in Africa. With projects like the PharmaBoxes initiative to provide mobile pharmacies and the 'Containers of Hope' program delivering humanitarian aid globally, CMA CGM continues to enhance its reputation as a key player in both logistics and community support.

+ CMA CGM ENHANCES REGIONAL CONNECTIONS THROUGH EAST AFRICA PARTNERSHIP

CMA CGM has signed a strategic partnership agreement with the Government of Kenya to support the development of transport and logistics infrastructure, including port capacity, inland logistics and freight management. This agreement was executed during the Africa Forward Summit, taking place in Nairobi from 11th to 12th May 2026, and was attended by French President Emmanuel Macron and Kenyan President William Ruto.The partnership aims to explore joint investments in Kenya's logistics and port infrastructure, which is essential for accommodating the growing supply chain flows into and out of East and Central Africa. CMA CGM stated that this initiative will also enhance the region's connections to global trade routes, showcasing the company's commitment to regional development.CMA CGM has been operational in Kenya since 2005, establishing vital links between Mombasa and Lamu with global markets and inland corridors across East Africa. This new agreement integrates Kenya into the larger African network managed by CMA CGM, which includes the operation and development of nine container terminals across the continent.The group's long-term strategy in Africa emphasizes securing port and inland capacity, developing regional hubs, integrating logistics services, and decarbonizing transport chains. Current projects under CMA CGM's umbrella include the expansion of Kribi Container Terminal in Cameroon, the development of Lekki Deep Sea Port in Nigeria, and plans for a deep-water terminal in Pointe-Noire, Republic of Congo.In addition to enhancing port infrastructure, CMA CGM is also focused on developing multimodal corridors through its subsidiary CEVA Logistics. This initiative aims to efficiently connect maritime, rail, and road transport, thereby improving reliability, transit times, and overall logistics performance across the region.

value.Title: May 05, 2026: Crowley Maritime Announces Safe Transit of CS Anthem Through Strait of Hormuz

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JK4-J7R3-RS50-S476-00000-00May 05, 2026: Crowley Maritime Announces Safe Transit of CS Anthem Through Strait of Hormuz2026-05-14T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:46:15ZLexisNexisANNOUNCEMENTSMay 05, 2026: Crowley Maritime Announces Safe Transit of CS Anthem Through Strait of Hormuz

Crowley Maritime says its managed tanker CS Anthem has safely completed a transit of the Strait of Hormuz, a passage that has seen only a small number of U.S.-flagged ships depart the Persian Gulf since the latest crisis began. On the 5th of May, 2026, Crowley disclosed that the voyage through this critical chokepoint is complete.

In its brief statement, Crowley stressed that crew safety and uninterrupted service for customers remain top priorities, and it credited U.S. Navy and government partners for maritime safety. It did not provide further operational details, noting a company policy against discussing vessel routing or security arrangements.

CSAnthem is now one of two U.S.-flagged commercial vessels confirmed to have transited Hormuz under the Trump administration's 'Project Freedom' plan, which seeks to evacuate ships through Oman’s territorial waters in a secured zone. The other vessel, Maersk's Alliance Fairfax, also left the gulf under U.S. military escort, marking a notable step in Washington's efforts to guide stranded ships out of the region. Despite the transits, the overall Strait environment remains risky, with mines, drones, and a larger military presence dampening broader traffic. Industry observers say these moves are coordinated, one-off transits rather than broad reopenings. Both ships belong to MSP or TSP and reflect the role of U.S.-flagged tonnage in crisis logistics. CS Anthem, a 49,990-metric-ton medium-range tanker, is managed by Crowley under a bareboat charter.

SECTION 1 CROWLEY MARITIME PROFILE

1.1 ACTIVITIES

Crowley Maritime Corporation, founded in 1892, is a family-owned logistics and maritime services company headquartered in Jacksonville, Florida. Employing over 5,300 people, it operates in shipping, energy, and supply chain management, generating over $2 billion annually. Crowley provides ocean transport, LNG distribution, and logistics for government and commercial clients. In 2024, it expanded renewable energy logistics, supporting offshore wind projects. Its culture emphasizes safety, innovation, and sustainability, with robust employee training. Competing with Maersk, Crowley stands out for its U.S.-focused operations and government contracts. Recent initiatives include decarbonization efforts and digital tracking systems, aligning with green logistics trends.
